53d1ed393ea97c8f96af3cb1d756ece39bf75f4d: Caderneta Predial (property tax booklet) from the seller
762413681973d6a655e35ac62f99302afb6d4805: Valuation report (avaliação) — the bank may require its own appraiser
c9400771df12df4b22c328d64dd885c9b554d1db: Contact banks (at least 3) → obtain "pre-approval" indicating the amount and rate. Duration 2-3 weeks.
7e81a9d0ba323e2d71c799e5c3e05e81903392a8: With pre-approval in hand, negotiations with the seller are more serious. Sign CPCV (contrato de promessa) + deposit 10% of the price.
c59ef596534b52ab0fff8741a2749c7635b76958: The bank appoints an appraiser. €300-500. If the valuation is < the price — the bank will only finance up to the valuation.
f8cdd6f4c131a4e6d16436c76769c3addae6a0ec: The bank issues a "carta de aprovação". Validity period 30-60 days.
cb7fdf85b1daa80a62cb4fdde35499806a5aa296: Simultaneous signing: mortgage contract + sale and purchase at the notary. The bank pays the seller, you receive the keys.

69da38e2d64430bd2cf1dd05df2e7b2dab68e558: IS (stamp duty) — 0.8% of the price + 0.6% of the mortgage

5656ca3239470dc99fe586faf38a89ff6a4f9986: Long-term rental in Portugal — legal framework and process
5723911e3d8fc6751ad5145b7a1de45633cc3c71: Long-term rental (arrendamento habitacional) is regulated by the New Urban Lease Regime (NRAU, Lei 6/2006 with numerous amendments). The minimum contract duration is 1 year unless otherwise specified. Deposit (caução) is typically 1-3 months' rent. A guarantor (fiador) or prepayment of 3-12 months is often required. The contract must be registered with Finanças by the owner within 30 days.
77721db6c7025bd91fc921ec581ca8f3107f5e96: Key documents: Código Civil (articles on rental), Lei 6/2006 (NRAU — Novo Regime do Arrendamento Urbano), numerous Leis and Decretos-Lei with amendments. Important 2024: Lei 56/2023 «Mais Habitação» PARTIALLY REPEALS Decreto-Lei 81/2024 (November 2024, «Construir Portugal»): moratorium on new Alojamento Local (AL) licences lifted, CAE (forced rental of vacant apartments) abolished, forced expiration of AL 2030 removed, IRS reform on rental income.
798b85766fa4537c2fdbb576bafcc068909a3fe5: Key principles: tenant protection (eviction only through court), major repairs — owner's responsibility, rent indexing — no more than once a year and not above the official INE coefficient (in 2026: **2.16%**, INE publication October 2025).
bc5db76c96449f1643914f6c0b70c2194dd19eb2: Caução under Lei 13/2019: maximum of 2 months' rent + 1 month in advance. More than this is illegal.
175b48e753f29c5dda337a20292d7e0640bb1ef6: Contrato de arrendamento habitacional — residential lease agreement
345f3380fb7d9f4fb1dea8ca644bac80b73e4502: Caução — security deposit, under Lei 13/2019 maximum 2 months' rent + 1 month in advance, returned upon departure
78c6eb3dafd724994399de16386d8cc5e81e0499: Fiador — guarantor: a resident individual in PT with stable income, who secures the tenant's obligations with their property
b9c439f463845e938d7f85373af97c41dea6d635: Seguro de fiança — insurance company replaces the fiador, ~5-7% of annual rent
f395f9602afa34ed10435892a0c1964409a3bf58: IMI — municipal property tax, legally paid by the owner
1b37242f06dc4af599dc9ff4f84b43a6112844ee: Condomínio — fee for maintaining common areas of the building (usually not included in the rent)
ae2a18ca862956abe603e88d06291c34f364abca: Recibo de renda — monthly receipt from the owner with NIF of the parties, basis for tax declaration

60091cb054d81ef763aea095cfa8514fb7ae5879: In Portugal, there are several categories of housing search channels:
92b6e919e313878c265864f7bedfe44be23beb6e: Online portal aggregators (largest: Idealista, Imovirtual, Casa Sapo, Supercasa) — the main channel, hundreds of thousands of listings
7ca7c7c4477631d93249c6017b4732201ca50360: OLX Imóveis — many listings directly from owners (meno fees)
5e823a269c0479265a9e55cfd17051263c4271b6: Real estate agencies (imobiliárias) — major networks: RE/MAX, Century 21, ERA, Engel & Völkers. Commission is 1 month's rent, often paid by the owner.

7f03eb1341aaa0509dd5c38b320cbf53be44a27a: The rental process — step by step
91abe7eab1235f001b489be070b969ebe68cc3e1: Prepare proof of income: employment contract, payslips for 3 months, bank statement, or IRS declaration
f5ab6956752ba15c8bd584cae8aa50fed8ba125c: Find a fiador (if available) — their CC + income
88ce94652ab0cf1c3a24a5c448573a42b042cdb7: If no fiador: budget for a deposit of 3-12 months or seguro de fiança
b86e49b416b66b8b87bbd2e36b6a6c766cced6c9: Create a shortlist. During the first viewing check: wall and window dampness (white marks in corners = mould), heating (critical in winter — Portugal is one of the worst in the EU for housing insulation), hot water (gas water heater esquentador or termoacumulador boiler), noise from the street and neighbours, water pressure.
778d351e079483268df152e15c85de959fbfdbc9: Discussed points: duration (12/24/36 months), amount of caução (security deposit), who pays IMI and condomínio (owners' association), furniture (mobilado (furnished) / mobilado parcialmente / sem mobília (unfurnished)), pet restrictions, rent indexing, preliminary obligation of the owner to rectify noted defects before move-in.
d9abdee9ff67a59a3c7e300942ad217f8e9a16a5: At least in 2 copies. Mandatory elements according to Lei 6/2006: names and NIF (Portuguese tax ID) of the parties, detailed address of the property, matricial number (artigo matricial) and freguesia (parish council), duration, rent amount and indexing method, amount of caução, start date.
7fb45d8d6c1392d0dfa94941c14b93ed351248c2: Prepare a condition report (inventário) — a list of existing furniture, condition of walls, floors, plumbing. Photograph everything. This will protect you when returning the caução.
fd6672021587eeb6dcc5b96af0f641e879e7f3f7: Within 30 days, the owner is required to register the contract on the Portal das Finanças (tax authority online portal). They receive a comprovativo (confirmation), and a copy is given to you. Without registration: you cannot use the contract for atestado de residência (proof of residence), for AIMA, or for rent deduction in IRS (personal income tax).
50d139941a253227d54c115989a1bf88cf4de085: If the owner refuses to register — this is a serious red flag (possible tax evasion). Insist or choose another apartment.
0b54bf81998d4ecbc23111a009516fb08a58092d: Newly arrived foreigners often do not have access to a fiador (guarantor). Options:
677dba4d8f0dea4cb263b9e31f7821699867f740: Prepayment of 3-12 months in advance — a common practice for international tenants
6c9e560cb174fc221de34ae1d42dfcc1cb455842: Seguro de fiança — an insurance company guarantees obligations, costing 5-7% of annual rent
5e37cf2c2252c7ccc55f1e81271115002f29befa: Medium-term rental (6-12 months) through intermediary services — usually without fiador, but 20-40% more expensive
dbd7d3f88000cd9316ed607ac01bf41b9da93593: Social housing fund (for certain categories) — through the municipality
5fc331e8e04840989d1a6ad93622eefebfb52910: Room rental (shared accommodation) — often a simplified process
9aa14f62e8657114e1f1e9f83938f1db8f257d35: Peaceful possession of the apartment for the duration of the contract
5a617002f2aadaaf5a4b41534bded3f715122e23: Protection against arbitrary rent increases — only once a year and not above the INE coefficient
653e234e9c0b250be3af80cd833328d90edcd326: Protection against eviction — only by court order, excluding cases of non-payment for 2+ months or grave violations
ae6d25ba69cea4b9a2d5dc3cecfd011fc2d1bc37: Major repairs — at the owner's expense (roof, electrical wiring, structural defects)
62aa69667b3c50b25703361219f0a168cb72df21: Return of the caução (security deposit) within 30 days after the end of the contract (minus documented damages)
38572fb10d17325cb2da1120d8101b06c51b0d85: IRS deduction for rent — 15% up to €700–800/year (threshold raised in OE 2024/25; check the exact amount at portaldasfinancas.gov.pt)
bb80f71859b3b2d075f52edc104f93c4669857ef: Timely payment of rent (usually by the 8th of the month)
2cd901b6070571ab0193fda23a69b8d21d4fd22c: Payment of utilities (electricity, gas, water — bills go directly to you as the tenant)
a3cd895c870c3cd564bf02b511b74ab7caf5a5d1: Ongoing maintenance (cosmetic, changing light bulbs, minor bricolage)
fbfa8b58fb2ddcc6372b1a39e57ba9b1b4f1e94b: Conditions for maintaining the apartment — keeping it clean, without damage
783a6694f6331e3b2651dfaa11d1ec971b7bad17: Notification to the owner 120 days in advance if you wish to terminate the contract (the period varies by type)
58c57fbf7f88f282c1f7fc14e93a99667de852cb: Non-refundable **caução** — written complaint to the owner, then to **Balcão Nacional do Arrendamento** (BNA) or **Julgados de Paz**
d028067123a4ed1077113e3db57d0e1f910c7f3a: Problems with technical condition — request by mail with notification, photos, deadlines for resolution
af97a46f4c033355df1cec79ec12cfad21b74187: Noise and neighbours — complaint to **Junta de Freguesia**, police (112 for loud nights)
2dac07ef6660219ffa72ad1746e43dfff54d31ad: Unwanted rent increase — contest at BNA; coefficient above **INE** is illegal
76ada1b2448b6a10bf3104e86f5b03fc773abe2a: Eviction before term — free legal aid through **Ordem dos Advogados** (if low income) or commercial **advogado**
0ac930c990a681d4133096c8a40a1de671cbfaf9: **DECO Proteste** — consumer rights protection, provides advice on rental contracts
9c787dedb621c4714854ac847d3adf1f7b6e686b: both parties — mandatory in the contract
cd2ab3cbe5139072bf72687b2c1eb9dd91cd6cc6: their CC, NIF, proof of income

d7c1fc4e7a8c50ea2611091052d7cefac9e32237: Portal das Finanças — Registo de Contrato de Arrendamento (rental contract)
e1d7bd11c5a672559e7bcd7087aa434a493cccd8: IHRU — Instituto da Habitação e da Reabilitação Urbana
d7273ad0659f196b75e42e8d29452a968cafb8f0: Where to search for rental housing — websites and strategy
78de42947982e8e03e8420f15fb91ec3a8534fc2: Four main channels: aggregators (Idealista, Imovirtual, Supercasa), medium-term (Flatio, Uniplaces), directly from owners (OLX Imóveis, Facebook groups), agents (Century 21, ERA, RE/MAX). For long-term rentals, start with Idealista + OLX. For moving before obtaining your NIF (Portuguese tax ID) — Flatio / Uniplaces (they only require a passport). The average search period in Lisbon/Porto is 3–8 weeks.

8f6849cb777d9ac61783a79a3ce6d0b7700682b7: 60% from private landlords (no agent = no commission)

4c77ab7fdc920beef997dd184dea18a29d66ee07: City groups: 'Arrendamento Lisboa', 'Casas para Alugar Porto', 'Expats in PT — Housing'. Moderation is weak, but quick deals are possible. Check the landlord's profile.
fb1354aa982d8b94401c90d7e435f7e4c6c0e784: In small towns, it still works. Ask the Junta de Freguesia (parish council), cafés, pastelarias. Often without commission.
7b91fbe4c5aa00af5e1f425164af8431570f5351: If you don't have time to search yourself or need advice. Commission: usually 1 month's rent + IVA (from landlord, but reflected in the price).

25bc39d2759982eff195ddc49d2294d93b35499e: Portuguese A2 — free courses, school programmes and CIPLE exam
7137cc0da3a16eb3964d0e9145f5ed4ca498ede0: A level A2 certificate is only required for naturalisation after 5 years (under the new law 2026 — 7 years for CPLP+EU / 10 years for others) of residence. For marriage to a Portuguese citizen, união de facto, and family reunification — it is NOT required. The cheapest options are state PLA courses (0 €, 150 hours) or IEFP programmes for registered unemployed (0 € + scholarship). Public adult schools — enrolment for about €20/semester. The CIPLE exam — €85 (2026), with monthly national sessions + international ones in May/July/November. If you do not wish to take the exam — a certificate of completion of 150 hours of PLA is sufficient.
b775add1ca8d7e69ffa51fb792b1ea00975c0138: When is the certificate required and when is it not
f6ec73a3acb470f0a3c13686697639d499d88504: Level A2 of Portuguese is only needed in a few cases:
bb4a350ceb3c272978252b4441bc7f4889b7c94f: ✅ Naturalisation after 5 years of residence (under the new law 2026 — 7 for CPLP/EU or 10 for others; the law was passed by parliament on 1 April 2026, awaiting the signature of President Segura)
d9954e991dea43d363197c3c7bacb7cca2428c30: ✅ For admission to certain IEFP programmes and state colleges
30f9ede4d1b638eeeaa1b78bc93e0fa703229168: When the certificate is NOT required:
639efcf05b06ded7ec82e40eacb174af0291637c: ❌ Citizenship through marriage to a Portuguese citizen or through união de facto — Lei 37/81 does not require language
7c79a3c4de5eec2c95e2dc1ea1c59fed4f07aeda: ❌ Citizenship by descent (grandfather/grandmother being Portuguese) — does not require
8a750685c1fdbbba64fa48ee42a45af51f501d40: ❌ Family reunification (reagrupamento familiar) with a resident of PT — AIMA does not ask for language
562a681b5a674618b9ae9f095e1727e26520215d: ❌ Renewal of residence permit — AIMA does not require
0bd05aeaa22303484856410b058070d06eb8ba3c: ❌ For CPLP citizens (Brazil, Angola, Cape Verde, etc.) during naturalisation — Portuguese is considered native

ba6f32d01342bcd70fa4a6b791b16b478f0f8988: 1. IEFP — job centre: course + scholarship (for registered unemployed)
04f22852e6cfa31fecfda9c23d8d963ed7e6f613: The Institute for Employment and Professional Training (IEFP, iefp.pt) organises Portuguese courses for foreign residents. If you are registered with IEFP as a job seeker — the course is not only free but also includes a subsidy (bolsa de formação): covering transport, meals, and sometimes a scholarship.
1d3782db3ad20de45fd716a58054e51547b1be94: You can register at the nearest Centro de Emprego (list available at iefp.pt)
0a4be1ed835df187b84040f02c9408a370b6bced: Required: NIF, NISS, residence permit, proof of address
e4a2587c2a5aea4469b2488518abdd28241d9281: Format: group classes for 4–6 months, several times a week
3bfa3f548f3c71d81fb4997c65a1a04bdcfc4229: Scholarship (bolsa de formação): partially IAS 2026 (€537.13) — usually 50–80% of IAS depending on the programme. Check with the Centro de Emprego
49d33de8a4fba49a14a1cacc9339ef33f468cb4a: Transport (subsídio de transporte): based on actual costs
b4d9812bb4d22cac3bb427314751a0af7efea98c: Upon completion — certificate of ≥ 150 hours, recognised for naturalisation
8c3ed6a97e47dea2418dad6b1eaba44e637c6a67: If employed during the course, the scholarship remains until the end (partially)
953d6d4642ffa8e62dec3271fdf60a667c095899: This is literally the best option: you learn the language, receive money, and they pay for your meals + transport. It is suitable even for those who are not actively seeking work — just being registered is enough.
de9a523baf2a206408c012f19402a8d1af1db5e2: The state programme 'Portuguese as a Language of Reception' is organised by Agência para a Integração, Migrações e Asilo (AIMA, formerly ACM) in collaboration with public schools. Completely free for all foreigners with any status (including refugees).
677ccf11e5d1f09571cc8a0b6b1e31c8e8d006d0: 150 hours for level A2 (sufficient for naturalisation)
6feebd5bc009dd39d3b28e8a8ba745ddbea67641: Register through Centro Qualifica at your place of residence (list available at qualifica.gov.pt) or through AIMA
cc177f37f67478d9a5e869a835dd3ab2425e1839: Format: evening or weekend classes (usually 2-3 times a week for 3 hours)
5e0128e1db99784e328bf85c7630d60546eef694: Upon completion — Certificado PLA, recognised by IRN for naturalisation on par with CIPLE
f0d9e7f523b39da09cc139b70c6ceac87a5e7239: 3. Public schools for adults (Escola Secundária) — symbolic registration
0a341254af6d384ead9fef12c7851239e930fb7c: Many state Escolas Secundárias offer evening courses for adults, including Português para Estrangeiros as part of PLNM (Português Língua Não Materna).

a1924303ec5b31774b93685e986a4565b95a9b19: Format: full classes with a teacher, certificate from the school
b42291c846e479ce45b88ad1f14b9c7bff3305ec: List of schools with PLNM: on the municipality's website under Educação

23cf5877e7472b7c5bda510d692b877f969200ca: Some Juntas de Freguesia organise free conversation courses with volunteers. No certificate is provided, but it's excellent for conversational practice and exam preparation. Ask in your area.
5a47af681b482c3505a0c56f68d03d27d003aaae: Tandem / HelloTalk — practice with native speakers for free
739a2c09dac587c4d05a06a0e8a0e8c983638ca4: Audio: Said No One Ever Podcast, Portuguese Lab
d8aa5e71dea76fc61c46bc2550944a02f808bab0: 6. Private language schools (the most expensive option)
b9e6c45ad075e473208d0df064326afecad6b955: Only if you need maximum speed and a personalised approach. Starting from €300 for intensive courses, full A1→A2 course ~€700–€1,200. Notable schools: CIAL, CLIC, Fast Forward.

0fa7efbd24c5cb3ec11df8f25245b99f1c129fb9: CIPLE (Certificado Inicial de Português Língua Estrangeira) — A2 level exam from CAPLE (Universidade de Lisboa). Required if you don't have a certificate from courses (PLA, IEFP or school).
9b1cfdd70687072d89f6349b6d589cbe728743e1: 4 texts with questions (multiple choice + short answers). A short letter (70–90 words): email, note, greeting.

2573108412bd5d0061124afcc203804948a86b1f: Passing score — 55% of total points. Certificate is issued 6–8 weeks after the exam, valid indefinitely.
4c14da2976cc62ee0132b2f36ad937ae9b939210: 📅 Sessions 2026: monthly national CIPLE-P (February–November excluding August) + international in May/July/November. Current calendar at caple.letras.ulisboa.pt

43386843616bb18530056e635175e05bfd4b09e8: 💶 Cost: €85 for 2026 (up to €95 in some international centres). Payment by card or MB reference

79262da9452434b5093170e37259ffb723ec9f21: Available to grandchildren/great-grandchildren of Portuguese citizens, if the connection is confirmed. Grandparent is Portuguese + proof of connection = right to citizenship. For grandchildren — automatic at birth + registration. For great-grandchildren — a more complex process.
5503ed0ad27779b9f15cc16a1ed56f0aedce8104: Child of a Portuguese citizen (any generation)
7329ed2639bf14f57bb3fcd060691bb1e4c528e7: If one of the parents is Portuguese (acquired citizenship by the time of the child's birth) — the child automatically has the right to Portuguese citizenship. Registration at the Conservatória.
dca277d2cbe7124972510aeafb94766f368dc5d5: If the grandmother or grandfather is a citizen of Portugal (and this connection is documented), the grandchild can apply for citizenship. Requires 'connection to the Portuguese community' — A2 language or cultural connection.
9441918f7fcf73dcd30d66ae4f7a987d87ea2824: Possible, but more difficult — requires stronger proof of connection (residence in PT, language proficiency, family roots).
fe8b7ee8744f961fef12d21887878b9732148798: Parent's birth certificate (through which kinship is established)
975ff47441a83bf883476510300d089ae89a3db0: Ancestor's citizenship certificate (entry in Conservatória or passport)
e78edaebf280f3cd1819ae54b98a79dccd1e55e9: Absence of military service in an opposing country (rare requirement)
92827d8f2db3410b3033295f02aa8a05ff356fa4: The chain of certificates must be continuous. If the ancestor lost citizenship, or records are lost — the process is more complicated.
eca792d7e4b607b7b528725bb873694c10baf690: Apostille + sworn translation of all certificates. For records from Portuguese archives — request through Arquivo Nacional.
faebba42ad6ffbb16a28b3c68ab7c404ac198cbe: On the IRN website or by requesting at Conservatória — ensure that the record exists.

8449a272558b68f72ec8393a4e03234ee2a20460: Conservatória dos Registos Centrais (Lisbon) or the consulate.
1c7c96248eba756c80c511c9c10810f1d2c03c30: Sephardic Jews — regime at risk of cancellation
f40d381578fcadf8c531863388df2e182a64c231: An accelerated route for Sephardic Jews (naturalisation without long-term residence upon proof of kinship) was introduced by Lei 43/2013 (through Decreto-Lei 30-A/2015 — Nationality Regulation) and was in effect for nearly 10 years. From 1 September 2022, the rules were tightened by Lei 2/2022 — requiring proof of a real connection to Portugal (residence, economic/cultural ties). After this, the number of applications sharply declined.
4362e5f213ec87ebba7be9f78c825ca9ac204f2a: Decreto AR 17/XVII (re-adopted by Parliament on 01.04.2026) cancels this route. HOWEVER: due to Marcelo's December veto + TC Acórdão 1133/2025 + the expected decision from President Segura — as of 22.04.2026, the law has NOT yet been promulgated. This means the Sephardic route technically still exists in the current text of Lei 37/81 under the regime of Lei 2/2022. Once Segura signs it — the route will close.
2ffd8dc113c171be21f164d521cd90f385e4fe74: If you plan to apply — do so BEFORE the promulgation of the new law. After cancellation, it will be practically impossible to restore the route.
760cbe228297c0957066a4b68b4b0075f425aa0c: IRN — Nationality by effect of will or origin
9f39de89521e02991756c68f84f736f28843e974: Lei 2/2022 — tightening of the Sephardic route
a78faabae30781517be7da5c094623fc4bcca08f: Citizenship of Portugal through marriage or de facto union
b22265c997823917c9a7ffa204db2bae7fc4ba4b: Spouses of Portuguese citizens can apply for citizenship after 3 years of marriage (or 3 years of registered união de facto). A CIPLE language certificate is formally NOT required for this route. However, up to 6 years of marriage, the IRN (Instituto dos Registos e Notariado) / Ministério Público may request proof of 'ligação efetiva à comunidade nacional' (effective connection) — language informally helps. It is not necessary to reside in PT — one can live abroad with a Portuguese spouse. The new law (passed on 1 April 2026, awaiting the signature of President Seguro) will increase the marriage duration to 5 years and strengthen the requirement for actual cohabitation.
c7c1eaf0aa4b9c6677b37bcfae8c8ca7f6123f2b: Decreto AR 17/XVII, re-adopted by Parliament on 1 April 2026 after a veto by Marcelo (December 2025) and unconstitutionality by TC (Acórdão 1133/2025). It proposes to extend the marriage duration from 3 to 5 years, require proof of actual cohabitation (mandatory cohabitation proof), and raise the threshold for presumption of 'effective connections' from 6 to 7–8 years. Awaiting the signature of President Seguro (PS, in office since January 2026). Until promulgation, the rules of Lei 37/81 (3 years) apply.
6d4ac788d970ef3adad9df135fc970bd9667cd7b: The process is the same for all countries
1640f35fb899c75c50fa12d91843d1e8683f7e71: Unlike naturalisation (after 5/7/10 years of residence), the marriage or união de facto process does NOT depend on the applicant's country of origin. Citizens of CPLP, EU, and third countries — the conditions are the same. There are no 'accelerated' routes for Brazilians or Italians — 3 years of marriage is required for everyone.
f3233226756838618f60e3fac345dcd9a9fe613a: 💍 Marriage to a Portuguese citizen for ≥ 3 years at the time of application (or união de facto ≥ 3 years, registered at the Conservatória)
85c5e4eb5c36137dfd7ec8e06bd98c21b9b28ca9: 📜 Valid marriage at the time of application (no divorce, no separation with legal status)
e134cc336cbf81d23d82b033e22e04fd7b8bfc3e: ⚖️ No criminal convictions in any country with a penalty of ≥ 3 years imprisonment
61c444f0ac6f0f366a8e876d0c6fab81f0eccc41: Formally NOT required for this route:
00b0da37440341d874f60cc6a3235e659f71f025: ❌ CIPLE A2 certificate — formally NOT required (language only for naturalisation after 5+ years of residence). In practice, the IRN may assess language proficiency as an indirect indicator of 'effective connection' for marriages <6 years
02ddb0e8464f238f8b2d04fe820941ad6b2b08fb: ❌ Residence in Portugal — not mandatory, the foreign spouse can live with the Portuguese partner abroad
387a9c7e091aa782a05ede03a5d28ded7b7d79f4: ❌ 'Connection to Portugal' as a formal checklist — not requested at the application stage. But see below about presumption
67b2f6d0052abd4ee4502976551acdb03c9eeeb6: An important nuance regarding Lei 37/81 art. 3: for marriage of 3–6 years, it is MANDATORY to prove "ligação efetiva à comunidade nacional" (effective connection to the Portuguese community) — this is requested by the Ministério Público in case of opposition. For marriages of ≥6 years OR when there are joint Portuguese children, the presumption applies, and proving the connection is not necessary. In other words, "not required" is a simplification for cases of 6+ years.
46ba1d33c314e154890005748e5662df70f9770e: Portugal recognises união de facto (de facto union) as equivalent to marriage for citizenship purposes. Registration:
aa461dfc22a0d25ebfb753bd48d39e8742757203: 📋 Cohabitation of ≥ 2 years (proven by atestado de residência from Junta de Freguesia + joint bills, rental agreement in both names)
2a034d70ca96bc16290c933c4ce95c361e5e8ba0: 📝 Sworn declaration at the Conservatória do Registo Civil (not at Junta — this is a notarial act)
42abe9f149216759418bd4c44be46303ff40ef9c: 🕒 Registration is instant; certificado de união de facto is issued on the same day
0fb89eba6f4af69ee03b36706801954e73b8df03: After registration, the 3-year period is counted from the start date of cohabitation (according to witness statements), not from the date of registration. Therefore, if you have already lived together for 5 years but are only registering now — the "3 years" requirement has already been fulfilled.
959edae6ef4ee783b5df877c43ba879dae3502ba: 📄 Marriage certificate (if the marriage was not concluded in Portugal — apostille + sworn translation into Portuguese). If the marriage is in PT — the assento number from Conservatória is sufficient
558a66bcb425d654ed222d18ad078c5a90c7f3d4: 📄 For união — certificado de união de facto

cd481d31f28ed66f1c4f52088bb8cf8ab2dddda2: 📄 Título de residência (residence permit) — to be attached if available, but NOT mandatory. For submission abroad (via consulate) — not required.
9fc079e7d11ac826e89e852228ca4c221588ff9a: 📄 Criminal record certificate from Portugal (certidão do registo criminal) — free at eportugal.gov.pt
dd8793d36600e2f4941d4b78a6c503dd5269878b: 📄 Criminal record certificate from the country of citizenship and all countries of residence for ≥ 1 year in the last 5 years — apostille + sworn translation
6e9f62d7933ee686c0666a95aa519aa66b5575a5: 📄 Birth certificate of the Portuguese spouse (assento de nascimento — requested automatically through IRN, or attached)
85058515c9f0ac15df59c6f879783f59b9a9993e: 📝 Completed form (modelo 1-B) — download from irn.pt, signed by the applicant
8b4d4ae31fefc2248030cf9d87eced3841f21b55: 💶 Proof of payment of the fee — €250
7ffdda55e1535c395abed65f9a1c8e70a63561ee: The applicant does NOT have a Cartão de Cidadão (CC) — this is a Portuguese citizen document; it is issued only after obtaining citizenship. The documents should include a título de residência (if available) or passport.

312661ae831e837fec98a99d8aa9da2502bed94d: After a positive decision, IRN registers you as a citizen in the Assento de Nascimento. Only after this can you request a Cartão de Cidadão at any Loja do Cidadão and obtain a Portuguese passport from IRN.
17bad1aea0ff1a029437af58a8955f3fd91ad499: ❗ Different transliteration of the name in the passport and marriage certificate — an affidavit from a notary confirming identity will be required
95583bccda64b444a5bfd6c685bde58e3c6c00d8: ❗ Marriage concluded in a third country is not registered in the Conservatória — first, register the marriage in PT, then citizenship
240c4041b802430f5bad497997dbe067a02acddf: ❗ Portuguese spouse recently obtained citizenship (naturalisation) — the '3 years of marriage' period is counted from the date the spouse acquired citizenship, not from the wedding date

27fd9f8c009e02cf2129342b5a9816966aed21a8: IRN — Nationality by marriage or união de facto
6024272e290ee86ed85c5e34b532831a84417268: Standard route: legal residence for 5 years + knowledge of Portuguese A2 + no criminal record. 'Connection to Portugal' is officially NOT a mandatory requirement (the law does not mention it); IRN may assess it in contentious cases, but the fact of 5 years of legal residence is already considered sufficient. Important: at the time of application, you do not yet have a Cartão de Cidadão — it is issued only after obtaining citizenship. A título de residência is attached.
28933b3c3efbf1faf528e73ffb50c4c3f3c1a93b: The Parliament re-adopted the law on 1 April 2026 (after Marcelo's December veto and TC's unconstitutionality). Awaiting the signature of President Seguro. PS sent a companion decree to TC. Below are both scenarios.
aa5c82095d54f48b37576ff96cabf39dc20037a9: Decreto AR 17/XVII — national reform, re-adopted by Parliament on 1 April 2026 after Marcelo's veto (December 2025) and partial unconstitutionality TC Acórdão 1133/2025. Awaiting the signature of President António José Seguro (PS, in office since January 2026). If signed, the naturalisation period will increase: 7 years for CPLP+EU, 10 years for others; waiting time for MdI / first residence permit will NO longer be counted; a cultural integration test will be added; the fast Sephardic route has been cancelled. PS has already sent a separate companion decree on 'loss of citizenship as an additional penalty' — regarding fiscalisation on 17 April 2026 to TC. Applications submitted BEFORE promulgation will follow the old rules.
9eb76fc525da91771189dedff63ea57bb424d2d9: According to art. 6 Lei 37/81 (Nationality Law) and Decreto-Lei 237-A/2006, the requirements are as follows:
b1a9a3f0e3619322358aa4b12825b904c770c483: 📅 Legal residence in Portugal ≥ 5 years (continuously or with breaks)
1876c1d9eef99f02b261055850e8cdcf9d194f00: 🗣️ Knowledge of Portuguese A2 — confirmed by a certificate (CIPLE, DEPLE, school diploma ≥ 150 hours, diploma from a Portuguese university, completion of 6th grade in a PT school). Not required for CPLP citizens.
32eb201b5320f57f9203b6ffbdb4acb0fe43b4b3: ⚖️ No criminal record with a sentence of ≥ 3 years imprisonment — in any country of residence
2d87a30eac47282c1c7b4df4932838edb5971ef7: 🛡️ Does not pose a threat to national security (rarely applied in practice)
c4f6df7e11d6df960bf68337cbfafb8fb3288f81: What is NOT in the law (contrary to popular belief):
193739c341ec0a88b15b8e14cdd67f7e553b2834: ❌ 'Connection to Portugal' is NOT a formal requirement in art. 6. It may only be requested in cases of obvious signs of fictitious residence. 5 years of legal residence permit is considered sufficient evidence.
2551663cc3df6b3633994033e2ed6b27f8ed90b2: ❌ A cultural/historical test is not required (until the new law is promulgated in 2026)
75fb5db89117598e313e68fc60cc6e9db35f5c6d: ❌ Renouncing previous citizenship is not required — Portugal allows dual citizenship
f6e79fb93802e3858ab64ce8fdb1bc00ba964052: How to count 5 years — a key question
dd8a242a49f5150914dcf97d76f01ebc758b8f01: The period of '5 years of legal residence' is counted from the date of the first ISSUANCE of the residence permit (título de residência / autorização de residência), and NOT from the date of entry or the date of application for the residence permit.
b56467a58426185ba26d4dccf878500694e027c6: ✅ Since 2018 (Lei 2/2020): the entire period of 'legal stay' is counted, including the waiting time for the first residence permit, if the residence permit was subsequently issued
de93c7ffcdb3c6eeb014163c27ce967fbea856b2: ✅ Manifestação de Interesse (expression of interest): waiting periods with comprovativo de submissão from AIMA are also counted towards these 5 years — this has been confirmed by the case law of the Supremo Tribunal Administrativo
e32a6188e1a3b9acbadde5fb86a64e1e78f18e0a: ✅ Short trips (holidays, business trips) do not interrupt the period
7355e3b6e539c85affa813abdb7e69e4c0802992: ⚠️ A long absence of > 6 consecutive months or > 8 months in total over 2 years may interrupt — it depends on the type of residence permit
644219883a2789474e1a9aa2737cf5191b1e7794: ⚠️ If the residence permit is expired and not renewed in time — there is a break in legality. After renewal, the period is counted anew from the date of restoration
aca0c2191a0d5b2c9168f07b5d34d4c993708843: For accurate counting, it is useful (but NOT mandatory) to obtain a 'Certidão de residência legal' from AIMA — it confirms the dates of all residence permits and waiting periods. The certificate can be requested online at portal.aima.gov.pt or by e-mail to the regional delegation.
adc0bbdd56511ec9614e6b6ef430ccf07b92f131: The law has changed several times. If your 5 years fall under different versions — clarify with a lawyer who has immigration practice. For example: the rules for Manifestação differed in 2018/2022/2025, and how these periods are counted — is sometimes a matter of dispute.
df19bba58f943e872f8f67531a72f9f4e53f6405: If President Seguro signs the new law (status as of 22 April 2026)
4ad88babfbf885ce58a1400469adc3772bbaafed: Brief timeline: 28 October 2025 — Parliament passed Decreto AR 17/XVII. 15 December 2025 — TC Acórdão 1133/2025 declared 4 provisions unconstitutional. End of December 2025 — President Marcelo vetoed. January 2026 — new president António José Seguro (PS). 1 April 2026 — Parliament re-passed the law (152 votes). 17 April 2026 — PS sent a companion decree (loss of citizenship as an additional penalty) to TC. The main decree as of 22 April 2026 is still on Seguro's desk.

51eadcc0e66b58a9bf018de6542f4e1b26281736: ⏱️ The waiting time for Manifestação de Interesse / first residence permit is no longer counted — the period is only counted from the date of issuance of the first título de residência

b9390f3db891133290f50a6c096f8674f307a867: 🏠 Stricter requirements for actual presence — it is not enough to simply have a residence permit, you must physically live in PT

616d49899807f92e759afc647a1d6083c5090993: 🔴 New ground for 'loss' of citizenship upon conviction for ≥5 years for particularly serious crimes (separate companion decree in TC on fiscalisation from 17 April 2026)

76fabada2e61d753f428e3a22107ebbf20551d68: Recommendation: if you have already completed your 5 years — apply immediately, without delay. Submitting halts the case under the current legislative regime.

f8742b6ab8423931b0c961ff30207f1928d23e50: What is advisable to attach, but not officially mandatory:

f9827321b8f115cc9d5c8b68b8266062a190a565: All foreign documents must be apostilled (or legalised by the consulate) + sworn translation into Portuguese (tradutor-intérprete ajuramentado). A list of sworn translators: on the website of the Ministério dos Negócios Estrangeiros.
c2177591155d15b2c3bb988a4e7137859397e39c: 🏛️ Any Conservatória do Registo Civil in your place of residence

9a33222721cbbf054c0862bbc4da980aca106dcd: Official deadline — 1 year from the date of application. Actual 2026 — 24–48 months (on average 36 months). Backlog at IRN >200,000 applications. Online applications are processed faster.
ba4803db220c318c4516b6313c8435f0cc26b015: After approval, IRN publishes the decision in the Diário da República. Only from the date of publication are you a citizen of Portugal. After that: request Cartão de Cidadão at any Loja do Cidadão (portrait + biometrics, €18 standard issue / €33 urgent / €53 very urgent — IRN rates 2026), order a Portuguese passport at IRN (€85), register newborn children as PT citizens.
03e614dd95e197fc0addc9556ee97dba52710a81: For CPLP citizens: under the current law — 5 years, under the new law 2026 — 7 years. Language A2 is automatically counted (Portuguese — native/official in the country of origin, CIPLE is not required).
cafebf36e8cd6c8cd03f14fd1083d84105d29591: For EU/EEA/Swiss citizens: 5 years under the current law, under the new law 2026 — 7 years (EU are equated to CPLP). Language A2 — CIPLE or an equivalent certificate is required. Dual citizenship is allowed.
c627fbb610741f567e6e4eb202d8a1540f89fdb2: For third-country citizens: 5 years under the current law, 10 years under the new law 2026. Language A2 (CIPLE or school certificate from 150 hours). Dual citizenship is permitted by most countries — check your country's law.

afd98e0d0e3c9fc00e8852b046c02042f1fa38f7: AIMA is overwhelmed: the line 808 202 653 is often busy, email responses take 2–6 weeks, and in-person appointments are by prior arrangement only. The real way: AIMA Portal (portal.aima.gov.pt), book through Espaço Cidadão, or contact the linha dedicada 800 203 373 for residents who already have a process. For urgent cases — email by region, list below.
278c427f484717a2f63a44768f9614aadbe8c1c9: Main channel from 2026. Access via Chave Móvel Digital (national e-ID) or NIF+password. Case status, document submission, notifications, appointment booking. Covers 80% of inquiries without calls.
eb6db674b0a8af516fba5c548b7d2ecc2644da8c: Some Espaço Cidadão have AIMA windows. Book at eportugal.gov.pt → 'Agendamento (appointment booking) AIMA'. Usually 2–4 weeks waiting time, but specific assistance for the case.
02f66aa89bcfe3094d6f04cd46cc9635827edf3c: Often busy; the best chance is the first 30 minutes after opening
aa048136a3217034b56a795d9729fb5e6cd19622: Speak clearly in Portuguese, English is possible but slower
8eedbfb5d998e81927f8bda2a5b1949d8350ff8d: For repeat calls — alternate the number with the area code
8133713ae5027526e1a654aa7c0108d463c8e67f: Free line for those with an OPEN process in AIMA. A process number (processo) is needed. The hours are the same. Useful for status inquiries, complaints, emergency assistance.
b7b4021e0a5b22e4953c069108f4ba39850cf415: Always include the processo, full name, NIF (Portuguese tax ID), and submission date. Attach PDF documents.
aabcc3d9103afe34303b5cbd48c797fdd06d6c18: The AIMA line 808 202 653 is often busy — the community has come up with workarounds. These tools are unofficial but widely used and save hours of repeated calls:
0230e66b1d51b9f96e41dda11f563781680edb96: 🤖 Telegram bot @callaimabot — automatic call to SEF/AIMA. You send the bot your number and process, it calls the AIMA line every N minutes until it gets through, and connects you with an operator. Free of charge
2f12ac2ff64c1a9967e4498acac8fdd248a47618: 📱 Community @aimacall — publishes successful call windows (message statistics)
25c6448c36e2125c7ae484f351eab1803303fe97: 💬 Chat @aimasefgroup — typical questions are resolved collectively: checking DUC, CN codes, payment errors
eff834d2c9e63a5eda29342771375baad1904cb4: ⚠️ These services are NOT official, AIMA does not regulate them. Your personal data is shared with a third party — decide if you are comfortable with that
4da66ec57fd49af9d8247993e93c9cd2e2c5a5e7: Practice: how to get a response
2cb48b0df7cfa6bb9e525a0769eeb6346dc0aed3: 📅 Booking at AIMA through the Portal — average waiting time is 15–60 days. Book in advance
1539c2e0887f0d8cec27e3f22e99702874a6cee6: ☎️ It’s better to call on Tuesday–Thursday. Monday and Friday are dead. The first 30 minutes after opening is the highest chance to get through
aff7504035523c9ac581fbde7c1a6d5067daf6af: 🗣️ Script: “Bom dia, tenho o processo número X e gostaria de saber o estado” (good morning, I have process X, I would like to know the status)
6c2244caa105d7652f4c87fd90add17ad0a5f4ac: 📧 Emails without a process are often ignored. Always attach documents or a screenshot from the Portal
5a3cd6c24a83b1df09aa98c9bf66936eda5a7627: 📮 Petition to the Provedor de Justiça (ombudsman) for long delays > 90 days — really speeds things up
633eab8db3e808de32ce7e92e8f3d03c2cb88f46: ⚖️ Injunction / Mandado de Execução in court — a last resort if AIMA does not respond > 180 days
1002ebaf7847bfae010c5e54dc4b641800bf0891: 📍 Lisbon Central — Av. António Augusto de Aguiar, 20
e83be159597d9f35101c04acedb183908f7cff9b: 📍 Porto Central — Rua D. João IV, 863
3d34023c3956a6e13bee849f4184561649175919: 📍 Funchal — Rua Nova da Rochinha 1, Madeira
a3b2081f2ea09fe50da81904031c503a4e12e42e: 📍 Ponta Delgada — Rua Eng. José Cordeiro, Azores
d7b28bdb5c5ed9f015651f6f3e87a42fa9cd283c: ⚠️ Without prior appointment, they do not accept. Walk-in is not available from 2024.
8d9d2f67d4db134dc7a7cf695aed678ff8172a2e: AIMA refusal → appeal to court within 30 days (Tribunal Administrativo)
71dcd3feb035afe94a4aa2aa0a16c6c20b882957: Silence > 90 days on petition → complaint to Provedor de Justiça (provedor-jus.pt)
4c357493420d49b343eb25acb4283681d74820e4: Employee misconduct → Inspecção-Geral dos Serviços (IGAI) of the ministry
21e84a757f7d0add16854fd25ff15ef2ba881d9d: Issues with residence permit and risk of deportation → urgent lawyer from Ordem dos Advogados (Bar Association of Portugal) with immigration practice
d7753e67fb0a5a2f909bca1c48b881e83130178d: Vulnerable group (refugees, minors) → CPR (Conselho Português para os Refugiados)
40892263d017a88d058584d53f34baf53b35a6d0: Renewal of residence permit through the AIMA online portal
64f8c2fc96f4d3c4ad51a62fe99b4e35b6374cf7: From 1 January 2026, the renewal of the residence permit will be done through portal-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt. No visit required if all documents are in order. Applications can be submitted 30 days before the card expires, but it is advisable to do so 60–90 days in advance. The residence permit letter will be sent by post, and while you wait, your legal status is confirmed by the comprovativo de submissão.
e774717bd80806d36477bbbc81b979fc84610dba: The launch of portal-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt is on 01.01.2026. The deadline for renewing expired residence permits until 30.06.2025 is 15 April 2026.
006ccbe596238469cc8f571394ff91a7718b6b90: In 2026, renewals go through several portals — it is important to choose the right one, otherwise the system will not 'see' your process:
3bc2cc8691c2a897c29f1338cf93e361cb8636a7: ✅ portal-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt — cards expiring from 1 July 2025 to 30 June 2026 (main channel)
c6fcee98130c7d3c18451324057ce68d29bbb18b: ✅ portal-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt — ARI / Golden Visa, expiring from 22 February 2020 to 30 June 2026
3ef5abbc4691ed1819dd9c2e5d1fda9421bf4174: ⚠️ Cards that expired BEFORE 30 June 2025 — through the AIMA main services portal (old channel), not through renovações
233e9e900add440a93ebaae3b0419d676900fbad: ❌ Cartão de Residência UE (for EU citizens) — renewal through this portal is currently unavailable; please contact Junta de Freguesia (for CRUE) or directly AIMA
9bdc9ed31dc52d4fae7542b7a6b041425c0efcc4: Do not confuse porta-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt with the general porta.aima.gov.pt (single portal). It is renovações that contains the renewal form.
332c5d5b10d6110f4477cef6a09765dd97e0fad9: 🗓️ Officially — from 30 days before the expiration of the residence permit
cd173b30ff40b8003842f56efaff5aef4d8375fd: ✅ Recommendation: 60–90 days before expiration (less stress, more time to correct mistakes)
fa28afbf4fe809437cba5beb68eb0b256097c502: ⚠️ Less than 30 days — the portal may accept it, but no guarantee it will be processed before expiration
616444db9b09809c7788050fd7a15bf34b8a45b6: ⏰ If the residence permit has already expired: apply immediately. Formally, the fine is €75–300 (art. 192 Lei 23/2007), but in 2025–2026 when renewing online, it is rarely enforced
0d60913fd4fbabc7cb64ad710ead23c390d66aea: 🛡️ Decreto-Lei 41/2023: if AIMA has not processed your request on time — you remain in legal status with the comprovativo
8d6d4d1b4d9404e7c3dbd618d1c38c3ba4ea81b8: All residence permits expiring before 30 June 2025 must be renewed by 15 April 2026. After this date, the status is considered invalid, and restoration is through a complicated procedure with a lawyer.
6012afe1eae805a9a21b156d97a98d99a2b6116a: 🔐 Chave Móvel Digital activated with signing function (not just authorisation). Check at autenticacao.gov.pt
8eac9e4f87b0536f2a993bb411a10436920dcd50: 📱 Current PT phone number in CMD (not foreign) — SMS codes will be sent to this number
6b3e8e901a6c1c0629b80710ba9885d03d148c29: 📄 Passport valid for at least 3 months after the planned date of receiving the new residence permit card
30db3755cb01c719ecb331c310b142274a7f620e: 🏠 Address matches: in AIMA, Finanças, NISS, and on the atestado de residência (if you plan to attach it)
cea0502820bdd1885c092c6a653057f1091d2ddd: 💼 NISS is active and without missed payments (mandatory from April 2025)
c894c511056e90fe62d2c8ccabf00ee6ef03a429: 💸 No debts with Finanças (certidão de não dívida) and Segurança Social
85830d2e8aebf84a5a16624aeb7ce2c2fe691853: 📷 Biometric data — if it has been more than 5 years since your last visit, an in-person appointment for new biometric data may be required (rare, but it happens)
e3f4854b056165f12a1b938ad01cf0b2dbef646d: Authorisation via Chave Móvel Digital (government electronic signature). For PT citizens — via Cartão de Cidadão (Portuguese citizen card). Important: this is a separate portal, not portal.aima.gov.pt (general portal) — the link is specifically for renewals.
a43364003ea517cc41232de3e72b4e7e6fdf5c25: 2. Select the type of residence permit
95d6d8dd62dae2190b47c186447596472f106ee6: The system will suggest the type based on your current residence permit (automatically detected). Check — if incorrect, your process may still be in the old SEF database, a separate ticket is needed via email to the regional delegation.
97984904cc4973f6257221ec617b164f820a783a: Passport — all filled pages, good quality JPG/PDF
ed8fd97d3eb7d53465bce268d314848f4e37b02d: Atestado de residência (recent, not older than 3 months) from Junta de Freguesia (parish council) OR rental contract registered with Finanças, OR utility bill in your name
f317d90b266989372648875e9e1ddd2eaa2fbd01: Criminal record certificate PT (certidão do registo criminal) — free at eportugal.gov.pt, valid for 3 months
7d6ef19df2e42e18becb4d96b8e88bc97498019f: For D7/D8 — proof of income: bank statement for 3 months, employment/client contract, pension documents
e4ddfab4281d4e19c63dd503aab8e4ced4cd4f80: For reagrupamento familiar — marriage certificate/birth certificates of children
ac1fb52a8f046521dd4070937c79b13cbd882a52: Proof of NISS + absence of debts (Segurança Social Direta → Comprovativo de situação contributiva)
5962b72d76afae57c5b17ce8efd4dc6fa342948d: Proof from Finanças — certidão de não dívida (free at Portal das Finanças)
59d467c93b801cef1561ab34ae3a9cfcafb014ed: Proof of health insurance or SNS utente (if required for your type of residence permit)
d77429dd26997f72434767a95711fac5c4454338: Practice: file size limit of 5 MB per document. If it doesn't fit — compress it at ilovepdf.com or a similar site. All scans must be in colour, not photos, and readable.
aa89714a9726eced7a914985bb761990ebb9a0af: The portal generates a Multibanco reference (DUC — Documento Único de Cobrança) or allows payment by card. From 1 March 2026, a new AIMA fee table will be in effect (+33% to the old one): application + analysis €133, granting/renewal of residence permit (art. 75) ~€307.20, permanent (art. 76) ~€351.10, second copy of the card — 50% of issuance. Payment is confirmed within 1–3 hours, but sometimes takes up to a day.
a261960eb1d73ea42b7e74cace3931214b86df8d: If the amount is paid partially (a typical mistake is to fall short due to the exchange rate): pay the remainder using the SAME DUC, but through a DIFFERENT bank (not the one from which the first payment was made). Avoid making additional payments through CTT or Payshop — they often do not register. You cannot create a new DUC for additional payment.
2321f607b541d1d4178f76f30dc18d63eaa58ec3: The final step is the electronic signature of the application using Chave Móvel Digital. You will receive an SMS code or a push notification in the Autenticação.gov app.
e12a4ad4bbabe40da7a32ff1938059c833b87b0c: After submission, the portal issues a comprovativo de submissão in PDF. THIS IS A CRUCIAL DOCUMENT. Save it everywhere: cloud, email, print it out. It confirms your legal status until you receive the new card and is accepted instead of the residence permit by banks, schools, SNS, and at the border when returning to PT.
f5a74649a2d19ba23e6b0715a9bfdbc4d6da4c85: Standard time is 30–90 days. The maximum by law is up to 120 days. Lei 61/2025 (in force from 23 October 2025) established a general deadline for AIMA decisions of 9 months for complex cases. The EU directive on the single permit requires PT to decide on single-permit applications within 90 days from 22 May 2026. Notification is sent to your email + in your personal account. Statuses: “Em análise”, “Notificado para entrevista”, “Aprovado”, “Indeferido”.
480876ab05239d4c116e81ebbb601db19e1d0c39: 7a. Comprovativo de Deferimento — your temporary residence permit
d1b3b2a9870c046740f224761a04f755b302c205: As soon as the process is approved, a button “Comprovativo de Deferimento” appears in your personal account — a PDF with a QR code for authenticity verification. This document confirms the positive decision BEFORE the physical card arrives.
1e984291b48f3d040b3b02ae579667974be007f8: 🪪 Accepted by banks, schools, SNS, and employers as proof of legal status
f7b4e0f394dabbf15c8e2c9c79977ee1542f730b: ✈️ At the EU border and with airlines — together with the old card (or without it, if it is the first issuance) and your passport
b6dbcc4c62650d4e88732939208afdb377076fae: 📱 Save the PDF in the cloud and on your phone — it is accessed more often than the card itself
04ab70c91fa984cd0d9d8a7019fcb81d379834de: ⚖️ The QR code allows the verifier to instantly validate the document through AIMA
e1e472b69f76b284f5a490a608c112868a70251f: The new plastic residence permit arrives by CTT registered mail at the address specified in the application. If you have not received it within 30 days after the status 'Aprovado' — write to the regional AIMA delegation.
e7936ad050dd38367ca6515dfb2f5b7378015113: The CTT tracking number is NOT displayed in the AIMA personal account after approval — you have to find out by calling the regional delegation. It is better to call two weeks after the status 'Aprovado'.
b2ab001947aeccc7174c202ef24c51d8293cc918: 🖥️ Submit from a desktop in Chrome — there are often file upload issues on mobile
9e579d14cdb2c1fa61112e61ec2385b5400fb591: ⏰ Do not submit at 08:00 / 09:00 on Monday morning — the portal is overloaded, increasing the chance of technical errors. The best hours are Tuesday to Thursday, 14:00–17:00
3c172b294ba9f30f23c1ce7dcdb1784bcf494ba3: 📸 If the process 'hangs' on one screen — take a screenshot, DO NOT close the tab, wait 2 minutes
d67278a2e377f61524986c3f636732815a8c7a01: 🚫 Do not attempt to submit again if the first attempt 'froze' — check your personal account after 24 hours, often the application was still sent
8212bd16b71c5bb4e168364abf12420537f2984c: 📋 Atestado de residência (issued by Junta) is sometimes given without witnesses — check with your Junta. A utility bill or rental contract is often sufficient as an alternative
56651b31f36e88ddd35845ebc7af224f24affdc1: 📷 Passport photos are not needed in scans — AIMA uses the biometrics from the previous appointment or calls for a new one if necessary
daa57737e66f4f526c4aeb7118a6c50e209b6423: 🧾 Order a certidão de não dívida from Finanças and Segurança Social in advance — they may take 1–2 days to generate
00f9d77f681298807744590373bce63722fc4117: ⚡ For residence permits under D7/D8 — the bank statement must show STABLE income (not a one-off large sum before submission)
fbe1a507d5fd16236b92588bb7755b6c4d52819d: 👨‍👩‍👦 For reagrupamento familiar: children under 16 are automatically included, teenagers aged 16–18 apply separately for their extension
80eda9c03e64a0b197106fd37ac9cca6727a537d: If there is an error in your data in the AIMA database (for example, incorrect transliteration of your name, incorrect address from the old SEF process) — submission through the portal is often blocked. The solution is to email the regional delegation requesting to correct the data, then submit.
7362f7ce60f43ae53b2ae3ba6dfa4ef2acbceaf6: Rejection or request for additional documents
868719c2ad9269858d31419849cec04acc6685d6: If after submission you receive a letter requesting additional documents (Notificação para aperfeiçoamento):
c65b2d22809ca6beae7fbb2a3e55fb66f67a5cde: 📬 Deadline for response — 10 working days from the date of notification
3a0a7bbdbdcb1fe0178557155196112025138a69: 💻 Additional documents must be uploaded through the same portal-renovacoes, tab 'Processos em curso'
1ebbbd2b25b0400e57e7587cb8052bb437c195ab: 📧 If the deadline is insufficient — you can request an extension via email to the regional delegation
d9e10c482a04a001a42770a91232755d81f796a5: 🚫 Missing the deadline = automatic rejection (indeferimento por falta de elementos)
93e9e844f963b0a94147f906693e9cd0e2a03896: ⚖️ Deadline for appeal — 30 days from the date of notification (recurso hierárquico in AIMA or judicial in Tribunal Administrativo)
897e033f81764ae3a2102ed1be6c2fcc5c11c24d: 🧑‍💼 We recommend hiring an advogado with immigration practice — the chance of reconsideration is higher
3e34c8065c915d3a7fb1ee851f525c87cb1b055f: 🏛️ In parallel — complaint to the Provedor de Justiça (ombudsman), especially if the rejection is formal
03b65b14793ad218f32852c6ebebc5ae81ccf735: ⏰ While the appeal is ongoing — your status is usually maintained by comprovativo
b6593985d0e24afe7bb4c7bd295d35a6e9e34c0d: AIMA has delayed the process — what to do
b1ba5b65132b2c70c3fd65559a16d848694bc1d2: Standard timeframe — 30–90 days. If 90 days have passed without a response — it’s time to take action. The maximum by law is 120 days, after which AIMA violates the procedural deadline.
7f93a597cff52723b852293115db16d77f605db4: 🛡️ You remain in legal status — Decreto-Lei 41/2023 directly protects you, as long as you have comprovativo
5b0e3b4a0aa11b342fd4b4078455f6c8aead465c: 📧 90+ days — email the regional delegation of AIMA (see the guide 'How to Contact AIMA') with the processo number and submission date
a22a95b08f5aba6b7c14905de3e819f3f7b2677a: 🏛️ 120+ days — complaint to the Provedor de Justiça at provedor-jus.pt (actually speeds things up, AIMA is obliged to respond to the ombudsman)
0ec6c4546a201dbcc741ea9624b45ee67ffba4d1: ⚖️ 180+ days — Intimação para prestação de informações at the Tribunal Administrativo. Paid procedure (~€200), but forces AIMA to respond within 2 months
cff3d4e63e0055bb5c000731cddc218750bfae5a: ✈️ For trips abroad — comprovativo PDF + old residence permit card. Airlines and EU border officials are obliged to accept. In case of issues — contact the Portuguese consulate in the transit country
c8d7378f7fbc9fa05ff40a820fcb2560ae95b552: Special cases — not through the main portal
316c4fbe0406067eb98ff45c68cc8a99f6a85d65: Some categories require a separate procedure, not through portal-renovacoes:
a31e262bdbcf02f3a2736268d7c6c2d06802fffb: Submission via AIMA feedback form (not through renovações)
0d82140b82387c35185be8d726ba89904a430163: Request a slot for biometrics — only through lista de espera
fc8389786feedbe1c08d501bd98d6dbf155cb871: Documents: passport, old residence permit, IRS tax declaration, employment contract, NISS, certidão de não dívida
1cf3162a190f024eaef1980328607de6629532b9: Waiting time for a slot — 2–6 months
557461973c8dd35e4e4db619860bd2331dc653cd: Register in lista de espera through the form on the AIMA website
d80fbddf486adafa54b04406a9d332c252d0e08b: Documents: copy of passport, proof of enrolment in the course (IEFP / certified centre), payment receipt
cef039cbac25f253f8242c78ae84cdfb4d938252: Later, AIMA will schedule a personal appointment — wait for the call via email
4795743eba43adeceab73859da066b2e0be78494: Renewal is possible through portal-renovacoes for ARI that expired between 22 February 2020 and 30 June 2026. Minimum physical presence in PT is 7 days per year (confirmed by stamps or comprovativo de alojamento).
b6363256842fb56e0d33bd96ed136e788b960afd: CRUE is issued and renewed through Junta de Freguesia or Câmara Municipal, not through AIMA. The renewal portal currently does not support CRUE.
34b35b0e7a6ee2c1e1fa32a36ccf68e433572082: Features of the first renewal (immediately after visa D)
060d46852e33f65c63d16df4027387bf22c13aee: The first renewal differs from subsequent ones: it is formally not a 'renewal', but an 'initial issuance' (emissão inicial) based on visa D. The process is the same, but the scrutiny is stricter:
083d2fa5af1379c5a525f32aaaffb87f036291cc: 📄 They check the compliance of visa D and the current situation (D7 → passive income; D8 → remote work; etc.)
de91b45ef29fd06674c3012862e9451a612e6ae2: 💰 Requirement to confirm income FOR THE ENTIRE TIME since entry (full bank statement)
a8109797690d177289fe8d0393929d7bd1058cf8: 🏠 Atestado de residência — recent (not older than 2 months)
36c218a911f6f7106d7c361cfc85e7dac2b86402: 📝 If there was a condition in visa D (for example, 'open bank account in 90 days') — attach proof of compliance
38bc093bf8bd70f5ccf321b4a3d1ccfda285c30f: ⏰ The issuance period for the first card is up to 120 days (longer than usual renewal)
bc536fecb18a17765e19641cd240e1c9bd7c4f8c: no older than 3 months, from Junta de Freguesia
ccb46c3461bdd9a986c1405038bcb1417532057a: Certificate of no debt from Finanças
ec5ed5c193bdebbeb8c335f54e142eba27f90b38: Decreto-Lei 41/2023 — protection of status in case of AIMA delay
026b085d1755b9dba55822a8731d7b2d87abfef8: Legalisation through articles 88/89 Lei 23/2007 — when and how
c3b5c770d6333b5920b031a95f8d097a6174095e: Articles 88 and 89 of Lei 23/2007 — "extraordinary" legalisation for individuals already in Portugal: art. 88 for employees (trabalho subordinado), art. 89 for self-employed individuals and investors. Lei 61/2025 (in force from 23 October 2025) abolished the 88.2 / 89.2 route for NEW applicants. Applicable only if you had a comprovativo de MdI before 3 June 2024 (Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024). The new law requires visa D before entry.
a1bb6e4a3b87b2bedbfadb9c3b4a6b68e0022640: The Manifestação de Interesse (MdI) regime — the mechanism for applying under art. 88.2 from within PT without a visa — was closed in two phases:
22a176e5feb1af7a4ae08d55dd038dc78c1716c7: 📅 3 June 2024 — Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024 ceased accepting new MdI applications
51a2088c7182a30e94ee45abc62107f3e32c168d: 📅 23 October 2025 — Lei 61/2025 came into force; 88.2 / 89.2 routes revoked for new applicants
41e250b24c026ee70e2f4f8224943ae8b278a068: ✅ Those who submitted MdI before 3 June 2024 with documents (employment contract + NISS + NIF + atestado) — procedures for art. 88/89 continue to operate
ecc91f80ab897000b3faab0ceb325ef62bd3cc18: ❌ Those who entered after 3 June 2024 — only through visa D at the consulate
05f4959635aab1a77d50ad4bb486ea697fa5ff24: ⏳ AIMA is gradually processing the MdI backlog — appointments will continue until 2027.
095bffa43230a066fd72ee9c6abef7198b184f2e: Do not confuse this route with new residence permits (D7, D8, CPLP) — they operate from abroad through the consulate.
83cb01df41f12cec6a6945a263a4bd77b5f9f8d9: For individuals with a valid D2/D3 visa with the right to work. After entry and employment, application to AIMA.

178080de1d059a1a392cd7b12fffe66c6c60fd29: Manifestação de Interesse (expression of interest) has been cancelled — what to do instead
059a74115534c34213a4605570d65ca06c8a3c58: Manifestação de Interesse (MdI) — the mechanism for applying for a residence permit from within Portugal without a visa — has been phased out in two stages. 3 June 2024 — Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024 stopped accepting new MdI applications. Lei 61/2025 came into effect on 23 October 2025 and definitively cancelled the scheme; 31 December 2025 — deadline for applications during the transition period for those who had a comprovativo before 3 June 2024. Now all non-CPLP third countries need a visa at the consulate BEFORE entry. Previously submitted cases are processed under the old rules.
df19f2183678af87efd5311f08dbdaf54b9998c2: Lei 61/2025 came into effect on 23 October 2025 and cancelled the MdI scheme. New applications were no longer accepted as of 3 June 2024 under Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024. 31 December 2025 — deadline for the transition period. Cases submitted before 3 June 2024 are processed under the old rules.
d3f019cb96d4094679d8b82c8097e37b42ec1825: What it was and the timeline of cancellation
5f5f757520a521866349d280fc21f923be6bc57b: Manifestação de Interesse (MdI) — a tool that allowed foreigners who entered Portugal on a tourist visa or visa-free (with Schengen) to apply for a residence permit from within the country without returning home for a visa. It was in operation since 2017.
57d840abf3b303fcb651cbc295ef540f661b31bb: Problem: by the end of 2023 AIMA had a backlog of over 400,000 applications — the bureaucracy was overwhelmed. Therefore, the scheme was phased out in two stages:
b8f7b3c4d9357fd1bcbba69bc024ce532986d6e7: 📅 3 June 2024 — Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024: AIMA stopped accepting new MdI applications. Applications submitted BEFORE this date remained in the queue for processing
bf778df12e81dd066d485d8a1c68e18518293c1e: 📅 23 October 2025 — Lei 61/2025 came into effect: final cancellation of the MdI scheme in the new text of the law
9a783902c2a1cd6107c52a831394b2ec8206b7ff: 📅 31 December 2025 — deadline for submission during the transition period for those who had an action comprovativo before 3 June 2024 (chain of transitional application processing)
0be0121de4fa9d16af4d6e0103293f74f6aecb9b: After 3 June 2024, it is not possible to submit an MdI. After 31 December 2025, the transitional route is closed; the scheme is legally dead. Reason: aligning Portugal with EU standards and reducing the backlog.
07d398ff44bc00151bc9081cf40e6122cc4e2aa8: All non-CPLP third countries: go through a consular visa BEFORE entry.
906674eaa26730f8f0905624c0671515f853585a: Brazilians: still visa-free + application to AIMA for CPLP residence.
033b97f247f566fede08e5b0abead7eeae1fe1a8: Other CPLP: now only through a consular visa.
f16fca075fa0abc73c70c2ded5c872db2381c62e: If Manifestação de Interesse is submitted before 03.06.2024
73ed2afccc50991550109849e53d23a4f9493e69: Cases submitted before 03.06.2024 are processed under the old procedure
510911e1f0643c09894ea7df78a5f3eb908f1b96: AIMA must complete processing and either issue a residence permit or refuse
79c2cf8a14968c6de042957a2e2073dfd0b895fa: Processing time — continues even after 31.12.2025, until AIMA closes the backlog
ca157b410755de38d1ed943d0a4fa18a589e5f21: If refused — you can appeal or leave and apply for a consular visa
5365d695e8d5d5139d67f5ea2c7e50b73743b184: Process status — via the official AIMA portal or email of the regional AIMA delegation
f715922fc2c25e4712dd8066d8328c737f44dc1e: Long waiting ≠ legal presence. If 90 days of Schengen have expired and you continue to stay in PT without comprovativo de agendamento (appointment confirmation) — this is a violation. Old cases only protect those who submitted MdI before 1 June 2024 and have comprovativo de submissão in hand.
b2ebf5278b33702a851197def477077479f92862: ‘There will be an amnesty’ — no. The government has publicly stated that no amnesty is planned.
a1c5ec6c5e6096e57a8bd4f328e1cddf81211465: ‘The old process will be restored’ — no, Law 61/2025 has come into force and does not provide for reinstatement.

30c39fde3e770b56c736247ecfaf85da05152896: Timeline from visa issuance to residence permit
99eefebb561468e3cc0a82464ea9e19e58dd2ccd: 1. Received a D visa at the consulate — it is valid for 120 days from the date of entry
9d123ec9770f199325fdfbf9d35af99b7c7a0a08: 2. Entry into Portugal — passport stamp with date
85f489476493939c8a4b1d01e9ec79c3ad039845: 3. AIMA must issue an appointment for biometric collection and obtaining a residence permit within 120 days
5b0479aea3ee15bd66d21abbfffdf0ec6937334f: 4. Historically, AIMA used to make the appointment itself, now it is not automatic — sometimes you need to initiate it
07f0e76c80ed6bedc8c1be563be683625954ba41: 5. At the appointment — biometrics, document verification, signing of apostille
1fbef9b57021a51ca18f90119ede66ceb7c0876e: 6. The residence permit card arrives by post within 2–4 weeks (sometimes up to 90 days)
5a1a3a93968e237a922e2b1ede1a676653032e94: The expiration of 120 days without an appointment does NOT automatically make you illegal — the law protects you if you have done everything in your power. But it is better to seek an appointment in advance.

bd0df4d7047834b83676b997a92decc2baa45634: From 2024, the old NHR is abolished. In its place — IFICI (Incentivo Fiscal à Investigação Científica e Inovação) — 20% flat rate on income from qualified activities (science, high technology, startups) for 10 years. 98be364c99bca21e0caa2d6fc0d5658e76ba5878: The old NHR is closed as of 01.01.2024. Those who applied before this date retain NHR for 10 years from the start. New applicants — only IFICI (narrow list of professions).
9679ce1775a29f4cc90f2d6c4ec5aff95e08141c: NHR (Non-Habitual Resident) — the old regime from 2009-2023, offered 10% on pensions + 20% on qualified work + 0% on foreign income. Repealed in 2024.

46ee5332ff9cd23c293f196a756e5248d4207230: Key point: IFICI requires 'qualified activity' from a narrow list. Freelance remote workers without such status pay IRS on a progressive scale (11.97%–48% in 9 brackets for 2026).

3266e701ca01cb726832d19373cc59e214dba3fc: IRS = Imposto sobre o Rendimento das Pessoas Singulares. It is submitted annually from 1 April to 30 June in Portal das Finanças (tax portal). For residents, it is mandatory regardless of the source of income. There is an automatic pre-filled version (IRS Automático) for simple cases.

Required: open atividade (business activity), NIF of the client, amount, description of the service. IVA depends on the regime (normal 23% / exemption < €14,500/year). c4761f8e17aaba0873b733866a78fb0d415a9031: The PT market turned DIGI upside down (2024) — mobile from €5/month, fibre 1 Gbps for €10/month, fibre 10 Gbps for €15/month. NOS launched low-cost brand WOO (€5/month, 100 GB, no commitment). MEO/NOS/Vodafone — more expensive (€14.99–€15 for the same volume), but better coverage in villages and more TV packages. MEO's junior brands: Uzo and Moche (do not increase prices in 2026). For the contract, NIF, CC/residence permit, IBAN are required.
6b3f04a8f1cd5a0e5400db9a00115782d6d5c928: With the arrival of DIGI (November 2024, Romanian operator RCS&RDS), the PT market has been disrupted: their 1 Gbps fibre for €10/month and unlimited mobile for €6 forced competitors to respond with low-cost brands. NOS launched WOO, Altice/MEO promotes its brands Uzo and Moche without increases in 2026.

6f9c863ed5163243b8123c99bc337dc921c6bb24: Exchange of driving licences for Portuguese ones
4a7423ec2bc92f569894025fdc0e22fab17823fd: EU/EEA citizens — automatic recognition until expiry (without exchange). CPLP (Brazil, Cape Verde, etc.) — exchange without exam according to IMT. From third countries based on bilateral agreements (UA, KZ, MD, etc.) — exchange without exam. There is currently NO reciprocity with Russia and Belarus — only full theory + practical test. Ukrainian licences are recognised for the entire duration of Proteção Temporária (until 4 March 2027). The deadline for exchange after becoming a resident (non-EU) is 2 years (DL 138/2012).
acadacf789bfafced12f079ce5a3afff79b87e88: According to Decreto-Lei 138/2012, foreign driving licences (non-EU) are valid in Portugal **for 2 years from the date of becoming a resident**. After that, exchange or re-registration is mandatory, otherwise, there will be a fine and violation. For Ukrainians with Proteção Temporária — validity for the entire protection period (until 4 March 2027 under Regulation EU 2022/1280).

12c669c59f4b88df282a8006a0fc8cd760f86521: The Azores — a volcanic archipelago 1500 km from the mainland, consisting of 9 islands. The main airport is Ponta Delgada on São Miguel. Green craters, whales, hot springs, unique nature. Plan for at least 7-10 days for 2-3 islands. PT residents receive a subsidy for flights.
f0cd6796c07ab6a35956256d13b31398e1337e6e: The Azores — an autonomous region of PT. 9 volcanic islands, divided into 3 groups: eastern (São Miguel, Santa Maria), central (Terceira, Graciosa, São Jorge, Pico, Faial), western (Flores, Corvo). Total area 2333 km².
83a76a0ff8e90ea8d551989d1cb0005c41b6bbd3: The climate is mild subtropical (+14-24°C year-round), but changeable: “there can be 4 seasons in one day.” Plenty of greenery, lakes, waterfalls.
3523eee539dea64bd8211ce58dcb3de4c16f84d5: The largest island (760 km²), with the main airport. Half of the archipelago's population lives here. A minimum of 4-5 days is recommended.

8a5af202e716f04b8514f591a6c32afa5facf753: Coimbra — university city in the centre of the country, 1 hour from Lisbon or Porto by train. Universidade de Coimbra (UNESCO, 1290) with the legendary Biblioteca Joanina. The atmosphere of an ancient academy, fado in a male style. One day is enough.
9a6ffe837188b9ee579b27f9b740df92bcaa38ee: 🚆 From Coimbra-B (the main intercity station) to Coimbra (city centre) — urban 6 min, €1.60
0dc9f513086f7105376e16de5a92d6ba265ccec4: 🚗 By car: via A1 Lisboa-Porto — 2 hours from Lisbon, 1.5 hours from Porto
dfd070ec4c97d2aebefa4d7aae97bd96e7125072: The main attraction — the oldest university in the country (1290), one of the oldest in Europe. On a hill in the city centre. Combined entry €14 including the library.
4e87b252888cee3e910ddb49f665bbf977e33619: 📚 Biblioteca Joanina (1728) — a baroque library with 60,000 volumes from the 16th-18th centuries. Special bats guard the books from insects! Slots of 20 minutes
7d4443a7d618d751d62eb5f3eba7bf716fd1e477: 🎓 Capela de São Miguel — university chapel with a baroque organ

335a294c30b7b0d50b3d93de3c07e77b18a637f1: 🗼 Torre da Universidade — bell tower, entry €5

d46740a866c3db64b638e80532306bd4f8c0fe4b: 🏛️ Mosteiro de Santa Cruz — royal pantheon, tombs of the first kings Afonso I and Sancho I. €4

0da41a8fa0e72794fbf830562fd7467f1b3b5df7: 🎨 Museu Nacional Machado de Castro — former episcopal palace + Roman cryptportico. €6
3a91ceecdd4a032260c482a500e16f58cce205bf: Students of Coimbra wear black cloaks (capas e batinas) — a tradition since the 13th century. In spring (May) — Queima das Fitas, the graduation celebration: processions, concerts, songs.
0ccab1d5a54bea8cddbba924e72e5c15b735d6a5: 🎵 Fado de Coimbra — the male version of fado, performed only by men in cloaks. It differs from the Lisbon style. Concerts at Fado ao Centro or ao Capricho
935c78ea91ea3b8ed1efa251b8a91df0598dbc0a: 🎓 Praxe — student initiation rituals for freshmen (September-October)
428ac80e70ce869850c4207629efa5e387587127: 🎪 Queima das Fitas — 8 days in May, concerts at Parque Santa Cruz
007a33329b7fa3224f871d965fe5a06e640be5e6: 🎭 Bar Académico — bars exclusively for students, but easy to enter with a pass

6371db09f37b155cf534f11b0c61c7dcafedcc77: CP trains (Comboios de Portugal) — how to use them
929753ced45f8878ceccac05910821c5370bfaa5: CP — state railway operator. Types: Alfa Pendular (fast Lisboa-Porto 2.5h), Intercidades (medium), Regional (slow), Urbanos (suburban). Discount cards: Cartão Jovem, Cartão Amigo. Lisboa-Porto Alfa Pendular ~€25-40, Intercidades ~€20-30. Book at cp.pt.
faf3689691a658bcb05b8c357553a51e5151865a: CP flagship. Fast tilting trains. Lisboa-Porto in 2.5 hours. Wi-Fi, power sockets, café car. 2 classes: Conforto (economy), Primeira (first). Routes: Lisboa-Coimbra-Porto-Braga, Lisboa-Algarve.
337a7872c4a1fb409a5b4ddf1b1c00fa9ef92daa: Intercity, 30-50 minutes slower than AP, 20-30% cheaper. Same main routes + branches. Comfortable.
e5addde6e527ff39b9ce8df895ed1bafc664584c: Lisboa-Madrid night Sud Expresso (no longer operates since 2020 — replaced by bus). There is a Porto-Vigo regional service across the border.
15134d767ed203060e63ab224df7046ad594b71c: Slow, frequent stops. For small towns. Cheap.
86d347c813bb6f957a5945b5e9c75adbf7b8d351: Suburban trains of Lisbon (Cascais, Sintra, Tomar, Setúbal) and Porto (Braga, Aveiro, Guimarães). Payment via Navegante/Andante or regular ticket.
b14b2087dad58029a67e37facf44c4468242f76c: Lisboa ↔ Porto AP: 2 hours 45 minutes, €25-40 (discounts up to -65% for 5 days in advance)
d887dcdd815dc27746c7c17ae83ce0e302c7a75a: cp.pt or CP app. Registration, NIF (preferably), payment card.
f1ce32be3d2be3d88ad9984f294d3182b51b4e55: 5 days in advance: -65% on AP (Lisbon-Porto from €40 to €14)
9fb43eeb12f819ffd70dc13f043adfe00f5a0951: PDF or in the app. The controller scans the QR code. If Alfa Pendular — seat is reserved.
11bde529802086e20d944ed47b42748f86be5838: No reservation required. Can be purchased at the station, through the app or with a Navegante (Lisbon) / Andante (Porto) card.
0903fe9e44299264ec0f51cbf9c9e6edf4acc063: Santa Apolónia — traditional, for AP/IC to Porto, north
da7e3153b691493006e39e7ef212eff550d4f802: Oriente — modern, Parque das Nações, all AP/IC + Urbanos
a98345d39599a35e210e9fc45387275f8204f298: Gare do Oriente — synonymous with Oriente, metro red line
f062cb6884779ba808a6725010bc47e4ce2ed542: Cais do Sodré — Urbanos to Cascais
75cf298d8079ebca932a96bf48956081b20b69d7: Domestic flights and ferries — Madeira, Azores, mainland
ca8adf461704a0768bd1ceb50f0d45011da2aa94: For mainland-island travel, a flight is mandatory. TAP and Ryanair fly Lisboa/Porto → Madeira (Funchal) / Azores (Ponta Delgada). Azores — subsidised prices for residents. Ferries Soflusa/Transtejo across the Tejo (Lisboa), Douro cruises (Porto), Azores between islands.
6cf21334e50fe0e1cf3c0e153c91db1a7d2532ba: National carrier. Lisboa/Porto → Funchal 4-8 flights/day, 1.5h. Lisboa → Ponta Delgada daily, 2h 30min. Prices from €50 with early booking up to €300 in season.
fde4e5f263f01ee5a30b01f477229dfd4d17743e: Cheap tickets from €20 Lisboa-Funchal, Porto-Faro. But there are extra charges for baggage and seats. It's important to read the fine print.
97576d45ff2b10b04e9c16767adf42d80cf97660: Regional carrier of the Azores. Inter-island flights (10-30 min), €40-80. There are also flights from Lisboa to the Azores.
4ae45fa53dd2add07be9731bcd379b926f7d89cf: TAP flies 8-10 times a day, 50 min. However, train or bus is often cheaper considering the airport. Train is recommended.
311260562fdac75d78ea4608961c1e38b5acde35: Residents of the Azores and Madeira receive subsidised fares on domestic flights — up to 80% off. Proof of residency is required.
531ef537ee222b333f04657384a742cf4793054c: Buy through the 'tarifa residente' on SATA or TAP indicating the residence permit for Madeira/Azores.
83be8dafe3c9a2b64bdf5e929b491c47c7f2ee3e: Transtejo / Soflusa — ferries across the River Tejo. Main stops: Cacilhas (south bank, view of Lisbon), Barreiro, Seixal, Montijo, Trafaria. Payment via Navegante or ticket. From Cais do Sodré/Belém.
838cb995aacd502c7b54743f54ff99ac7c0a975f: Short cruises (6 bridges, 45 min, €18-25) and longer ones (to Régua, full day, €60-100). For Porto residents — rabelo catamarans from Cais da Ribeira.
1802c29ff540c3f88c1a81c716702b3b14579627: Atlânticoline — ferries between the islands. In season (April-October), all lines operate. In winter — limited service. Between neighbouring islands, 1-2 hours, €15-30.
12cc7de71dbee5ecde2544197804d5a5eed15594: Porto Santo Line. Daily ferry Funchal → Porto Santo (a small island with beaches), 2.5 hours, €30-60.
627ff306b8ea8b0ae1d752ea67d9cecf8e5e1a65: Madeira and the Azores have different climates. Madeira is consistently +20-25°C, the Azores have changeable weather.
9229dc45a6538f7632a53a7123eb3aec2af7e109: For Madeira / Azores — book early, peak season July-August x2.
92fed891941e442d1f999862e2852deb62fbb6b2: Ferries Lisboa — use the Navegante card, it's cheaper.
23a989338fcb821681198976557b5ddefa34e07d: Airport → city: Lisboa Alameda metro €1.70 20 minutes; Porto Aeroporto metro (purple line) €2.25 30 minutes.
d8ce2eddb51fedd26189917fef41950bc6265a04: Ryanair — always with hand luggage, extra charge for checked baggage.
12830138334cdb2b2fb8048e0031c5cf8d6eedf5: TAP Miles & Go — earn miles (can be done via Millennium BCP card).
087224ef93c953d09e98b7cee6fb7927c97adbab: Douro Valley — a UNESCO wine region
a554817231e6601f89ea1130d66ea90150bc9da6: Douro Valley (Alto Douro Vinhateiro, UNESCO since 2001) — the oldest demarcated wine region in the world (1756). Terraced vineyards along the river, port wine, picturesque villages. From Porto: train to Régua/Pinhão (2-2.5 hours), car, river cruises, helicopter. Ideal for 2-3 days.
037ab9fbff14a5856956a04e7000136320c26720: Douro is a river that flows into the Atlantic in Porto. Upstream is the Alto Douro region (250 km from Spain), where grapes for port wine and table wines are grown. The first protected regional designation in the world (1756, Marquis of Pombal).
ef3d8945dee795ee72658ff1f8921c21ba1ca079: Terraced vineyards (socalcos) — a man-made landscape listed by UNESCO. Slopes up to 60°. Grape varieties: Touriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz.
32001211cda12861cf154f2a4c89613145e0e90d: Porto-São Bento → Peso da Régua (2 hours, €11) → Pinhão (40 minutes, €4). The train runs along the riverbank, one of the most scenic in Europe. Sit on the right side (if coming from Porto).
d5de5b16a49ae11ad31f4018a439dc2506176b17: From Porto 1.5-2 hours to Régua. Further to Pinhão, Favaios, São João da Pesqueira — all along picturesque roads. It is recommended to rent for 2 days.
f1d8b191bbf8c1a7e37c0df9a20988db3feee85b: From Porto (Cais da Estiva / Gaia) — day trips (to Régua, €60-80) or multi-day (3-8 days along the river) with CroisiEurope, Douro Azul, etc. Includes lunch, sometimes dinner + tasting.
00d3da77a2b50ce6a5d609752b1d934b97fa6f85: Helitours from Viseu/Régua — 20-minute flight from €150. Car tours from Porto — €90-150 per person with a guide and visit to 2-3 quintas.
6dab365e55bb6171cfb5e4177d03e03eccaa0f78: The main 'entrance' to Douro. Museu do Douro (history of the region, €7.50). Departure point for cruises. Many quintas nearby.
3fa9f36639319dcc40e130546937207d95c46314: The heart of the region. Train station with azulejos — legendary. Panoramic views from Casal de Loivos (10 min by car). Numerous quintas within a 10 km radius.
939d85d1265eb62d3c8ac380514091a892fdb897: An architectural gem — a well-preserved 18th-century village with barquinho (traditional bread), fountains, and heraldic facades.
7d9bf91bd99e6a788669c57b450bbe58cfc060be: The upper part, less touristy. Old quintas, churches, views.
08e5f794b305771f9b2314943e3d0d73e1579e01: Moscatel do Douro (sweet wine), famous since the 17th century. Village hugged into vineyards. Visit Adega Cooperativa de Favaios.
c5a6bd2f958ac8c7e04b602a3f213a199fbcc413: The dam with a navigation lock at 35 m is the highest in Europe. Cruise ships pass through.
ebd2559d0c8b0e66139d87928f4080d060394a03: Most large quintas are open to tourists with tours and tastings (€15-40 per person). Book 1-2 weeks in advance during the season. Many have restaurants and guest houses.
