fe8968b25e65225258578a5e3f4f901dd62a1da0: Évora and Alentejo — white towns and wineries
42883bc75b6c172861b46d429fbfea5975e8f098: Évora (UNESCO) — the main city of Alentejo, 1.5 hours from Lisbon. Chapel of Bones (Capela dos Ossos), Roman temple of Diana, cathedral. Surrounding are the plains of Alentejo with whitewashed villages, olive groves, vineyards, rare authenticity. 1 day — Évora, 2-3 days — the region.
c0804cbea3036232e391164b011116fe37a26c63: 🚗 A6 motorway — 1.5h. A car is almost essential for the Alentejo region
4b9f31c273c2c720d347b755344c08e9a324f0c3: 🚐 Day tour from Lisbon €65-90 — not recommended, too rushed
5a520dd6f66a46b4ee1e08a0404c93e3223dd193: 🏛️ Templo Romano (Templo de Diana) — Roman temple from the 1st century, 14 Corinthian columns. Free entry
b902f285d64d45a291c244fb6d65c94de5ab43e6: 💀 Capela dos Ossos — chapel adorned with the bones of 5,000 monks, 16th century. Inscription above the entrance: 'Nós ossos que aqui estamos, pelos vossos esperamos'. €6
993b8e566d8d39c97b52cedcf6e78ed921572987: ⛪ Sé de Évora — Gothic cathedral from the 13th century, treasury with the Holy Shroud. €5
eb9650ad7b73623a85bc2e72609f1986c8b3869d: 🏛️ Praça do Giraldo — main square with a marble fountain
81493ffd312502663da2501568c2ca3f61538725: 🕌 Igreja de São Francisco — above Capela dos Ossos
50ed15b1c142deda21f86a5d78ab0cf465cbe422: 🏛️ Museu de Évora — in the bishop's palace, sacred art
be35e3d395163bc67db49d4fdc691cd474304ae0: 🌊 Aqueduto da Água de Prata — aqueduct from the 16th century
5a41c8a28ef480dd04d2f15148c1f6324883b2d4: 🎓 Universidade de Évora — Jesuit university from the 16th century, the oldest after Coimbra
1feadfb61c569c333aa72b96b03b23ec3b690b99: Alentejo is one of the largest wine regions in PT. Character: full-bodied reds (Aragonês, Trincadeira, Alicante Bouschet), aromatic whites (Antão Vaz). Tours range from €20 to €60.
6d2dc0630a6f5f89ee8dc3cebabfd163f2169583: 🍷 Herdade do Esporão (Reguengos de Monsaraz) — large, with a restaurant on the hill. Tours + lunches
ae6abc5248fd609077a158ebbc0acd9a68dc470f: 🍷 Adega Mayor (Campo Maior) — architecture by Álvaro Siza Vieira
0836a006336995314510d94f3d9eaee73d8b4106: 🍷 Herdade do Rocim — experiments with amphora
89c9faf5daaa1f09845582f70304a2e76a9852ed: Medieval 'deserto' on the hill, view of the Alqueva lake (artificial, largest in Europe). Castle, narrow whitewashed streets. 1-2 hours.
452b1def104ca4651e2022b3196a88a71bcdbe86: Mountain town at the Spanish border, castle, views of Serra de São Mamede. One of the most atmospheric places in PT.
420e9c1f7e9d5298d04cdc0ac59797f11390cb79: UNESCO — the largest preserved bastion fortification complex in the world. Amoreira aqueduct, fortress.
b6a84d5dca97245f61490da6cb662118c05899a7: Marble everywhere, palace of the Dukes of Braganza (former royal residence).
205507f79376ea39e1f06f0ffc04f79e65cb5529: Saturday market with antiques, castle with pousada hotel.
58e5cb6727d7dba8e8a5613ebddbfb03eaadad74: Former Arab capital, former mosque-church, Moorish architecture.
eaf07459a27e293548322c1b531fd3ceb4b6c686: 🥣 Açorda alentejana — bread soup with garlic and coriander
a3ceececa3e4334f0546500f2e53dd605d205a7f: 🍄 Carne de porco à alentejana — pork with clams
05572f7049f8584aeed7df70a98a69bbf3262c09: 🍮 Sericaia com ameixa de Elvas — sweet pudding with dried plum
c4c04484ce4001161e749402e08cd31efd693896: 🍷 Vinho da talha — wine aged in amphorae, a millennia-old tradition
578052dca393a2a4d56f76cd0539413d716ee7eb: 🗓️ Best seasons: April-May (flowering), September-October (harvest, mild climate)
86d325b99cb03947201e7dd03c5be5a2b8f9c5a8: 🔥 In summer +35-40°C — hot. Plan visits for the morning and evening.

d3bfb6ac3fa5ef297f8bb18d66d767fa36324510: Buses between cities in Portugal — Rede Expressos, FlixBus, Alsa
f063b43a272040897968ac5534a4bb14929e4aea: Main operators: Rede Expressos (main network PT, from Sete Rios), FlixBus (international + part of PT), Alsa (focus on Spain), EVA Transportes (south, Algarve). Lisboa-Porto ≈ 3.5 hours €15-25, Lisboa-Faro ≈ 3 hours €20-30. Book online, cheaper in advance.
6c366580913e7a4a93651e22c187c9d41905dc26: The main network in Portugal. Covers all major and medium cities. From Lisbon — Sete Rios station (metro Jardim Zoológico). Booking at rede-expressos.pt or the app. Prices €10-30 depending on the time.
86cefafbecd8f11fd7d36c2e28ff1d12c3d40633: German operator. Strong coverage Lisbon-Porto, Lx-Faro, + international (Spain, France, Germany). Best prices with early booking (from €5). Wi-Fi + sockets.
9665acbaaead7aa2973703d49be7daffe57d3833: Spanish operator. Good for routes to Spain (Madrid, Barcelona, Seville). Within Portugal — Lisboa-Porto, Lisboa-Faro. Often cheaper than FlixBus.
02daa97f780322031c84a8d3a4212f53f0ffb8ab: Regional operator in the Algarve. Good for moving around the south (Faro-Tavira-Lagos).
f1d4f11851a0c8d2f92b0f836f85809128eedda0: For trips to small towns and villages. Timetables are published at the bus station (rodoviária) of the respective city, as well as on the IMT website — the transport regulator.
6b9e8baab8892afeb2c7428251dcc3caa8ba6456: Lisboa ↔ Porto: 3.5h, €15-25 (FlixBus from €5 a week in advance)
8030cb979328180db1b675b0bd1de0f96e6b145a: Compare prices on operators' websites directly — this always gives an up-to-date picture. Ticket prices change dynamically depending on the load of the service and the day of the week.
6dab371b285e3d398744aa59e0a389bd2fd1ca4a: Booking directly on the operator's website is usually the most reliable. The best prices are 2-4 weeks before the trip; on the day of departure, rates are higher by 30-70%.
9941cfd6367e9bf3c43505ce8cf751b9e32971cd: QR code in email or app. No need to print. Check the bus number/platform on the bus station display.
f4bbc6a7a6fbebf30035cdd32e6313a97d7b201c: The earlier you book, the cheaper it is. 2-4 weeks in advance — from €5 for FlixBus
d1bc8cfa2d4b60f062603ae60a008ac03a85ac13: FlixBus/Alsa is more punctual than Rede Expressos, but all are fine.
2ea0beb78ddea0ad4e97672241350784b8b47019: Luggage: 1 hand luggage + 1 suitcase usually included. Extra charge for a 2nd suitcase
d740130a22a1b8acdf2e49380acac60388500934: Wi-Fi on most modern buses, but unstable
3899514070b3c8af580ab6bfb756c18001a39ccc: Late evening/night buses — save on accommodation
fdbeae8f01e4453adc17c534d36f55aa4665226b: Sete Rios in Lisbon — MAIN station. Oriente — also serves some routes
396d9ff0428808140eab078670c68ec774f31df6: In Porto — Campanhã and Rodoviária Campanhã
26b8dcacef278066072149937249fa59d9781fcb: Intercity transport in Portugal — train, bus, plane
1860171c7e69e729f2426b3f11f9e3c2bf2d209f: Three channels: CP trains (comfort + Lisbon–Porto–Braga network + Alentejo), Rede Expressos buses (cheap national network) and FlixBus (international). Islands — TAP, SATA, Ryanair flights and Atlanticoline ferries. Algarve — EVA buses. The aggregator Omio brings everything together. Book 2–4 weeks in advance — prices drop by 30–50%.
be628f651db202c611be8bb06c467aa2c95b3fa1: National railway. Covers Lisboa — Porto — Braga, Lisbon — Alentejo — Algarve, Coimbra, mountainous routes Douro. Does not cover the islands.
5d44d96cd845458fcbefced1fba60c8af36b6c40: Alfa Pendular — high-speed (220 km/h), Lisboa–Porto 2:40, comfort ‘Conforto’ / ‘Turístico’
6454ae76e3c9cee2758d0d5cfff7235d45e4084d: Discounts: 50% for youth aged 4-13, 25-50% for students, 50% for seniors 65+
e5334c8cf49f26451004f436e20e569167a6c857: Interrail Pass: available to residents of PT
39074130add7658dbbf339495e5060c8a3b94531: Website cp.pt — mobile version in EN
2932fb134990240202350ad95c1b8b1a9767460a: International network. Within PT: Lisbon–Porto (€10–25), Lisbon–Faro (€15–35). Abroad: PT–Spain (Madrid €35), PT–France (Paris €100). Plus: cheap prices, downside: fewer routes than Rede Expressos.
e532f9efe10ded5a49e6e48258e9cb992b07e087: Spanish operator, PT–Spain routes. Lisbon–Madrid, Porto–Santiago. From €25.
357c0809e2fc1055b041f61102117237fa3b4264: Algarve. Faro–Lagos, Albufeira and rural routes. €5–15.
4b94c91e7435627c4342769ccc7736ab357f41ac: Rodonorte (north), Rodoviária do Alentejo, Rodoviária do Tejo, and others. For remote villages and towns — often the only transport available.
6c1e292b87e1bdae2ddde361e4fabc959c4bde1b: Domestic flights — only to the islands
7d69d5abd8381643040c7a76766d226966b1f42c: ✈️ Between Lisbon/Porto and mainland cities, FLIGHTS are only rare (Faro 1x a day)
08957c325e97b80163d3856373b296de4a1e3b1e: ✈️ Main connections — to Madeira (Funchal) and the Azores (Ponta Delgada)
99d1159b443bc24c7347983152788b52c57eaa89: 🇵🇹 TAP Portugal — the main carrier, Lisboa hub
8929fb64c175d1d2d46adf2d2b36ad9d3743374a: 🇵🇹 SATA Azores Airlines — specialist in the Azores, inter-island flights
667299420ab0d577090bb29058025b500bf29c7e: 💶 Ryanair — Porto/Lisboa to Madeira ~€40–80 in advance
ce45e70a02bcd75453c42a2ecefa17c24a17539d: ⛴️ Atlanticoline — Azores inter-island. São Miguel ↔ Terceira ↔ Faial and others. From €15
74b54c04031b5a2c503ee2f71ac2aa4e434692e9: ⛴️ Porto Santo Line — Madeira ↔ Porto Santo, 2:30 hours, €50–80
9f7701e85b3b850068948c1fecc204d6e170cf58: 🔍 Omio (omio.com) — search for trains + buses + flights
8ac59365a567f21e66cbbe842fd233f6da493d90: 📱 CP + Rede Expressos + FlixBus app — direct booking is cheaper
57faffb5783eed44a95df17e49da5bdf3fc6a7df: 💶 Combo ticket: often cheaper to buy CP + local bus separately
2f79165b1099d08c59706f58a02794365d53eeb1: Centre (Baixa/Chiado) — expensive, touristy. Alfama/Mouraria — historic, trendy but noisy. Avenidas Novas / Saldanha / Areeiro — modern, metro, family-friendly. Parque das Nações — new, family-oriented. Campo de Ourique / Estrela — intimate, green. Almada / Cacilhas — the other bank, cheaper. Cascais / Oeiras — suburbs by the ocean.
a61722d3f34360e7648e4bffc77ebd6ed9b68cdc: Shopping centre, cafes, museums. Very touristy. Rent for a T1 from €1,500-2,500/month. Pros: everything nearby. Cons: crowds, noise at night.
8c0824408542a691070447ac91ad231738c7d08f: Oldest neighbourhood, fado, cool streets. Characterful, but limited parking, poor accessibility for families with strollers. T1 from €1,200-2,000/month.
d3c62d8dbfd666a41eb1e8fafd72ee31c7ed9f63: Nightlife (Bairro Alto) and boutiques (Príncipe Real). Noisy at night, not a good choice for sleep if you're not a party-goer. T1 €1,500-2,500.
3add06332458224bba3d83bf24d2cb49f3b54e89: Business centre. Metro on blue + yellow lines. Many foreigners, restaurants, Corte Inglés. T1 €1,400-2,200, T2 €1,800-3,000.
9a0379cc9c1e453094c4ec7d0e2278ffd43aabf2: Quiet, with schools. Green line metro. Choice of Russian-speaking clubs. T1 €1,200-€1,800.
fd7fd7259753696b9918615804873478c3a77da9: Built for Expo 98. Modern, spacious, many parks, oceanarium. Family-friendly. T1 €1,400-€2,200, T2 €2,000-€3,500.
2a982deb12ad49cad148191608ddc07261c27d1e: Charming, green, market. A favourite area for expats. T1 €1,300-€2,000.
17f718e1a22b16baf94d0c5719f95ac1421846a8: Diplomatic, with Estrela garden, prestigious. T1 €1,500-€2,500, villas €3,000+.
3bfb2a6826691d08246b6ce4f10588570a741ba3: City centre of Lisboa, but cheaper. Developing, trendy for young people. T1 from €800-€1,500.
94f225e46fb82817ea55ec88b1296d49b1c56e55: Multicultural, diverse cuisines. Green line metro. T1 €1,000-€1,600.
5dad1513ce59a9f561101366f96513f0a6c8d88d: Northern part, quiet. Schools, parks. T1 €900-€1,400, T2 €1,300-€2,000.
130f57aba7218d23a9daa52a7760eb70c48e019d: On the southern side of the Tejo. 10 minutes by ferry to the centre of Lisboa. -30-40% off city prices. T1 €600-€1,000.
18414f8ecf02c416e0fd411748f59d155653e321: Even further south. Many commuting workers from Lisbon. T1 €500-€900.
d72e6a3736b32e84eb50d81063f4c6150bc424b7: Resort town by the ocean. Prestigious, many expats. Train (Urbanos) to Lisboa 40 minutes. T1 €1,500-€3,000, villas €5,000+.
f1741d905690fbd59f034d6893e539643d6e9b0f: Between Lisboa and Cascais. Tech hub (Lagoas Park). International schools. T1 €1,200-€2,200.
9bd47d5c915119f76cea744fb2ca6838d2be4a95: Between Cascais and Oeiras. Casino, beaches. Expensive. T1 €1,500-€2,800.
f1a27d2049083e1c3c87a293acd363e04f333c3a: Work in the centre + no car → Avenidas Novas, Areeiro, Saldanha
047c118997c87dafee0e1ab41fe1b0bbb84fd4d4: Family with children + schools → Parque das Nações, Oeiras, Cascais, Areeiro
a26fcc998200a1ed6018ccd1b0958a1de14520f2: Nightlife → Bairro Alto, Cais do Sodré (but not to live)
27432e0078597735ff5e3080ef54c1a50ccfdbe9: The atmosphere of the old town → Alfama, Mouraria, Graça
2836109756c408f27917e331046c983f5cabbe36: The ocean is close → Cascais, Estoril, Oeiras, Caxias
ed0512de8ef02eb1e441cff50311d22dc61b18db: Young expats → Penha de França, Campo de Ourique, Arroios
19df2ee5d0e6eda08a4eab845fc0de3a667c04fb: Câmara Municipal (municipality) of Lisbon — guide to districts and freguesias
64cab2fab8168ee941149c647a645e02a2a60d87: Lisbon — a hilly city on the Tagus River, UNESCO in Belém, tram 28 as a symbol, fado in Alfama, viewpoints with views, Jerónimos Monastery and Belém Tower — must. Minimum 3 days, ideally 5-7. Lisboa Card offers free transport + discounts on museums.
a7273b7daf1806116a85a14fb308736450d927e8: Western part of Lisbon, 6 km from the centre. Here is the era of the Great Geographical Discoveries: Vasco da Gama set sail from here. Entrance to most attractions is €10-14, Lisboa Card offers free or discounted entry.
f66998471e2c3799c63fc474a0a3f095823f4188: 🏛️ Mosteiro dos Jerónimos (16th century, UNESCO) — Manueline-Gothic monastery, tomb of Vasco da Gama and Camões. €12, Tue-Sun 10-18, Mon closed
9879ca20dc3e500d6e7d4766fcda5ff9bc13da82: 🗼 Torre de Belém (16th century, UNESCO) — a watchtower on the river, a symbol of Lisbon. €8
85ca362b8c4a81cce350c58cbdb3a02ea111295b: 🚀 Padrão dos Descobrimentos — monument to the discoveries from 1960, viewing platform on the roof €5
a2799061b43eeb34ebc65f75a2458fe258a36285: 🥐 Pastéis de Belém — the original pastry shop for pastéis de nata since 1837. There’s a queue, but it’s worth it
653812a34de77ff8ecc38941a3b328588370e2ad: 🏛️ Museu Nacional dos Coches — royal carriages from the 16th to 19th centuries, €8
0a30f0503420a20a080dd75b084a6ae2c80128a2: 🎨 Museu Coleção Berardo / MAAT — contemporary art, often free
f182ab1c69bec7e4cece5e808a161f8d63e80ff4: How to get there: tram 15E from Praça do Comércio, train from Cais do Sodré to Belém, Uber/Bolt €8-12.
011bf11ea5693efa1a75bb1b7027cd6272bce701: 🟨 Praça do Comércio — the main square by the Tagus, triumphal arch, equestrian statue of José I
2aec711c397fdfb7c4fb865921add3ccd24deab6: 🟥 Praça do Rossio — lively central square, D. Maria II theatre, wave-patterned cobblestones
43030b038a283c639f4dfa116f6305d925280558: ⛪ Elevador de Santa Justa — cast-iron lift from 1902 in the style of Eiffel, viewing €5.30
b1a0d4616be1793de0345f15ccc2a460a2dc581a: ☕ Café A Brasileira (Chiado) — statue of Fernando Pessoa, a coffee legend
ec2258b6ab06d5c7da3d04792e136326e53adc15: 📚 Livraria Bertrand — the oldest bookshop in the world (1732), Guinness
59e946fc8fbf3447319324afab17c04dc03f591e: 🛍️ Rua Augusta — pedestrian shopping street with a triumphal arch
c02b67845497458a0796f3ef9f8ed951d17e4130: 🏛️ Museu do Dinheiro — free money museum in a historic bank building
fdfef86a73cd9e6f139f17762188cc455157310e: 🚞 Ascensor da Glória — funicular to Bairro Alto
538178d21952e6cf5148396a88bd69b38d57f8d4: The district survived the 1755 earthquake — narrow streets, azulejos, fado from every window. Hilly, wear comfortable shoes.
2f496c32e7ecb427855ef1a6363dbc7a29039c2d: 🏰 Castelo de São Jorge — a Moorish castle on the hilltop, the best views. €15
288e105fd980e9a9c55409f09432dbe6def415c9: ⛪ Sé de Lisboa — 12th-century cathedral, free entry to the main part
1f1eb2dc3b67a499417e4bf0b60716fa2ec777dd: 🎵 Fado at Clube de Fado / Mesa de Frades / Tasca do Chico — evening concerts €30-60 with dinner
1be4d9dabd4a2c0daaf9e0a52ff90c52694308d3: 🚋 Tram 28E — historic route through Alfama, Graça, Estrela. Be prepared for queues and pickpockets
7bb3243eb5842a20eedc9c0cce2617ad47cfd920: 🌅 Miradouro de Santa Luzia — classic view with azulejos
d72587b0f57758683a420c9d1fea15c56316ee81: 🌅 Miradouro da Senhora do Monte — the highest point, panorama of the entire city
ac9233536d25dfb94777fccbda2bcc6b381dabf1: 🏺 Museu do Fado — the history of the genre, recordings of great performers. €5
951d85993cb37a624e99c21be28f637fc4a4953a: 🎎 Museu Nacional do Azulejo — former monastery with a collection of tiles. €8
1818bae01193a5b5a74165e077a83afabc64d658: 🌃 Bairro Alto — nightlife. Quiet during the day, after 10 PM — bars, live music
f9a483243c3c4d2b0c679effaebf1b8eab7b1723: 🌳 Jardim do Príncipe Real — park with a huge 150-year-old pine, Sunday flea market
035d4fbc0295904a4a126a9e4d4d26448b08772e: 🏛️ Basílica da Estrela — baroque basilica from the 18th century, free entry
3a47dba843ba7c4ca7a7a31d9381fff5859181a4: 🌲 Jardim da Estrela — Lisboners' favourite garden for picnics
e7a314b6669791e5f183e300d070078a381a0c7d: 📚 Museu Nacional de Arte Antiga (MNAA) — the main art museum, €10
5df9430695fae05e3ca0d52698054e0d13ca97a9: 🌅 Miradouro de São Pedro de Alcântara — view of the castle and city centre, Glória funicular
41f30772bbcaa64483f7416f4c5609a14cbb7779: 🐟 Oceanário de Lisboa — one of the best aquariums in Europe. €25
761efb80e2bbeb0b873925aa5be94257e126ac31: 🎢 Pavilhão do Conhecimento — an interactive science museum for children. €10
d49a32bb41ca876370436102fdc6f5f99dcdc74c: 🚡 Teleférico — cable car along the river, €4/adult
33fb8586301493d8d7b64787c13d0e47a06d0d08: 🏗️ Gare do Oriente — a masterpiece train station by Santiago Calatrava
da3bfd40be7920a0c7a466427c5b14680cbc67c2: 🛍️ Centro Vasco da Gama — shopping centre
d4c8a5081511c2cac5fc6e52f4c2e729c79a6c10: 🌉 Ponte Vasco da Gama — 17 km bridge over the Tagus (the second longest in Europe)
a31e5f9bc6587bb7c0a26158266767cdf16eb560: Lisboa Card (€22/€36/€43 for 24/48/72 hours) — free transport + 30+ attractions
17804d28cbcdd10ff8fbeb262de35d4ddf78b725: Viva Viagem — card for single metro/bus trips, better than a ticket
3c7baf73310464fee097ac96f37f5a9877228da9: Tickets for Jerónimos and Torre de Belém — combo €18 instead of €20, but book online in advance (time slots)
fd60eb1a5cfca84f3cfe99f970c0755c6e1303c6: Tram 28: early morning (before 9:00) or evening — fewer crowds and pickpockets
fed0ffb1663da145ff29364fcda822636c9c86a9: Water is not free in restaurants, ask for 'água da torneira' (tap water, not always provided)
0d15ce3ccc4c65b415d0a363a9ef9881e1ec3faa: Swimming in the Tejo is not allowed — it is polluted. Beaches in Cascais / Costa da Caparica
71db14ff57fdb55339679c018b874956e07c53f9: Evening walk: Castelo → Alfama → Tejo waterfront
c04fe966782dce0301618a227a520cc9614b3378: Walking route through Alfama — the oldest district of Lisbon
80e597ed256c088ce93dba2be684090fddc6c22e: A 3 km route taking 2.5–3.5 hours through the heart of medieval Lisbon. Start — Sé de Lisboa, finish — Miradouro da Graça. A leisurely climb through narrow cobbled streets, a castle, 3 viewpoints, a fado museum. Convenient to combine with dinner at one of the tascas with live fado.
309e2ae57cb2e7a40f80771bd27d9b1f09db8002: The 12th-century cathedral, the oldest church in the city. It has survived three earthquakes. Inside is a cloister with archaeological excavations from the Roman and Visigothic periods.
a1d6770790336a58739328aec0e4cd9a376dc574: Built on the site of the birthplace of Saint Anthony of Padua in 1195. There is a crypt in the basement. On 13 June, the city celebrates with a wedding marathon right outside the church.
e6f25a2164004b8337e7836f3daaa0440cd1d623: A viewpoint with an azulejo panel depicting the 'Capture of Lisbon from the Moors' (1147). From here, there is the best view of the tiled roofs of Alfama and the Tagus River. A breeze, benches, and a floral pergola.
ccbfa59f92adab106f26e5ec39b120937e22e1de: 50 metres higher is the 'Gates of the Sun'. A statue of Saint Vincent (the city's patron) with a raven. From here, you can see the dome of São Vicente de Fora and Campo de Santa Clara with the Feira da Ladra market on Tuesdays and Saturdays.
74f81a58a3d5a5d1a8d71434eb102dfd7fb843e5: A Moorish castle atop a hill. The walls date back to the 11th century, with a restored citadel and 11 towers. Peacocks roam the grounds. A 360° panorama of all of Lisbon.
5ec420abe4d9f493105841e7b15286bbff63de13: 6. Igreja de São Vicente de Fora
0afbbe1d188348bc73d6a3d6be306881e509619b: A 16th-century monastery, featuring azulejos depicting fables by La Fontaine. In the pantheon are the tombs of the kings of the Braganza dynasty (1640–1910). You can climb to the roof for a different view of Alfama.
1b9b1b984771367fdc6d625afc0675238875f97e: The museum of Portuguese fado — a form of urban romance included in UNESCO's list of intangible heritage. Biographies of singers, old records, and performances.
90fff4bbf29620c9e1dd26ab16718238716fd2e0: A final viewing point under the pines with a view of the castle and the river. Nearby is the church of Graça and one of the best tascas in the area, "Damas". In the evening, enjoy the sunset over the Castelo.
f7fe6e02857dc5c21f659db5294d75b6d086b697: 👟 Comfortable shoes are essential — the cobbled **calçada portuguesa** is slippery in the rain
4f82988d046292620d7ccd7936f5c8d9bbfc4b08: 🕙 The best times are 09:30–12:00 or after 16:30 to avoid the midday heat
dbaafd80a9222ffdf0fb524145b98a5e9547be45: 💧 Bring water — cafes are available, but they are rare and expensive
19d5d2746ef1ce99641318dccac62e9403d4a874: 🎒 Watch your pockets in the crowd of tourists at Sé and Castelo
89f011d1e7bdea6b122f411e7d6b36d2172b381a: 🚇 The starting point is 3 minutes from the metro station Baixa-Chiado (exit "Rua Augusta")

5e40d3ede6221d641bea7d4c87ef211e790aeff3: 🎵 In the evening in Alfama — dozens of tascas with live fado (dinner + wine ~€35–50/person)
2020df784269cda0841d831c8eb5a55a658f5f17: Alfama is one of the few neighbourhoods that survived the 1755 earthquake. The name comes from the Arabic "al-hamma" (hot spring). Before the Reconquista, it was a Moorish quarter, later a fishing district. Today, it is a UNESCO-protected historical area with unique architecture, where narrow **becos**, houses with balconies in various colours, and lines of **azulejo** coexist.
54b362215bb2620ebafa0a22a5b124c360b5201e: The 1755 earthquake — the context of the neighbourhood
b2a493b787768f20ab1997824de4175451b79fc1: Visit Lisboa — What to visit in Alfama
eb853e0d9ae2a89c30191d43921c59dfc4caeb1a: Belém — route of the Age of Discoveries
8eeda1bd2fe38bd761decbda6ab85e73659122e9: A 4 km route taking 4 hours with museums and without — from Mosteiro dos Jerónimos through Padrão dos Descobrimentos to Torre de Belém. The Belém area holds monuments from the 'golden age' of navigation: Vasco da Gama, Henry the Navigator, Magellan. Along the way — the legendary pastry shop Pastéis de Belém (since 1837).
2bd3229039d20a79358ce0820fa6be6babe98af2: The Manueline monastery from the 16th century, UNESCO since 1983. Funded by a tax on spices from India. Here lie the graves of Vasco da Gama and Luís de Camões. The cloister is a gem of the style.
cff94d123c98077f17daa4c52ce769f7aa100850: The pastry predecessors of pastéis de nata made from the original recipe of the Jerónimos monks since 1837. The takeaway queue is quick, it's better to have coffee in the room with the azulejo panel.
fe80187287a544779b47f5019eb120d68e4fa9db: 3. MAAT (Museu de Arte, Arquitetura e Tecnologia)
fe6132d59fa99dc9f212e437101ea0b08ef54fd6: A contemporary museum under a wavy surfboard roof designed by Amanda Levete. Inside — exhibitions of design and video art, above — a panorama of the Tagus River.
97ad72a9de032e701e48ee94411263b90b026ac4: A 52 m monument in the shape of a caravel with 33 figures of pioneers: Henry the Navigator, Magellan, Vasco da Gama, and others. In the square — a marble compass rose (a gift from South Africa, 1960).
84c6440f30bbfe9a23a4eccac67d61f72a6d97ba: A garden with a fountain between Jerónimos and Tejo. 32 coats of arms of old provinces around the fountain. A great place for a picnic in front of Torre de Belém.
2eff666d28bf5b2ce5a1efaea12267524c684e63: A 16th-century fortress and lighthouse, UNESCO World Heritage site. Historically welcomed ships returning from India. A symbol of Lisbon. Inside — a stone rhinoceros (the first European sculpture of this animal).
e219eb704ea62ce7d68760b1760f3e329877ebee: Optional. The largest collection of royal carriages in the world. Luxurious Baroque pieces from the 17th to 19th centuries with gilding and painting.

58997d51190f063ab01cd3d38fa6eb5a06035918: In the 15th–16th centuries, Portugal was a superpower. Vasco da Gama discovered the sea route to India (1498), Pedro Cabral discovered Brazil (1500), and Magellan circumnavigated the globe (1519–1522). The wealth from spices financed this region.

c037273cd46f304d4174a272a9287b4dcbe27fb5: Autonomous region, eternal spring +18-24°C all year round. Funchal is the capital. Digital Nomads Village in Ponta do Sol. English is widely spoken. SNS is operational. ZFM (Zona Franca da Madeira) — special regime for **corporations** (IRC 5% instead of 21%, not IRS for individuals!). For individual residents, Madeira's IRS is usually 1–2 percentage points lower than the continental rate. Rent is cheaper than in Lisboa, but has increased in 2024-2025 due to digital nomads.

74239627af62aad54c99daf257574980acc4480a: Porto Card (€13/€17/€22/€27 for 1/2/3/4 days) — transport + 17 museums + discounts
e8954120d45e04adb58de596f01c8b434e628f44: Andante — travel card for metro/buses/trains in the Porto area, €1.95-€2.50 per trip
d74fa7a8d620dc48df896ff0a79ed615c61de9f7: Livraria Lello — book tickets online, the queue can be an hour
d8a959943631abbec25b67e18e0b86f7a474dd44: Port wine cellars — book tours online, especially in summer
1efc90318ebd96fd772e6ec58f8d33da38b758c8: Tram 1 — does not always run, check on stcp.pt
e34cedf5de50e3bf438ee1c1ba7c5ee1125b2e86: Porto is rainier than Lisbon — take an umbrella
1e18a34035ce61ef5ceb3182cead794039ee3996: Evening promenade: Ribeira → Ponte Luís → Gaia → sunset
9a34477ea8b2190e2608a14971ec4a7926507cc9: Walking route through Porto's historic centre
0346de37bb03504bc0d52424d489e1b8f1a81d2a: Route 2.5 km in 3 hours from Clérigos tower through Lello bookshop, Sé cathedral and São Bento station to Ribeira waterfront and Luís I bridge. The historic centre is included in UNESCO. Many descents, stone stairs — footwear matters. At the end — crossing to Vila Nova de Gaia for the wine cellars.
114354c3148ea6a0bc600e9769ce6883a42319cd: A 76 m bell tower from the 18th century in Baroque style. 225 steps to the viewing platform — the best panorama of Porto. Nearby is the church of the same name with an oval nave (a rarity).
b6b59a0e9a824ae04d79671961fd046dc5fdf633: A bookshop from 1906 in neo-Gothic/art nouveau style. The legendary wooden spiral staircase (likely the prototype for Hogwarts, although Rowling denies this). Entry €8 (deducted from book purchase).
afab5010aeab18e84c2a3cb60654129ac5cb274d: Two churches merged into one façade, separated by the narrowest house in Porto (1 m wide). The side wall is completely covered with azulejo panels (1912).
1510b864516f8c0e376d7ffe3516c38e8f85f710: A train station from 1916. The vestibule is lined with 20,000 azulejo tiles: scenes from the history of Portugal, the Battle of Arcos de Valdevez, the entry of King João I into Porto.
2829b4436e157362234f62928069a62ccdf783e0: A 12th-century cathedral of fortress type. Cloister with 18th-century azulejo, silver altar in the Sacrament chapel. Nearby is a monument to Vimara Peres, the first count of Portucale.
21dd729678386a1bf03b74fbca99fe5f58b0aac9: 6. Rua das Flores / Igreja da Misericórdia
f75d44c6f750b2ec996aa8a1ed8acddc0317dc0d: A pedestrian street with cafes, boutiques, and a flow of tourists. The Misericórdia church — from the 16th century, nearby is a museum with the masterpiece 'Fons Vitae' (by Flemish masters).
7416800b8a14c59e817e7f1cc9e937ff8c68849f: A neoclassical stock exchange palace from 1850. Sala do Árabe — an imitation of the Alhambra, 18 years of painting. Tours available in PT/EN/ES/FR.
ec04d908e84e98e945086b0337faa240f5af2d32: The waterfront with colourful houses — UNESCO. The Luís I Bridge (1886, Teófilo Seyrig, a student of Eiffel) — walk along the upper deck in Gaia or along the river on the lower one. Views from the bridge are postcard-perfect.
488eaf4bb9aa3c2c50dee6ac562b5002c87cfd0c: 👟 Steep descents from Clérigos to Ribeira — wear non-slip shoes
5e9fc72b6cc46b0510be427310ca09d94fb1544b: 🎟️ Lello — timed slots, book online in advance
39f40b840320fa8f85aeef2cf0c87386984c1861: 🎟️ Palácio da Bolsa — only with a guide, tours last 30 minutes, €12
25d2765cb43b4330609b259ff3b2e338f6ccaa6c: 🚇 Finish at Ribeira → metro ‘São Bento’ or ‘Jardim do Morro’ (Gaia)
16c7b7380b56ffbbb8ff2414ab22a6532b01eb5a: 🍷 Optionally: across the bridge in Gaia — wine cellars (Graham's, Sandeman, Taylor's, Cálem)
894a1a0d437d64928dfaf71128b9f7a91612e13c: 📸 Sunset from the upper deck of the Luís I Bridge — the best photo of Porto
febb6d5d9d47b981cd40de9c2346b22f06d21b34: The city gave its name to the country (‘Portucale’) and still shares an informal ‘capital’ role with Lisbon. In the 19th century — an industrial centre. The historic centre was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1996.
258a9b4e00f117b27a95cf50acca05cd493cfc5b: Uber / Bolt / FreeNow / BlaBlaCar in Portugal
aacc4cf8bcb1eaa0cf37c9013cce78426a054c51: Uber and Bolt are the leaders. They operate throughout the country. Bolt is usually 10-20% cheaper. FreeNow is a taxi with a driver (official taxis). Taxi (black and green) — payment by meter, more expensive at the airport. BlaBlaCar offers rides between cities.
49d9cb87152624612277dd53c2d14279f5cd1f92: Operates in Lisbon, Porto, Algarve, Braga, Coimbra, Aveiro. UberX (standard), Uber Comfort, Uber Black. Often used for airport trips.
1e535dad473d69df6186dc4b8b85d9a5b6ecc4c0: Estonian company. Usually 10-20% cheaper than Uber. More cars in major cities. Bolt Category Econômica / Standard / XL.
f97d741926599755fd7b6470c17e2cd8a6aec5a0: Taxis (official black and green) via app. Price by meter. Often more predictable cost during peak hours when Uber/Bolt surge.
0df78fe86d9d2798ab651ecbe4c1dd0f749a3d37: Flag down on the street, at a rank, or call by phone. Tariff 1 (day) €3.25 starting + €0.47/km. Tariff 2 (night, weekends) +20%. Airport — fixed prices by destination.
8ff97a24f452ddba1869d81f64fddbf8e7a01b16: Carpooling between cities. Lisboa-Porto can be €15-25 (cheaper than the bus). Lisboa-Madrid is popular. Payment via app, no cash.
12a951688ca399c125af5260eabaced7039201b7: Peak hours / surge: up to x2 multiplier
10c5a33d47091c9dc82fa98e8977a2bf4c393343: Compare Uber and Bolt at the same time — prices change minute by minute
bbe99a2c896d3de04e419075c9398e028676b52e: Uber has Uber Pool (poolshare) — cheaper but slower (not everywhere now)
d9a2acdc43d4574674ea0d5996a40dc0de64ca2c: Avoid taxi traps in tourist areas — they start the meter before you get in or choose a longer route
af69ca576b98ca954a4ba395e17e62c9bb4eacdd: For long trips, BlaBlaCar is cheaper: Lisboa-Porto from €15 vs €20-40 train
485bc7cae80f56f6cf36dbef8460e9631f8df242: Tipping: 5-10% is customary in PT for Uber/Bolt (can leave in app)
23c6c945b80ae84a7d132a9d787585b4b0daf0a0: Payment: any international card + MB Way
fd2d6f27bd60ad111b42525c74fb9c838b35c737: If the taxi overcharged — remember the number and complain to Autoridade de Mobilidade e Transportes
553c790115d4278bb6577ec875548af6c024e81f: AMT — the regulator of transport in PT
42027fb1fda187a5a5f060e3aaa8b2d8654bd700: Sintra — a day trip from Lisbon
7b767d71a2d69d79c18228a241007b413abd2d01: Sintra — a UNESCO city with romantic palaces in the mountains, 40 minutes from Lisbon by train. Main must: Palácio da Pena, Castelo dos Mouros, Quinta da Regaleira. Full itinerary — 1 day, active walking in the hills. Arrive early, leave on the last train. Online tickets are a must.
892e897bf05d8f3a4c940e1d20fad41cdd17e29b: 🚆 Train (recommended): Rossio → Sintra, 40 min, €2.45. Schedule every 20-30 min. First at 06:15, last at 23:15
952148be21fbfeec40fea1ed4fcc97d24fc68199: 🚗 Car: via A16 / IC19, 35-50 min, parking is difficult in season
723378abd87b327890724ec30e6f801c2c95e8ec: 🚌 Car tour from Lisbon with a group: €50-90, usually includes Cabo da Roca + Cascais
233119bedb6e460e70ba938b8fff1983fd673ecc: 💡 Tip: arrive before 9:00, otherwise there will be crowds at Pena Palace
539e2074141e6edad777e7a001c0bc5194e38eed: A colourful Romantic palace from the 19th century on top of a mountain. A symbol of Sintra. Entrance to the park + palace: €14. Park only: €7.50. Open 9:30-19:00 in summer, until 18:00 in winter. **Online tickets with a time slot are mandatory.**
c9cab4b7b145dd78521afe7ecd59363a0c7ad52e: Stone walls from the 8th to 12th centuries on a neighbouring mountain. 360° views. Combo with Pena: €20.50. Separate: €8. Lots of walking on the walls — not for those afraid of heights.
eabeb64df5698c10f2d885790af6710a939ca14c: A mystical estate from the early 20th century with an underground 'initiation well' (Poço Iniciático) — a spiral staircase in the rock. €15. The garden area features Gothic architecture, grottos, and tunnels. **Must-see.**
cdb0550b4f98f962e018a6629fc38fa0ca70ef0c: The city palace of the kings, from the 13th to 18th centuries. Two white conical chimneys — a recognisable silhouette. €10. In the historical centre.
07933186a97b39fa34cf6327b9201cc29256f1e7: An exotic palace-villa in Eastern style. Less visited. €8. 4 km from the centre — taxi.
fed5e54a2c43fbdeef9385ca97d2eb90b3c1648c: A Franciscan monastery hidden in the forest. Ascetic cells in the rock walls. €7. 10 km from the centre.
47fec5211e88f9984d0efb8a16219a2b8cf3a558: Train from Rossio. At the exit — bus 434 (€5/day, hop-on-hop-off) or taxi/Uber to Pena (€6-8).
788929195180c27121d654627b4425df3b41d83d: With an online slot, enter inside, tour the palace + gardens (2 hours). Visit the main rooms + panoramic terraces.
d4c6ed11f4a6af74bf4f92fe387469361475df0a: Walk down (30 minutes on a forest path) or take the bus. Walk around the walls perimeter.
d75e4019d290f0769427ce6bdb7ac0451dccc74b: In the centre of Sintra. Traditional queijadas and travesseiros de Sintra (pastries). Café Piriquita — iconic bakery since 1862.
359a4fed463bfddea54cbe2e40c34f381f3eadf9: The main city palace. Quick visit 1 hour.
01c476db5a42a0b2a2a615528eebc36d0804302a: 10 minutes walk from the centre. Initiation well + tunnels + gardens. At least 2 hours.
13c3792a8e25c0fec774dd94df94a28c5cc08a17: The westernmost point of Europe, 18 km from Sintra. Bus 403 Scotturb, 40 minutes. Sunset over the Atlantic.
261fd90fe5a8eb13f7176999dab6eddb4bda4f0c: Train to Lisbon. Last Rossio at 23:15.
9dece91c5ebd27d15a0740410dca3c0d2d5caa79: 🎫 **Online tickets** for Pena are **mandatory** (parquesdesintra.pt). During the season, slots can sell out a week in advance

1cc8ad8f6bcba1505642a61ff5677a48c2bc912f: Four channels: general (LinkedIn, Net-Empregos, Jobs.at), IT (Landing.jobs, ITJobs.pt, Stack Overflow Jobs), executive (Michael Page, Hays), frontier jobs (remote work for PT companies from abroad). Minimum wage 2026 — €920/month gross. Average IT salary for middle level — €2,500–€4,000 gross; senior €4,500–€7,500. For foreigners, knowledge of PT/EN is essential; only EN is needed in IT and startups.
508788242499e421ab39adc3303728d4c0abfdbb: Dominates in IT, marketing, sales, top management
6ca510561c7264138ad06db43eaeed842e02cfb9: Best for expats — most recruiters speak EN

ef55e796f01b8b8f0908a6169ca300445b3c9058: Aggregator. Cross-posts LinkedIn + Net-Empregos + direct job vacancies. Convenient filter for 'EN-speaking'.
244764570074db88c158555c113afab1461de591: German platform, popular in DACH companies in PT. Many EN job vacancies, good conditions.
c162f1e581dca997b267b9a93b7e1b2db022ed7c: Government-run. Registration is mandatory for unemployed individuals receiving benefits. Low-skilled + certain subsidies.

9612bc3f75396264f0881e4b21729f0c339709ec: Local PT IT portal. More corporate and enterprise (banks, telcos). Often requests for PT.
e2f43d612d1d022e186da7f978b6822cb6e98ae6: International remote job vacancies. PT companies are present, paying according to European standards. Often 100% EN.

bde140b5285323040373245f3f0c7a87f761030a: In PT, salaries are negotiable. The phrase “Qual é a vossa banda salarial?” (what is your range) is standard. Do not mention the first figure; if pressed, state expected gross, usually with room for negotiation.
7a6ccdd41c957672cfc4f433c7d60eb9a4d3697c: Type: sem termo (indefinite), a termo (fixed-term 6m–2 years), sem vínculo (freelance via RV)

cace5af41b85f288e685f8744f83f7b90b78334d: 🌍 Remote — company from another EU country: Portugal hiring via EOR (Deel, Remote, Oyster)

7533f50818ae991ea53733f21fcbda182fe3a1ed: Recibos Verdes = digital invoices for the self-employed in Portal das Finanças (tax portal). Used by all freelancers and entrepreneurs. Simplified regime: 75% of turnover is the tax base, 25% is exempt. Social contributions 21.4%. VAT — only if turnover > €15,000/year.
c5bed69f42bfa505256a39ecf86c9ffa71b137cd: Select CAE (activity code). For IT — 62010 (programming), for consulting — 70220, for design — 74100.

66db662afb166faf38b1dd0f384bed7d30e2e629: Dogs in Portugal — registration, dangerous breeds, tax
a870297f8632625ba1bc1016f625c92b647cf320: Dog registration: SIAC (Sistema de Identificação dos Animais de Companhia) is mandatory after microchip — free at the vet. Municipal licence (licença canina) — €5-15/year at Junta de Freguesia. Dangerous breeds (Category A/B according to DL 315/2009): civil liability insurance of at least €50k is mandatory, owner must be ≥ 18 years old without a judicial record, muzzle + leash in public places. List: Rottweiler, Pit Bull, Staffordshire, Tosa Inu, Fila Brasileiro, Cão de Fila, American Staffordshire, Dogo Argentino, and mixes.
bba344ee934e10015d70c862061d48cb890dfdab: Mandatory for **all dogs** ≥ 4 months (and domestic cats)
cdc64320f235608326903d87f7883effd812b340: Automatically registered in SIAC (centralized database) — free
2be5cbbb0b18048c6f90a183cda6c01f017cbe04: Submitted at Junta de Freguesia where you reside
06a62569a8828b7dbfba9c5bdb89373d2c404f46: Documents: SIAC registration, rabies vaccination, certificado de esterilização (if sterilised)
1ab6b28637787eb15f84399cd4667013632c99cf: Cost: **€5-15/year** (depends on the municipality; categories A/B — more expensive, up to €50)
c9147ffa872a9b8408aa822156bab0f3ffe1b636: Badge (medalha) — a ring with a number, attached to the collar

4b024f4d626c982b28edd5a59cdd4f8563cc2e19: Importing a car from the EU — detailed procedure
8ff3f03ab2657fb82586e024403a1de2c652908b: Importing a car from the EU (Germany, France, Spain, Poland, Netherlands, Italy) is popular due to a ~20-30% price difference. Procedure: 1) DAV (customs declaration of vehicles) within 20 days; 2) ISV (depends on CC, CO₂, age — for used cars partial discounts up to 65%); 3) IUC annually; 4) Registration (PT number plate) at IMT; 5) IPO (vehicle inspection); 6) COC or certificate of conformity. For new EVs — ISV 0%. For relocation (transferência residência) — full ISV exemption if owned for 6+ months.

3a653d5137f335f9f4af51588fd5ae726ded9c68: The government subsidises the purchase of a new EV up to €4,000 for individuals and €6,000 for companies (Fundo Ambiental, application limit is annual — runs out quickly). e8d31e7dfa19bad839181667dfd59bca8cc3e814: Portugal annually funds the Incentive for the Introduction to the Market of Low Emission Vehicles programme. The budget is reset on 1 January and usually ends within 2-4 months.

dc12a300c2527961dca0ce158645f961f9782979: How to check a used car before buying in Portugal
b81d0ac4aaf07f17746e97c64f8d745de054ab1e: Check **4 layers**: (1) **ACP / Standvirtual** — historical report by VIN, €15-30 provides fines, km, accidents; (2) **Carfax Portugal** — international database with US/EU history; (3) **Portal das Finanças** — check for IUC, ISV debts; (4) **Manual inspection** + independent mechanic €50-150. The seller is required to provide DUA (Documento Único Automóvel), valid IPO, manual, keys (original + spare). Penalty for the seller for concealment: up to 3 × the value of the car.

8df3f417e3ded0fd825124b4b250ab1211ebc22d: Car rental in Portugal — tips for residents and tourists
89967d50fdb68356f9675ad6e90868ee116c2bcf: Renting in PT — from €20/day (budget compact) to €150+/day (premium). International companies like Hertz / Europcar / Avis are more expensive than local ones but more reliable. Budget: Sixt, Goldcar, Centauro — cheap, but many hidden fees. Requirements: driver age ≥ 21, licence ≥ 1 year, credit card (debit not accepted everywhere). Full insurance (CDW + TP + zero excess) ~€15-25/day — mandatory if you don't have a premium credit card. Deposit €800-2500. Via Verde is usually included, toll roads are selective.

440b8a78a976a7a50db452ef8de87702c9ce5bd5: In large cities, the municipality sells discounted parking permits (dístico de residente) to residents — from free to ~€80/year. It operates based on residency (atestado) + vehicle registration. Lisbon operates EMEL, Cascais — Cascais Próxima, Setúbal — Divisão de Trânsito. Without the dístico, residents will have to pay hourly like tourists.
1ce397a8e988311c9f74f943f1f4c7a95b4755ee: EMEL (Empresa Municipal de Mobilidade e Estacionamento de Lisboa) manages all paid parking in the city. Residents of one of the 24 freguesias of Lisbon can obtain a dístico de residente.
a6b4cdb5b74adc10277a97f8cfa4a30727d1d585: Proof of residência — atestado de residência from Junta de Freguesia in the parking zone (not in the neighbouring one)
3f4dea823fe399b9e9ab5b796477683f2c855b4c: The vehicle is registered under your NIF at IMT (not someone else's)
3905429de177b79746f9fdd7bb289d92cd25112e: EMEL.pt → Dísticos → «Pedir Dístico» — online with CMD. Or at the EMEL office (Av. Casal Ribeiro). Issued within 7-14 days.
be0e4047dc1f4720c174fe465ef3c9cd71f045f4: 1st car: €12/year (the first year is often free)
0db6cf615cf652678a0507259e765ae153b81418: Dístico is valid ONLY in your freguesia. For neighbouring areas — pay hourly. Electric and hybrid vehicles have a separate rate, plus free parking in some zones (see below).
68f1b7d4c884f127b32eccda6e073f886c37557a: The municipality of Cascais offers resident parking through Cascais Próxima. Residents receive a viver Cascais card (city ID card), which provides access to discounted parking.
6e8fc7f01b78c188f41d49884358d0cf6884a93c: First — obtain the viver Cascais card (see separate guide)
8b9075ebe042474576d0dd2988e9b4885a5dbd0d: After activation — apply for dístico at cascaisproxima.pt
cb43ae485739c36243e6789eec615f647a998b31: Works in the centre of Cascais and Estoril — in other areas of the municipality (Alcabideche, São Domingos de Rana) parking is free
d560a998a82ba42b14813702c12d798064b0f49a: Atestado from Junta + vehicle registration on NIF
2c942e94b03eded76c4eeb02d8dd2852d6276282: Submission to Divisão de Trânsito, Rua Dr. Francisco Santana Ramos
68fb7af6002f5155f87bc3f63ee0ab4b480421e6: Works in the centre of Setúbal; Azeitão, Palmela and beyond — free parking
80e444d36b270ee9958e4fe80978c9eb4412af5d: Lisbon: EMEL — first 2 hours free for EVs with a green number plate (MEV marking)
6b40e869855653d820e9ca6f5c1d9a769cb09c80: Porto: EV parking — 50% discount in municipal areas
9ec4115e21f67fce3606c32ad1677a84436b9f5e: Cascais: free parking for EVs at municipal charging stations
759a3cf78d9261a85eafbdf8a1b1f1c14128acfc: Nationwide: free parking in Mobi.E charging zones while charging
8ed55f9c6143588e06ae8ea79b8c9cdd64e2235e: Distinctive sign: EVs receive a designated symbol in the certificate + green frame for the number plate
1eea27fdf2cc27335ed2049dab9e2f1805fb8e0d: Parking without a dístico or payment: €30-50 (for quick payment)
881cbcf048a72fde6b551800f605d5b489eff7c2: Parking in a disabled zone without documentation: €120 (fine + penalty point)
e286c530fb539abd10bc42646bf69f122eecc1c1: 50% discount for payment within 15 days (minimum da coima)
9991fa1321e3f5354ee5531f47d025f7f0730304: Appealing the fine — through EMEL.pt or Câmara, deadlines 30 days
dde9b1b84625f88c8c2f3b260ec0be97ffb5038c: per year, often the 1st year is free
d13653417bc4d38e5ea225aa75bed5d0083f4d2b: Serious traffic violations — criminal traffic offences
19c6c15fa059bd346158f332a7b42dcc9a2de6af: In Portugal, some violations are not administrative but criminal (Código Penal + Código da Estrada). These include: driving with a BAC ≥1.2 g/l, refusal to take an alcohol test, driving after a driving ban, manslaughter / serious bodily harm through negligence, hit-and-run. Penalties: driving ban from 2 months to 3 years, fines up to €15,000, imprisonment up to 5 years. Points deducted from the licence: 4-5 points immediately. A criminal record remains in the certidão do registo criminal.
4f885d7cf1d29a78365d9901279b7d6ac5dc10fb: Alcohol behind the wheel — the main trap
25ccb3d81529ed2dcf0fbc1be6ecb43b0da8b167: Portugal has one of the strictest BAC control systems in Europe.
805e16d1fa70ee48c1c8b0bfd47efe32b7370120: 🍺 **BAC 0.5–0.8 g/l** (about 1-2 glasses of wine) — **serious offence**: fine €250-€1,250, ban 1-12 months, minus 3 points
2d4132dcefb7919284ac72c83bf1e0ec2793d645: 🍷 **BAC 0.8–1.2 g/l** — **very serious offence**: fine €500-€2,500, ban 2-24 months, minus 5 points
ab9cd22cc567b66ed27634b5d1cce7282dd335ac: 🍸 **BAC ≥ 1.2 g/l** — **criminal** (art. 292 Código Penal): up to 1 year imprisonment or fine up to 120 daily rates, ALWAYS a ban of 2+ months, criminal record for 5-10 years
b3a9d93a24b306c73d8863916468ef71a1a87109: 🚫 **Refusal to take a test** — equated to BAC ≥ 1.2 g/l + charge of "desobediência qualificada" (up to 2 years imprisonment)
e62880770183e94012590717aea70e7829b795f8: ⚠️ For new drivers (<3 years of licence): limit 0.2 g/l — even a pint of beer can be a violation
dfe70b872d4a98ce1f87d6fe0014b77fa3d58f50: 💼 For professional drivers (taxis, trucks, buses): 0.2 g/l
1e09b66776e8ec3c5d99a3d7bb0e51b35668d842: Since 2016, the points system (carta por pontos). All drivers start with 12 points. Each serious violation results in a deduction of 1-5 points. Reaching 0 means loss of licence, retaking theory + practical tests.
d080e84608bd451b0519a30547a72bbc27216dc2: Exceeding speed by 30-60 km/h in the city (60-80 outside the city, 80-100 on the motorway)
e129c9da5dbec8affc4345087967764fbf670ef8: Using a phone while driving without hands-free
64ebae26d6d5c2c0c34b52ea407717009ee4ee80: Failing to yield to a pedestrian on the zebra crossing
2dc2716c18fc641b099b3a15047c6f71cb4543ca: No child seat for under 12 years / under 135 cm
f7e0e2a53c1fa7eee017c60d06defe2382de546a: Exceeding speed by 60+ km/h in the city
17a9c93a04bf9f6ae26918fb46dbd43baaf19343: Failing to stop when required by GNR / PSP
3aab48ae410a93160ac4109e107fba153cc52a93: Driving in an unauthorised area (one-way, pedestrian zone)
1b1a14526da01e17a6a929ac62623039c88e8403: Driving without a licence or with an expired one
6b27e5358eb7ee41b0d40b666dcecbc5eddc8309: Driving without a valid IPO (technical inspection)
0beb161596e3999adf931986ecee2090605304ee: Refusal to take an alcohol/drug test
19ce114cd42bb24dc3b831903654fbf496cb5fdb: Manslaughter / serious harm due to negligence
4569de42b2c286fe27e3b24c1eac7bff81725690: Points recovery: +1 point for every 3 years without violations, max 12. A 'rehabilitation course' is possible — 2-3 days at IMT — +2 points (paid, ~€250).
05c990cc6529548ac91e8b7b050650ce699f30af: Cameras — margin of error and how they work
725743cafb9f2dfafd742421e03245a39071c4fa: 📐 Official margin of error: **7%** (built into the fine calculation)
d55fa846ee0076e3b22638351c718c71236a1aed: Example: limit 120, speed 140 — recorded as (140 × 0.93 = 130.2) → fine for +10 km/h, not +20
1a7391c920caf38fc7a687e69ddb866af5f7a8db: Fixed cameras: warning signs 300 m in advance
9350d3154100602d39fb70c23b7ff2e739c8f600: Hidden radars ANSR: no warning, may be in GNR vehicles
9da77e67cd5a19c34daa8a0b2d22c42d5a194c4d: Mobile radar — often on the roadside in small villages on national roads
dd456a17e91135893b36116a29b2d35b994a3f31: The fine arrives by post at the owner's address within 30-90 days
c61aaeec65ebeb75116f3ac8ab0630e3c07d4abe: 🕐 Payment period: 15 days — minimum fine (×1), 30 days — no fine, afterwards — ×2-3
2a1c3878ce6c5d384b8bc7e5b597a4dcaed3d574: ⚖️ Appeal: through recurso in tribunal, within 20 days of receiving notification — lawyer €150-300
cb07bf3f2cf6bb041f86cf9a5d38cb17a7ff9f89: How to check your fines and points
5d98e8598898da7b5b6b01bcb252a84ea09ee1b9: 🚦 IMT Portal Digital (imt-ip.pt) — log in with CMD / CC → "Consultar carta" → shows points + history
de23d69bde818fdadd75e3f94284242a2e6149da: ⚖️ Portal das Finanças → e-balcão — unpaid fines and court cases
002bcbccacde71c95bdf580f35e02db187cfdc3f: 📧 Email notifications from ANSR — subscribe at ansr.pt for alerts about new fines
ec91769ad2250673d8bb2465db650c66b8e215f4: 📄 Certidão da carta de condução — request via imt-ip.pt, shows complete history (for work, visa, court)
85080e27bc189416989523f9bfdd8e9ec1654ccf: The points system applies to **all** drivers in Portugal, even those with foreign licences. Upon reaching 0 points — driving rights are revoked in PT (your home licence is invalid in PT); when returning home — the licence operates as usual.
f7f399ced07f139769e5e962d6f1c80d2a1dd3f8: Fines for foreign licences are recorded in the central EUCARIS database (for EU licences) or in the national registry PT
66a38c6626a5a080ef231d17fea4d70661ef03a6: When exchanging for Portuguese licences (after the exchange) — points are transferred
d387b515a5c973a2cf57f48392286021accb134a: Notifications of fines are sent to the vehicle registration address
b762e84fad4629581e282788f6daa75d8e7367fc: For rent-car with ENG licences: the company receives the fine and issues a bill + €30-50 "admin fee"
94763eea32756be2c4e442b019652a59a215238b: Via Verde vs alternatives — all ways to pay for roads
04e1248530959231bebaf2276b3dcbba94d2ca6c: Via Verde — an electronic identifier for toll-free passage. Not the only option. Alternatives: Via Card (one-time), pre-paid Toll Card for tourists, post-payment via CTT / Payshop within 5 days, EasyToll for foreign plates.
bd437ccb41e3e20697e9b4f63ef37b8a74e9250e: Via Verde (the best option for residents)
e17ed3977a5cba5b8955b7ea8c82b583b1bd07af: Electronic identifier (green box on the windscreen)
888663bd2164a8f27cb6d2358fc98c2207a80ddc: Works on ALL toll roads, EMEL parking, Galp/Repsol fuel stations (discounts), Transtejo, Soflusa ferries
dc26d899439375a5751f6f1bc62a5736d9488c68: Installation: order online at viaverde.pt, delivered by post, or at Via Verde/CTT branches
ca1f7f73bd016df30ef3819b37c50e66597cc98c: Free (no monthly fee): Via Verde Gratuita subscription for those with <€8/month traffic
457646427562636b0dd02cb2856a424bd2f54169: Plastic pre-paid card for a specific amount (€5, €10, €20). Used at 'electronic' toll booths on old toll roads (A1, A2) — insert into the Via Verde apparatus on the contact strip. DOES NOT work on Scut (toll-free).
01b23d6d18bb67405e429882923c075e098b747d: Upon first entry onto Scut, you link your bank card to the licence plate at the EasyToll ports (available at the border and airports). After this, the toll is automatically deducted. It is valid for 30 days.
04bff4b9e67ed816b78a20b31a942f81f60ba46d: Card with a balance of €5/€10/€20/€40. Activated via SMS → baixa. Purchase at Galp, Repsol, BP petrol stations. For short trips.
abe7c7dc34d4f7a79a6d0fa92b49d91144086f77: If you drove on Scut without Via Verde and did not link EasyToll — you must pay at a CTT post office or Payshop within 5 working days using the matrícula number. After 5 days — fine + legal proceedings through AT.
ed938163545b8c1874af5c1bbf02eaadcf0a329c: What is Scut and how to distinguish them
d53b77bfb77d9946c9848760d0c886c35cfcbcec: Scut (Sem Cobrança ao Utilizador) — previously 'free-flow' roads, now toll roads via electronic portals. There are no booths. Do not stop — cameras read the number and should deduct from Via Verde or EasyToll.
6cada8fc6c3a439fc9502b1ddcadc24c65fb4a67: Sign: portal above the road with the sign 'Pórtico Eletrónico'

c5a92ab5f43940500bb5b8c3812d658833d9d91b: Queues at Portuguese consulates (Moscow, Kyiv, Minsk, Almaty, Istanbul) — 2-6 months waiting time. Slots open unevenly, often at night. Strategies: **check every few hours** (slots appear due to cancellations); **monitor bots** (Telegram bots notify about new slots); **travel to a neighbouring jurisdiction** (Yerevan, Tbilisi, Vilnius — often have more availability); **private advisor** (VFS Global, agencies) — €50-200 but guaranteed slot. Alternative: travel without a visa to PT (visa-free Schengen for 90 days for several countries) and apply for a D visa there.
c4ed4930f2c745a496c1792167877b90c7f209cd: The PT consulate only accepts residents of the country of accreditation. However, you can apply through the consulate of the country where you are **temporarily located** (tourism, business trip). This is a legal workaround.

1e3211282676d7625c5ba4a7189393865a0344ed: How to learn Portuguese — schools, apps, tips
f73091e5b759857aac75e18e0b797a895aca07eb: PT-PT (European) differs from BR-PT in pronunciation and partially in vocabulary. Three options: (1) free state PLA courses for residents (150 hours, A2 in 6 months); (2) subsidised IEFP courses for the unemployed; (3) private: Fast Forward, CIAL, CLIC — €350-900 per course. Apps: Practice Portuguese (specialising in PT-PT, best choice), Portuguese With Carla (YouTube), Pimsleur (audio). Duolingo — only BR variant, not the best start for PT-PT.
ee95df372f8060cc8ff1bde1a79d4cf46f3356a3: PT-PT vs PT-BR — what to learn?
70d891b60f3e46bac6b54e852d58aba15ad8baab: The Portuguese language has two main varieties: European Portuguese (PT-PT) and Brazilian (PT-BR). In Portugal, people speak PT-PT.
f03dfe5feb0b5b979edcd57c2101d8a0259c2271: 🗣️ Pronunciation: PT-PT — 'closed' vowels, swallowed syllables ('Lis-bo-a' → 'Lʒbwa'). BR-PT — clearer, more open
2363846392493f5a9f194c12d7249d7d9db07fbd: 📝 Grammar: varies slightly (tu/você, construção com gerundio, estar/ficar)
4ed95fd1dd5705873c6d1879d4b372eddbdad055: 📚 Vocabulary: bus = autocarro (PT) / ônibus (BR); train = comboio (PT) / trem (BR); you = você (often BR) / vocês (formally PT)
fdab1550fb648fb9061c6e5c2b287ef6b187ccf2: 🎬 Films: Brazilian films always have subtitles in PT — PT news are not shown in BR without translation
6cd4b05154a443d8c4a6fad7c869e1fd82e5d9b9: 🎯 **Conclusion: learn PT-PT if you are coming to Portugal.** BR is understood — but for A2 citizenship requirements, PT-PT is needed. BR content (Duolingo, most YouTube) — fine for background exposure, not for exams
52d050acf8e18b70081f61eb89581adeabedd28c: 150 hours for level A2 (which is required for citizenship)
6a8f0fc7591f43027b4cbccd4a20605b11717517: Free for **all** foreigners with any status (including refugees and non-residents)
53805bf7bde6471c3f9a4db20129557af4c75dcb: Registration: Centro Qualifica at your place of residence (qualifica.gov.pt) or AIMA
5640bc57c772be4855560d15f2987b8de49061d5: Format: evening / weekend classes, 2-3 times a week for 3 hours

9261549dee15237a2b97d5d8ed1145810872b088: Transporting belongings to Portugal — container, companies, luggage
5e395456f7d6e26fbd7b4fa046e59877316782ee: Options: (1) **sea container** 20' (33 m³) — €3,000-5,000 from Moscow/Kiev, 4-8 weeks. 40' — €4,500-7,500, 2 containers per family; (2) **groupage cargo (LCL)** from €600 for 5 m³; (3) **car transporter / furniture van** across Europe — €2,000-4,000 from Poland/Germany; (4) **Mudança within the EU** — specialised movers €800-2,500; (5) **luggage on the plane** — from €50/suitcase extra. e3084615a7c9f64ba7c24f282acd705f07879a3b: When moving to Portugal, you do NOT pay import duties and IVA on personal belongings if you meet the conditions of Regulamento 1186/2009 EU.

1559885bd13b67203b944b6ae048327718d07fb4: Complete inventory (detailed list of items with value estimation + photos)

427ce4bfec067378800da66b409717c8d0f09fbd: Proof of residence in PT (atestado / contract)

e5d89a4b61712c1269de379a502d3401167d43ed: If necessary — CITES permits for exotic items, regulations for antiques

297f36f896f1f27bc3f5089337fdd6c76de7afd3: **Exempt import upon relocation** (Isenção ISV): the vehicle must have been yours for ≥ 6 months before relocation, purchased in the country of departure

944d1d11fe6b73412753ed26e646c22321f947cc: Registration at IMT: €270 + booking verification + IPO
4d6c502fe86401eb30bac1e10ee9a21e8a3ced66: Alternative: import without Isenção = full ISV (from €2,000 for a small car to €10,000+ for a BMW)
ab22095b514d515aa72311bca99aa2a420a497de: ⚠️ Electric vehicles: 0% ISV when relocating, plus reduced IUC
f99f580d5e78c56b38a2262dd62ffe253597fafd: 📄 Separate guide: «car-import» in the vehicle category

34676f174e5dc813c2524df6be929d619ec1fe79: 🔍 **Check reviews**: Google Reviews, Trustpilot, Facebook groups russia-pt

3d820f34dcd9a5a0da7bd704ab83e8caceb0077f: Mail forwarding when moving: **Reencaminhamento Postal** — 3 months (€17.55), 6 months (€28.90), 12 months (€51.30). **Reserva domiciliária** — holding mail at the post office for up to 2 months (€9.60). Parcels from non-EU: 23% IVA + duty from €150. Customs clearance — on ctt.pt via ‘Desalfandegamento Online’ in 5 minutes, receipt €5 per parcel + IVA on value + shipping. Lost parcel — CTT online assistant, compensation up to €50.
cbdd2ebdd8833d6e893c6eb3f4435b572da00ad0: When changing address in PT (internal move) or leaving the country — CTT will redirect all incoming correspondence.
ee0b010f16def301524461e5aae6a361c6fb836a: Order: ctt.pt → ‘Mudança de morada’ or at any CTT post office
a516bcea514c438b54f63563e73e0900a1c9dc17: Starts working 3 working days after order
fc151af6ab8c89780e79ea3a2ed070e898e380ad: All letters and non-registered parcels are redirected
2478ef152d66c2b8545e3dd5ca9639dfcf150171: Registered mail (registada) → notification goes to the new address, the parcel itself goes to the original post office, must be collected
a164bd741d6b07d4b340482475fed6fc7060bd5d: **€9.60** for a period of up to 2 months
032507473a802ff61063d986b8ce62cda05f5606: Mail is not delivered, but held at your CTT post office
cbac958a860bfcf5345fb739c701be9a8e127412: Useful for long holidays / business trips
2c0dc10123024dd3a88389481cffd6d078cc554c: Collect everything at the post office on the specified date
ef30f85fa4d64b40f462e015378e12862df473f7: Do not confuse with reencaminhamento. Changing the address in Finanças, IRN, AIMA — through ePortugal 'Mudança de morada' is free, but changes not the mail, but official records.
a068947e0cfa1781585eda97cbd64334f00313c8: **No duties or VAT** — EU single market
912c994149901216ae7e7251b26403ea246917f4: Arrive within 2-7 days by standard delivery
139c86a741c4d603886a140e7e8febe19c8d383d: Tracking: on ctt.pt by number, or through the CTT app
f3a8e1a3c5c7c25e8accfef85b575777be68941b: If you are not home: a carton ('aviso') in the mailbox, 14 days to collect at the post office
5eb0c4a0f3b61b230b0f5ba6edef1293416c8610: All packages from non-EU (Russia, USA, China, UK post-Brexit, Ukraine until TPD) undergo customs control.
19a0a800bd0af8f7165605a1080c8123b5abd676: **IVA (VAT) 23%**: applied to ALL packages from non-EU since 1 July 2021 (previously the threshold was <€22)
2761bbbbce1ba5bdfa786975330609c6268b3830: **Duty**: on goods > **€150** (value + shipping). The rate depends on the TARIC code of the product — 0-17% for most, up to 50% for certain categories of clothing
355f917674c90eec30b6ebb1af5a7a26f9de1a02: **Additional CTT charges**: €5 handling fee + €1-3 per additional operation
261bc3bcc7544f6d4ed2519d5d02ce7d5dc05044: Total for a package valued at €100: €23 IVA + €5 CTT = **€128** total tax
55a8c57f7046329feaeb6ac4530fde6dfd78cf6c: For a package valued at €200 (value + shipping): 23% IVA + ~12% duty + €5 CTT = ~**€77** tax
ae1a27cad25217745458099ea3337ae723b767ee: 🛂 How to go through customs clearance
6b89f38b793f978dbb8be43369f2a9b1d350ad00: **Automatically** up to €150: CTT issues IOSS (if the sender is a large retailer like AliExpress/Amazon with IOSS registration) → you only pay for shipping, the tax is already included in the price
31f28309e8442ce36f8d45a178c835e7cebaa51a: **Through CTT Desalfandegamento Online**: if the sender does not have IOSS — you receive an SMS/email with a link for processing
c7c003c96aa70bff9a07bf0e99e6dcf90eb04787: **Open ctt.pt → Desalfandegamento → enter the package code → upload invoice**

a06e30f47d1c725a6d7a2a5e4d5b0ddd64c76348: Copy / brand-fake — confiscation + fine for the sender

fe1d53a6fab4dea5fc75d5e1b32e27ec1ac44d32: IOSS (Import One-Stop Shop) — a system since 2021 that allows non-EU retailers to collect IVA at the time of purchase. For consumers = hassle-free receipt.
1a48e6c9fde2b273f478de8d0422f6d5b163af35: ✅ **Amazon.com, AliExpress, eBay, Temu, Shein** — IOSS-registered, IVA included in the price, package passes automatically

9b87c941b5be9ff2b978d0fc2e9207505f81a4e0: Drones in Portugal — registration, rules, where to fly
90f2b6c48c5953dc06c83a01f719d137ad4e1cc9: In Portugal, the EU 2019/947 regulations apply: operator registration with ANAC (free) for drones ≥250g or with a camera + theoretical training. You can fly up to 120m in the Open category. You cannot fly over people, within 5 km of airports, or over national parks without permission. Fines of up to €2,000 for violations.
52936aae09c21b0bc6765c78b7e5aa93510e18e9: Portugal applies EU regulation 2019/947 through the national agency ANAC (Autoridade Nacional da Aviação Civil). Registration is mandatory for:
39149291f1ba1de84cee21093dc820b3218935f7: Any drone with a camera/sensors (even <250 g)
c8f3c44f93d683268312feec0956598e8d6497f3: Any drone ≥ 250 g (with or without a camera)
a39741edcfb979132248c630fa85934164db72c3: Registration is NOT required for: toys <250 g without a camera (DJI Neo in its pure form — yes, registration is required, as it has a camera)
5266d313acf24b6a3c20c8ad97d2e466fd9df051: 1. Create an account on the ANAC Drones Portal
64080161c9eead22fcf12b74ad69049f64e7d6db: voanouceu.anac.pt → Register operator → CMD / CC / NIF + password. Enter your full name, address, NIF, contact.
2782810be02b421d8f43981f7f59c2d9350df18a: The system issues a UAS Operator ID in the format PRT-RP-XXXXXXXX. You must stick this number on the drone (sticker or engraving).
8875126dba77cbc9ebb845d9e42da5368c7857f1: 3. Take the online exam (for Open A1/A3)
0392a84325221fbf66fceff156f129287e38adc7: On the same portal — 40 questions, free of charge, can be retaken. Upon completion — A1/A3 certificate (Open category), valid for 5 years. For A2 (closer to people) — an additional in-person exam for €90.
e2bb65bd9d22f6cf94a0740a598765a42ebfce5f: Mandatory for commercial operators and drones ≥ 20 kg. Recommended for recreational use. Insurers: Fidelidade, Generali, AXA — civil liability up to €750,000, €50-150/year.
eb557cc319411e660e563ede1506f6b03ae6c59c: Where you can and cannot fly
b6ee1cb8784be8812591a4e1ae51276aeea2a086: A1 (C0 <250 g): over people, but not over mass gatherings
8375a12686118aed2b6892206675e5ff016d79c9: A2 (C2 <4 kg): 30 m from people; 5 m in ‘slow mode’
936ccb99403871506fbffd4eefd85993618c3f0c: A3 (C3/C4 <25 kg): 150 m from residential/commercial areas, only over uninhabited places
b52f2e4e86ab4fe45ec30fe81c547ce9b418f787: Up to 120 m height AGL (above ground level)
948aec9f7fb8dea902dd0d3a4057bd69addc1b16: Only during the day and within visual line of sight (VLOS)
3ca15c23897d32b2ad5c568223bd66011965d42c: Within 5 km of any airport/aerodrome (Lisboa, Porto, Faro, Cascais, Tires, etc.)
c1f1e72001a863ad4e85aaa9cad32e88354f96f9: In NOTAM/geozone areas — see the map at voanouceu.anac.pt/geozonas
e64e5e7800b857ebc15b3c8c687c343fcf262608: Over national parks and nature reserves (Gerês, Serra da Estrela, Sintra) without written permission from ICNF
6e99d11ebeaeab2b3ca8904156319892a86b1a61: Over palaces, castles, churches, UNESCO historical zones
c7d97247f70c7b659151c2694bfa4ec86651cc3e: On beaches during the season (June-September) — ICNF + municipalities ban
c66930a588d328bdfe40be5f59294bbb962d69d9: Over mass gatherings (concerts, football, markets, demonstrations)
15227259c8235bbe053050a5079b22b29834d79b: In weather conditions: wind >12 m/s, rain, visibility <500m
2b502f1abd7878356769cf982808c2086c64eeb2: 🏛️ Filming commercial photos/videos — registration as a commercial operator + tax declaration (recibos verdes or legal entity) required
ff361da48a8cb12085895bdfab1439e6fda3dbea: 🌊 Beaches: off-season (October-May) — allowed; in season (June-September) — ban from 8:00 to 20:00
18bd95eb1918d915f3f0c62e69efc59c8c86610e: 🏝️ Madeira, Azores — same rules + additional restrictions for natural zones
a8bb1a2fcab5a6f62740f6e07a7b6fe2b6d53f31: 🎥 Filming weddings, events — permitted in A1/A2, but it is recommended to notify ANAC 48 hours in advance
d09f6950b81629420d2f6adafe67d79507acd141: 🛫 Flying beyond VLOS (BVLOS) or above 120 m — only in Specific category with separate authorization from ANAC
791f794a3c84354c909fde7c6fb7f8927fd66b55: From the EU: duty-free, free import
1fcd71c93706e423cdd20ada35db2d065e69f0a0: From third countries (Russia, USA, China): through customs for value >€150 — IVA 23% + possible duty
01a0559970a234d66816d2252507322f6aa37d1f: Batteries: LiPo >100Wh — prohibited in hand luggage on flights, only in checked baggage

fde213e8855a784a16f55abcba6a4358182bed71: Basic expat toolkit: MB WAY (payments), Autenticação.gov (Chave Móvel Digital), SNS 24 (healthcare), Portal AT (taxes), AIMA Renovações (residence permit), MobiCascais/Navegante (transport), CTT (post), Bolt/Uber (taxis), Glovo/Uber Eats (food delivery), Continente/Lidl Plus (supermarkets), Via Verde (toll roads), Folhetos.pt (discounts). By law, from 2023 many government services require Chave Móvel Digital — install it first.
d0c4b15cf323e8b1def33fbf8524b5a4c236df18: CMD (Chave Móvel Digital) — government electronic signature. Without it, the following do not work: Portal AT, AIMA online, SNS 24, IRN, Segurança Social Direta. Activation: autenticacao.gov.pt → Aderir CMD → NIF + PT phone number. SMS code for each operation.

8008c6ad1c25cc77987b15526585b300eaecf153: Higher education in Portugal — for foreign students
d4f880711c51e3bf88d626e5752940550a964636: Bachelor's degree (licenciatura) 3 years, Master's degree (mestrado) 2 years, PhD (doutoramento) 3-4 years. Entry — Concurso Nacional de Acesso (for PT residents) or Concurso Especial para Estudantes Internacionais (only for non-EU citizens). Costs: EU residents pay as PT (€700-€1,400/year state), non-EU — €2,000-€7,000/year state, €3,500-€15,000/year private. Top universities: Universidade de Lisboa, Universidade do Porto, Universidade Nova de Lisboa, Universidade de Coimbra (5 in the world top 500). Erasmus+ — full access. D4 Student Visa for non-EU.

444a24bbf916353d35492133f0855f248b3f63ab: How to avoid waiting in urgência all day
1b83705a175b2e0f8e358a86b07f712cd671b42f: Urgência in PT operates under the Manchester Triage System — a colour is assigned based on the severity of the condition. You will be attended to within 10 minutes (red) — 240 minutes (blue). To reduce waiting time: (1) first contact SNS 24 (**808 24 24 24**) — a doctor will guide you to the appropriate level; (2) use SNS 24 Atendimento Complementar (at night / on weekends — via the app); (3) private urgências (CUF, Lusíadas, Luz) €20-80 per visit; (4) local medical post; (5) check online queue status through the "Tempos de Espera SNS" app.
b3db37e23621daa997bc331f8e15af129ba4bf6f: SNS 24 (**808 24 24 24**) — government health line. Operates 24/7, free of charge. A doctor/nurse will determine what to do over the phone:
c8f56c4551acfb59c687d7b45e7147af3bf97008: 🟢 Not urgent → book an appointment with a family doctor at the health centre
2758f1f7a0fa69d78b16a2ed7a6de58896735422: 🟡 Need help, but not right now → private clinic / non-urgent atendimento
40e9a2f35bfe4d2561848d8af6169f75881c80bc: 🟠 Urgent, but not emergency → special urgência (paediatric, child, gynaecological)
3215fc02f4f543bb8538bcc62f179980ae07d864: 🔴 Emergency → call INEM (112) or go to urgência
e9431e9725c9bd380f2f371baa907a1e510a9b2b: SNS 24 app: chat with a doctor, video call — no waiting for a telephone operator
4c996f1cf195f48cc88a801ce6cd5327c5b2e70d: 💡 SNS 24 is often less busy than urgência — 15 minutes for diagnosis
fe817b2328f706f4b7e7d8b28e57380e9eeb05e6: 🎨 Manchester Triage System — how you will be assessed
6c7e6c163918167f71a50a83a867625f0f7cfe22: Upon arrival at urgência, you will first be sorted by the enfermeiro triagem — a 3-5 minute questionnaire → a colour will be assigned.
28f412b67e66b63db6fae482e5c67e89140702e3: 🔴 **Vermelho (red) — EMERGÊNCIA**: treatment **immediate**. Cardiac arrest, stroke, severe injury, loss of consciousness
da765aed613a81ec00c168e69268d3753e7fdb8f: 🟠 **Laranja (orange) — MUITO URGENTE**: within **≤ 10 min**. Severe chest pain, difficulty breathing, major bleeding
c63537373615fb9075f24eec0a59089773d6a792: 🟡 **Amarelo (yellow) — URGENTE**: within **≤ 60 min**. Moderate pain, fever with deterioration, broken limb
0b538a242fa4663220bc3478b977dd8670449b6d: 🟢 **Verde (green) — POUCO URGENTE**: within **≤ 120 min**. Wound without bleeding, moderate pain, nausea
d09bc00f67ce0100fa3b96407ae7679b0f3637ec: 🔵 **Azul (blue) — NÃO URGENTE**: within **≤ 240 min** (4 hours!). Symptoms ≤ 7 days, mild cold, slight pain
8d6e452022ed9aee3a77fd53470aeaf20d7582a0: ⚪ **Branco** — does not require urgent care (should go to family doctor) — sent home
462843e4b42f72c5afc3146bc0dbb1257890aff4: 80% of patients in PT arrive with yellow/green/blue — and wait 2-4 hours. If you are blue — it’s more realistic to book an appointment at centro de saúde or go to a private clinic.
1cbf0bdc5bdeac50bad5c99f25fb2a9a7cb0775d: Health centres have dedicated **Atendimento Complementar** for situations that do not require hospitalisation
43e1ea631a6411ce8c38835cda33caacaa357a24: They operate until 20:00 in some centres, some are 24/7 (Cascais, Almada)
cb7370b8d13be339d372ded1034d59a713b58518: Appointment through SNS 24 (call/app) — arrive at your scheduled slot
331e74159905b95b89bc54271860baac41685169: In Lisbon: Hospital Beatriz Ângelo (Loures) — usually faster than Santa Maria
08e61404daabb46ed2883025d70535d708cade24: Hospital de Cascais (if you live in Cascais/Estoril) — often quieter
e079fcf1f71a4842e5251362d1ff7e9355b80ee3: Porto: Hospital de São João — 4-6 hours; Hospital de Pedro Hispano (Matosinhos) — sometimes 2-3 hours
9d15cf7763383bd6631b3a937806ded10c3fd196: 📱 **App “Tempos de Espera SNS”** — real waiting times for all hospitals!
0fb3ef1e1c8ab9744c0dd51405a17ea6ee103e6c: 💡 Check before heading out — go to a less busy one

a01de77bdd5c18946e3cafa1f36149c19cd244cd: Cost: €20-80 per visit (no insurance), €0-15 co-payment with private insurance
e0c260c9717ca860457f60dcc54f2f9f97c453c3: Network: **CUF, Lusíadas, Luz, Trofa, Hospital da Misericórdia**
a65af65234b9692d2695c0068bba5cdabea33e48: Open 24/7 in large centres, from 8:00 to 24:00 in smaller ones

21b9bb5482ecb3e92168d35f962516c6a874d05a: **Gynaecological / Obstetric**: Maternidade Alfredo da Costa (Lisbon), Maternidade Júlio Dinis (Porto)

ddbcdb1d6b4249264d108229a75279d286b43c30: União de facto — legal status of cohabitation without marriage, recognised after 2 years of cohabitation per Lei 7/2001. Grants most rights of spouses: joint tax filing, inheritance, SNS beneficiary, right to family reunification, spouse's citizenship after 3 years. Registered at the Conservatória do Registo Civil for €10 + 2 witnesses or through a certificate from the Junta de Freguesia. Applicable for both hetero and same-sex couples.

c6dc18ae5fd80dd405ecd1da19f7e1a02cb2f2e1: Portugal — the world capital of surfing. Nazaré — records for the biggest waves (30+ m). Ericeira — the only World Surf Reserve in Europe. Peniche, Sagres, Aljezur — legendary spots. Schools from €30-60/lesson + €15-25 rental, full weekend camp ~€150-350. Peak season: September-April for big waves, May-August for beginners. Any level from beginner to world-class.

c1619f85dc4488e219a3d6bcef2b4ff4fb9f0ca8: The European Health Insurance Card (CESD), also known as EHIC, provides access to public healthcare in all EU countries, EEA, Switzerland, and the UK under the same conditions as local residents. Free, valid for 5 years. Issued to residents of PT with Segurança Social (NISS) through SS Direta in 10 minutes. The plastic card arrives by post in 10-14 days, but a temporary certificate can be downloaded as a PDF immediately.
42abb711cb92e2992a1a860360c731f7ed336712: Residents of Portugal with an active NISS (employed, self-employed, pensioner, unemployed on benefits)

9cf1d7da91d1065f3406af30b4cadaac134d3113: Erasmus students — through their university's SS
16a725e82c0751b7615c9457c2ecf6d2047cf8a1: EU citizens receiving a pension in PT — through Segurança Social
fec746cdc688ea155b10819ad56e1377989b4aa6: ❌ Tourists / non-residents — no, they need private travel insurance
250705a69817d5b0bc790c8cfbd12d137b98a42c: CESD operates in 31 countries: 27 EU + Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Switzerland, United Kingdom (under the Brexit agreement, from 2021 UK-EHIC / GHIC works mutually).

98966b22e727abd63fa55e5ca726815dac0ed90e: ✅ Check-ups for chronic conditions (dialysis, chemotherapy) — to be agreed in advance

4a8248b9a208ec8da18f0008df8efbd19e6df1f9: CESD complements, but does not replace travel insurance. For a comfortable trip (repatriation, private clinics, luggage), still purchase a policy for €10-30 from an insurer.

7834825702ef22cbb1abc24d4dddc15b8a98fc35: Paediatrician in Portugal — SNS, private, what to expect
2984c3e8999bc48ce368801fede53443584ae270: In the SNS, each child is assigned a **médico de família** (not necessarily a paediatrician — usually a family doctor). Scheduled check-ups are on the timetable (Saúde Infantil): 1, 2, 4, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 24 months, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 13, 15, 18 years — free of charge. Specialist paediatricians (pediatria) — via referral from a family doctor or private clinics (Lusíadas, CUF, Luz) €60-120/visit without insurance. Children's emergency: Hospital Dona Estefânia (Lisbon), Hospital de São João Pediatric (Porto). Scheduling: SNS 24 app or through the health centre.
649fff86bf29400d51fc309a07b5e88a23ad1fdc: An important difference from Russia/Brazil: in Portugal, children are monitored by a **médico de família** (family doctor) — not by a paediatrician as a separate doctor. Paediatricians are specialists for complex cases.

3baf32028fb4fb3c64fed9483596297db03d4d58: ⚠️ If your health centre has no available doctors — you will be placed on a wait-list. In 2025, approximately 800,000 children in Portugal will be without a family doctor.

99ad6680747b461d9ad4805fca35b810f51c3ca7: Social assistance in Portugal — RSI, subsidies, benefits
040d9f342c638d42564bbc5854b8bae98dd71dd0: Portugal offers a wide range of social benefits to residents: **RSI** (rendimento social de inserção) — minimum for incomes <€242/month; **Subsídio de desemprego** (unemployment benefit) — 65% of the average salary for up to 12-30 months; **Subsídio de habitação** (housing subsidy) — up to €200/month; **Tarifa Social Energia** — 33% discount on electricity; **Social Housing** (habitação social) — rent based on income. Requirements: NISS, minimum SS contribution period (depends on the benefit), legal residence.
7f5aee6613d5a4277ed5a51313170e548f1d91a0: RSI — basic benefit for individuals/families with income below the minimum. The state guarantees a minimum income (social contract 'you provide work, we provide basic income').
1862e15ac0f2cadebce269e0d5fbe89073296ce4: 📊 **Amount**: ~€242.30/month for the first adult (2026, linked to SS reference value). +50% for a spouse (~€121), +30% for a child
f00434c381643209cf8722d37a316bf0ba75dd4d: 🧾 **Conditions**: total family income < threshold amount; no significant assets; willingness to work (there is an obligation to seek work through IEFP)
57bd3547d60a2b7e8965a5d2251e7f56a8fb4502: 📅 **Duration**: 12 months, renewable each year if conditions are met
6deb874d06b0b0dc19f7f109d31c7009517f41ab: 👨‍👩‍👧 **For foreigners**: legal residence in PT ≥ 1 year (3 years for non-EU). Exception: refugees, TPD — from the moment of obtaining status
1ef35f29798165734627ea7280028a689939fa43: 📍 **Application**: SS Direta → 'Prestações' → 'Rendimento Social de Inserção', or at the ISS office
d0808e83196ce747019a78bc0afe785eee6a8b80: 📋 **Obligations**: registration with IEFP as a job seeker, attending mandatory courses/interviews, not refusing 'suitable' work
0d10e77d49dd6e19d40fd93c8f2abe813f43e091: RSI is incompatible with: subsídio de desemprego, full-time work (>RSI threshold), significant assets (>4× RSI in the bank). Non-compliance with rules = loss for 2 years.
0615e5dd3cdf18be45c21c5e194c3671e3de5bbb: ≥ 360 days of contributions to Seg. Social in the last 24 months
88cf16721761532bc48339eaa9999d265578a94b: Dismissed **not through their own fault** (art. 381, 387 Código do Trabalho) — redundancy, bankruptcy, extinction
982feacf55b85649630019c5647881e33b30072a: ❌ Voluntary dismissal — not granted (exception: severe personal circumstances)
8572c69ddfebc980f8b252751bb4f6f0c1ed45d0: ❌ Self-employed (recibos verdes) without cessação de atividade — not covered
194ad4f966d058afa4b1f2cf7cbe97f7b39371a0: Must be disponível trabalhar + registered with IEFP
f131ee1942238c38116711c672b791c90df6cbda: **65% of the average salary** of the last 12 months (brutto), cap ~ 2.5× IAS = €1,342/month (2026)
f086d01eb36c2cfc380b034e1c8a719b0dd5edbb: After expiration: Subsídio Social de Desemprego (80% RSI, 6-18 months additional) if family income is low
1669e3de74bae5f869379fb017c51c8521e9c898: **Step 1**: registration at Centro de Emprego (IEFP) as unemployed
99536c9cbacf715b6be47fc4e168ac034811573e: **Step 2**: on SS Direta — «Prestações» → «Subsídio de desemprego» → fill in
4e9101acb58efb9135bf906b7af41c539d6da518: Or: through the ISS office with documents (contract, termination letter)
b03bb16f0b96471eb25620e695c18682f5c2ee20: ⏱️ Processing time: 30-45 days, payments are backdated to the date of dismissal
08666fb181d8edd0764ab38b4f165b73dce05072: **Porta 65 Jovem**: for youth aged 18-35 — up to €200/month rental subsidy
2c1e682c655e8785720783a07575b568f512dfab: **Apoio ao Arrendamento** (IHRU): for low-income families, up to 50% of rent
73a01e4ad5bd6ca9b8e90c8b006f4cdc3ed7a7e3: Requirements: income ≤ 4× IAS family, rental contract
08d7f408e770a35a123b496494e99ad3315694a3: Renting an apartment from the municipality based on the formula (income × %)
e58c75b126a540cf19373798774faa0156f8938e: Rate: usually €20-80/month (depends on income and family size)
1d45be03793f6a8309f354e8cc283cbbd12ba00c: Queue: 3-10 years in Lisbon / Porto, faster in regions
78da77c7e57076931075424d423d87010bc469cc: Submission: at the Câmara Municipal in your place of residence
1f3b95980c8f4ec56a81dfbc8ee209038341a3e2: Priority: multigenerational families, disabled individuals, pensioners, victims of domestic violence
37a9871bcb5cf883dd6b9495e1201ee67d6a806b: Programme for the working class: rent 20-30% below market rate in specially designated buildings. Lotação through SIGA, budget from municipalities.
5a06c7a9c336dc388abd3be219e54f902bcdc55f: Automatically applied if you are in one of the categories:
