e7bdf158a210d26167524e17adec5c2b5b97c4e9: 🧒 **Abono de Família**: for children < 18 / 24 student / 27 disabled. €40-190/month depending on the tier (see separate guide)
8a3a182d7a1ca7f61c548a0653fcbe8b586ffc13: 👶 **Subsídio Parental**: 120-180 days of maternity leave 100% / 90% of salary
2ed2bd15ed46087827ac86abdea9cfdae139e022: 🎒 **Apoio às despesas escolares**: discount on textbooks, materials — ISS.gov.pt
34c52ddc2b36d0598e087a959cc36b6e60deef2c: ♿ **Complemento por Dependência**: for disabled individuals and those in need of care — up to €100-200/month
8044ee753f839cd1db9e85a97406428a7c09ee8c: 👵 **Complemento Solidário para Idosos (CSI)**: for pensioners with income < IAS
378f83713baea2f7fdf68e3b2bc73838138fb6fe: 🧑‍⚕️ **Isenção de taxas moderadoras**: automatically for benefit receivers + pregnant women, children < 18, pensioners > 65 with low income

cb8726d6131db5546a94aff46328bca24d3c55d2: 2. Gather: CC/residence permit, NISS, IBAN, proof of income (3-12 months), family composition, dependent documents
2589ce06b1eb7f001ab37e33b59ec2b676f466ec: 5. If approved — automatic payments to IBAN monthly

81d487895ddd446d62a83eae2fd2b0124a3988a1: Vaccinations in Portugal — PNV, children, adults, travel
6d3f94f17add87277c35359c9633a3b18c4b7b8c: PNV (Plano Nacional de Vacinação) — a government programme, free in SNS for residents with utente. Children receive a set of mandatory vaccinations according to the schedule (0-18 years). For adults: Tdap (tetanus) every 10 years, flu annually for those aged 65+ and risk groups, COVID booster as recommended by DGS. Travel vaccines (malaria, Hep A, yellow fever) — available at private travel medicine centres or CINAP.
1d052a74e83845c171635f451e3a7bc0f536d421: PNV (Plano Nacional de Vacinação) — a government vaccination schedule, approved by DGS annually. The latest edition is for 2024.
33c47bd42c6fb72fc673633cb03307c94d9460e8: 💊 Administered at the health centre in your area (Centro de Saúde)
6da07c1b0ed67295d93e1b21b29bc4ae8aa1db9d: 📱 Track: e-Boletim de Vacinas in the SNS 24 app
8c287b341153d8ecfbe9434c82488d53f3557ebe: ⚠️ Private clinics — the same vaccines for a fee, €20-80 per visit
260f5c4e6e42af24fca2664bf173310fb389b3b2: 10 years (girls): HPV — protection against cervical cancer
383878aceefe70f5910903c9dde3be512354047f: Some private schools require a boletim de vacinas (vaccination booklet) upon enrolment. In public schools, the health centre checks and notifies if vaccinations are overdue.
5d55ab2140f40e190e6a3dfced0a54d3f7510b50: 💉 **Flu**: annually from October for those aged 65+, pregnant women, and medical risk groups. For others — paid €15-25
577efed4e74d50447600a6e46f928cdcf33c9233: 💉 **COVID booster**: as recommended by DGS. 2024-2026 — usually for those aged 65+ and risk groups
edae78ab7823f452b11a627c586a683e7080da66: 💉 **Hep B**: if not received in childhood (before 1995 — not included in PNV). Series 0/1/6 months
625849c8ffc4d64c3c6964df8fcaa8b1251b90ae: 💉 **Shingles**: recommended for those aged 60+, not in PNV (paid, €150-200 per dose)
78f080fe547abf0ddd563562b4d849e0ad7685bd: 💉 **HPV for adults**: available up to age 45 for a fee (3 doses × €180)
38d2b082be83f58cb002ec7e0f96dbe514e098dc: 💉 **Pneumococcal PPSV23**: for those aged 65+ or with chronic conditions
17dece6628f40543d1957bf1937c0ba355465f7f: Vaccinations before travelling to tropical regions / Asia / Africa.
64721fbd737ef8cf6f5c9b5b5b4bc3a86f5a983b: **CINAP** (Travel Medicine Consultation) — public consultations in Lisbon, Porto, Coimbra. By appointment, free consultation + paid vaccines
77b9123ef70b69483fdd925abb239aae2ca20c0c: **Lusíadas, CUF, Luz clinics** — travel medicine department
88becec84eab0fb623b6ddd08fda936a91efceb5: **SNS** — Yellow Fever vaccinations are only done at specialised centres (Lisboa Hospital CUF Infante Santo, Porto Hospital de São João)
2c29bc2535d355c24d5f14f9d753bce0c9332b46: **Yellow Fever** — 1 dose = lifetime, required for several countries in Africa and South America. €40-60 + certificate
2a98a034f0d7a227a12c067d347d763fc79a08cf: **Hep A**: for India, Africa, South America — €40-60 × 2 doses
0dc9dcde0ed6cc7a91dedff3a60df10d1c465f87: **Rabies**: for zoo workers, long treks — €60 × 3 doses
d894ffb0635006b6103e85c3b72738e560fba0db: **Malaria**: not a vaccine, prevention with tablets (doxycycline, Malarone) — €30-100/course
d33f78b0fb2be9e52897ec1e04ecc28d16b7e896: Since 2015, the boletim is electronic in the SNS 24 app

9156c4b614636edcfefc49125326d7e5648f8bbb: 📋 Bring the boletim de vacinas from your home country (translation is advisable, but often PT doctors can read it in Latin script)

12ce4661b15ebb6bd1fee28a233a1f5225c532eb: Seguro multirriscos habitação — home insurance. **Mandatory for homeowners with a mortgage** (required by the bank) and **for condominiums** (common). For tenants — not mandatory, but recommended (seguro de recheio, €50-150/year). Covers: fire, flooding, theft, civil liability (€50-300k), electronics, repairs. Best insurers 2026: Fidelidade, Generali Tranquilidade, Allianz, Zurich, Liberty. Annual cost: €150-500 for a 70-100 m² apartment depending on coverage.

6f0096a961464a4c6cd6614319882031a2f7e06c: Portugal — a country of quiet daily protocol, but neighbours can be different. Noise rules: Regulamento Geral do Ruído (DL 9/2007) — quiet period 22:00-08:00 (in some regions 23:00-07:00). Where to complain: GNR/PSP (one-off) → Câmara Municipal (persistent violation) → tribunal (if everything else fails). Noise fines: €200-€2500 for individuals. In a condominium — the general assembly adopts internal rules, assembleia at least once a year. Mandatory Fundo Comum de Reserva.

904075b8fd58337c634866eef28f44491f15c494: Overstay is a serious violation. For exceeding 90 days of Schengen / visa / residence permit, fines of €80–700 apply, entry in the Schengen Information System (SIS II), risk of refusal for extensions and future visas for 1-5 years, in severe cases — entry ban to Schengen. The worst is cumulative overstay: the longer you are out of status, the lower the chance of legalising back.
07233e3bdcc5b6ffe0c27d611df35b962e6fe670: Visa-free tourists (BR, US, UK, CA, ...) — 90 days in any 180 days in the Schengen Area

7ff6f17164e2a45eaebbe16fc0a5ef820c434be9: Visa D allows 4 months for entry and meeting with AIMA. If you missed the AIMA agendamento and the visa has expired — you are in overstay even while in PT.

e844473430341a59979f01c6da2aaada7e172b97: Overstay 90+ days: fine €300–700 + possible entry ban

1336aec98161ddf4781917009c910776c42148ca: Your willingness to leave voluntarily and pay — mitigates the fine
a5d5928221d9726ab3ba82bbff4aafcbbe34d08d: Overstay blocks: new D visas (AIMA checks history), applications for residence permit from within the country (under art. 88/89 legal entry was required — after DL 37-A/2024 option 88.2 is closed, 89.2 — only under transitional regime). There are three ways out of overstay: (1) voluntary departure + payment of fine + new visa from the consulate; (2) applying for CPLP status/refugee/humanitarian status; (3) risk: deportation by court decision.

db35a20c8049b288c960ec924f7e3929f69d13ef: Brazilians, Angolans, Mozambicans, etc. can apply for a residence permit under CPLP while in PT, even with an overstay. AIMA prefers to grant permission instead of deportation if there is a contrato / funds.

e905a342f6624822b9dc72b7821023f1c98b2ac8: A valid marriage / registered união de facto grants the right to reagrupamento familiar or residence permit as an EU citizen family member. Overstay does not block this, but AIMA may require the fine to be paid first.

14a8da88409e6d693915d71bfe9221396c41825f: Medical treatment, pregnancy, asylum, family circumstances. Through advogado → AIMA / court. Long, complicated, requires justification.

0464725a09f9a5e26c513ca01cc01ff3fa55a022: Permanent residency in Portugal (art. 125) and EU Long-Term Resident
568718035d36cdc1e5e6919a41bfd82e7098c002: After 5 years of legal residence, you can apply for **Autorização de Residência Permanente** (art. 125 Lei 23/2007) — does not expire, renewal every 10 years. Alternative: **EU Long-Term Resident** (DL 29/2012 transposes Directive 2003/109) — grants the right to live and work freely in any EU country. Both — without the need to obtain citizenship. Requirements: stable income ≥ SMN, health insurance, A2 Portuguese (art. 7), no criminal record. Application to AIMA.

c0e6a009e88cfe6a4ba2b987b9270881dfd7eb99: Until 2022, crypto was not taxed for individuals — a 'tax haven'. From 2023 (OE 2023), the following was introduced: **28% tax on profits for sales within <1 year**; **0% for ≥365 days of holding** (capital gains long-term); **staking, mining, professional trading** — taxed as operational income. IVA 0% on exchanges. Declaration in IRS via Anexo G and category E for staking. IFICI does not cover crypto. Exchanges and P2P — available, major banks accept transfers from Bitstamp, Kraken, Bitfinex with AML checks.

This distinguishes PT from other jurisdictions (e.g. Switzerland is fully crypto-friendly), but the regime with 365+ days holding = 0% — is still one of the best in the EU.
c340eab0540b63d7c661d2ac42e8a2cf67137f83: 📅 For long-term 0% regime: use **tax-loss harvesting** only considering the 365-day rule. Do not swap unnecessarily.

5d26506d66ffaf682da89462f2a9fff27840de55: Cromeleque dos Almendres — a megalithic complex 15 km west of Évora. Older than Stonehenge by 2000 years (built around 6000-5000 BC). 95 granite menhirs arranged in circles and ovals, aligned with the solstices. One of the largest in Europe. Free access, open 24/7, accessible only by car.
10293f249deb2306a91f20c1cc603d147983b523: Cromlech (circular group of standing stones) from the Neolithic era, built between 6000 and 3000 BC in stages (4 construction phases). Of the 95 remaining menhirs, some reach 3 m in height. Many are decorated with carvings — solar symbols, anthropomorphic figures, spirals.
16f630a7304fb4b224e830048b2537bb6e189d34: The orientation of the stones is related to astronomical events — especially the sunrise at the equinox and solstice. It is believed to have been a cult site and/or observatory.
d725b4bb19abfa47db8075d51a32e753806ef38d: 🚗 By car from Évora — 15-20 minutes (15 km west)
7d417aa22d9eb2b8458f4fce07a90efcf4c5b694: 📍 Coordinates: 38.5579°N, 8.0606°W, enter "Cromeleque dos Almendres" in Google Maps
92bd0ced1c18159c1f29cda3de0622d2cb35a208: 🛣️ The last 4 km is a dirt road through a cork forest. Passable for cars in dry weather; after rain — only for 4x4 vehicles
99f4218a584dbab659a22dd7db3824b5bf9dc88a: 🚴 By bike from Évora takes 1 hour on the roads
1f3eb7b7057d9b75ad1695a16a5646e8929b6f6b: 🚶 From Évora ~3 hours on foot, there is a route along tracks; no water supply
04f7f964f58c3f886caa60927be37620da3668bb: Two intersecting circles of 95 menhirs (diameter 60-80 m)
0295de6cf431cd50905e80898c2ca352e7b05be6: The engravings are explained on information boards (EN/PT)
0c5b49f5bf87888c07357c263b259b3cb5205048: Nearby — Menhir dos Almendres (a solitary 4.5-metre menhir, 1.4 km to the north)
c4b95781c498fda0f39824d8d682fa6b9262d43f: Carries — dolmens (tombs) scattered around the area (Anta Grande do Zambujeiro — the largest in the EU, 10 km to the south)
b78017d6ba18bf60fcf6fdf074dfba6c5123ff97: ⏰ Best time: dawn (summer 6:00-7:00) — the stones are illuminated, no tourists
6d46b35426485367416548111f0b0e93c2525821: 🌅 On the equinox (March, September) and solstice (June, December) — unofficial gatherings of esotericists and archaeologists
b641426f8f175db5c877c969bece444a988e3ed4: What to include in the trip
59a026f2234635d33e5449a5e92aec28958aa365: 🏛️ **Évora** (15 km) — Roman temple of Diana, Capela dos Ossos (Chapel of Bones), medieval cathedral — 1 day minimum
e6da43e3253afd85425d64b7f0eaf1d30b1a6963: ⚱️ **Anta Grande do Zambujeiro** — one of the largest dolmens in Europe, 10 km south of the cromlech
4eeedefea52c61b019761608c66fb18bc01ed9a6: 🥘 **Lunch in Évora** — traditional alentejano: migas, açorda, black pig
b3e1bb4baff6e7d45799318ea8ea9d7931767d78: 🍷 **Wineries in Alentejo** — Cartuxa, Herdade do Esporão — tastings €15-25
9ed2e7d20e8d5075819a8d81710cd66ac7bb1d38: 💧 Take 2+ litres of water — there is no water or café on site
1862820ddf28e553e7d23f47a1f94bd5f5794bd9: 🌞 The sun is very strong, especially from May to September — hat, cream
d99df72d96202bde84e8dacf9b0d3565c38cbf1b: 🐝 In spring, there are many bees (cork trees are in bloom)
b72cc42a95e02413481d15206e63016f86cf5cc8: 📸 Photo: morning or evening light is best
a80bc6b89de2dde31a4579afa835f83df18f0c55: 🌿 Early spring — a carpet of wildflowers around (March-April)
c7976ad9f8219526228795dd198ba9f58670773d: ♿ Wheelchair access is limited — the path is uneven and rocky in places
8838956fcd9fdbd5f94d80d0e82bf3ceafb4fc54: 🚫 Do not climb on the stones, do not move them, do not write on them — protected monument
dcae23af741ae4ad2bade90d57c623b0dfe2e4a4: 🌙 Night visits are safe, but the road is unpaved — better during the day
f0b3c963b8dfe1afabc4977fa488f990df1223c1: Azores and Madeira — subsidies for residents + how to buy
f75b53877dce2d4eabb2f05a9a3a1f3a9373c502: PT residents receive the **‘Social Territorial de Passageiros’** (STP) subsidy for flights to the Azores and Madeira — up to €134 reimbursement per ticket (2026). This applies to PT residents with NIF + NISS. They purchase a full ticket, then claim the subsidy through SATA Azores / TAP / Ryanair (some routes). Application: online at sata.pt. Compensated for up to 4 trips/year. For residents of the islands — even more subsidies through Governo Regional.
4a9b9ca593aa0b2a908d039debce731c7066be30: A flight subsidy programme between mainland Portugal and the autonomous regions (Azores, Madeira) — compensates for the price difference between the mainland and the islands.
7df50f3b9c98eb93d057adda6fc78d38a697ad27: ✅ Who is eligible: **residents of Portugal** with NIF (foreigners with a residence permit — also!)
f6549f8920260e1fcbd1e7534f701c115710931e: ✅ **Residents mainland → islands** (as tourists): lower subsidy, depending on the route
cc1c935af8dd4a2ef0243d2343b6b8eb1d4c4562: ✅ **Max 4 trips per year** for residents of the islands; 2 for tourists
6ddd0d6c8cf74c42940c73446846958f5c8207d4: ❌ Not applicable for: business trips (if paid by a company), non-residents
6d02f7a0204e769dde19bc115468408bfcda7955: 💰 Example: Lisbon-Ponta Delgada ticket €210 → subsidy €80 → net cost €130
ef27adef234c8e9758cb7c0b8ce22576eec95521: Option A — built-in at purchase (SATA, TAP)
bcbe28ef4766bce1b16396bbc53ad8d24d601005: On sata.pt or flytap.pt — enter your NIF when booking
c46af6b59acb60eb0aa54b81b532aacd22a8b6b6: The system automatically applies the 'residente' tariff
133bb2bcaabcbe9c4159b1a03a7ed8dd0cd57f19: Option B — refund after the trip (Ryanair, others)
4d6da6cc8beafad1371974588adf3febcd41163c: Bought a full-price ticket without NIF
8a809860530a9022c414074461587432360f85f5: After the trip — application on sata.pt → 'Bonificação STP'
d724c792e604f0a5fcfe2a0e1f6af0e384b3c6ae: Attach: boarding passes, invoice, atestado de residência
d17e8ad3f8585716dec6f72604ada62631cd10c3: Refund period: up to 30 days after the flight
66e56b8f9be12637ced159246b7a89c40f87cb81: Bureaucratic, but works for international carriers
b6b673fdaa92878415b40aac614deae028f2eb23: Atestado de residência (if requested; usually just NIF in the system is sufficient)
d9a82ece608c029efc97532c8fd68668a7328a48: Boarding pass both ways (for option B)
4a4bb794d9b32c2592f223f8c6437bbcd4e5cbfd: 🏝️ For residents of the Azores / Madeira
97068205d60ea96c3cbe5eabdd1c6a2314e8a84a: If you live on the islands, you receive additional benefits from the Governo Regional.
01bb1d3b8c817b1e135fdaccf7171431e4f426f5: **Super-subsidy** through SATA for residents of the Azores: up to 80% ticket cost refund
739b88ecd6788a4c9d9efbe43d7f35144b9a1b11: Additional subsidy for students / medical cases — up to 100%
55e4494f059e9846bc3ad97d9c34eecac15d0b8c: Application through srprorainol.azores.gov.pt or municipal offices
ab11a931bdd8d3b6131b64d748e32ed2588ab83e: Similar programme from the Governo Regional da Madeira
85a7f17cd5e0b364952e836bcd4c175da4cecc41: Serves: TAP, easyJet, SATA Madeira routes
42546dd0e2295f535e548858e9a28f952e52b180: Beach island 30 minutes flight from Funchal
c7fb34c3e64d4948947a6a685f42188e5c91e370: **Porto Santo Line** — ferry company (3 hours, €50-80 round-trip, 30% discount for residents)
371aa9c6ff8014231055ef1c799b12c3f976c920: **SATA / TAP** — 10-15 flights per week, €35-60 one-way (for residents)
c86b2d4cb8311f9a097e7528975826c73dad4265: In season (June-September) — crowded, book tickets 2-3 months in advance
0afaca056048208727e7ff9227dfaa7817f6bca7: **SATA Air Açores** — the only carrier between the islands
9a72a37c8d6320b90378ef7f139ab63439944bed: Residents: super-tariff €35-60 one-way between neighbouring islands
db32c7f336761c5b8e809a6ce3442f0bac0b8397: Routes: Ponta Delgada ↔ Terceira, Pico, Faial, Corvo, Flores
5e961306790dcc864e44088422d08a186717db00: ⚠️ Weather: flight cancellations are frequent in winter — buy flexible fare
2205ddd29a43787324d7155805ecef522563dcd3: Atlânticoline ferries — an alternative, cheaper, 40-90 minutes between neighbouring islands
f4595edacdb71e03b83081ab45f982a9ed17717f: 💡 Tips for tourists in the Azores / Madeira
4bf4159c3f1c2267fbd842a0b11d022ccc2420ca: 📅 **Season**: May-October is the best — warm, no storms. But high season = high prices
e63358e43c52606ca0121a6f61a2d9f579de16cb: 🚗 **Car rental**: essential for activities — the islands cannot be reached by bus; €30-60/day
d99980369ed40f59590dd530759b772fb6a78493: 🏨 **Accommodation**: local AL (Airbnb) cheaper than hotels, often with a view
51a7915f4d7ec1a82dadac39425b3850325974b4: 🥾 **Trekking**: Azores — 8 islands, each with its own trails. Madeira — levadas (water channels) are a must-experience
d29a74ac6726cbdc9a710bfa8af896b2c297ffe3: 🍷 **Wine**: Madeira wine, Pico vineyards — UNESCO heritage
0a5c8f3770373b8a4c25522ce9a88c55bc0db941: 🏖️ **Beaches**: Porto Santo (Madeira) — the only sandy one in the region; Azores — volcanic, black
f30d8932a6bf3e5188a6ef4d42668e7f4be7149d: Berlengas — nature reserve and fortress island
6eb133393d848ece4b1f7811b53902e4a1ee4f43: Arquipélago das Berlengas — a group of granite islands 10 km from Peniche. A UNESCO biosphere reserve. The main island, Berlenga Grande, is open from May to September, with 550 visitors per day (booking is mandatory). On the island: fort São João Baptista (17th century, overnight stays in the 'cells' are possible), beaches, diving, trekking, sea caves. 45 minutes by fast boat, 1 hour 15 minutes by regular boat.
7f266b8bf6a8311671f7a1c3ffe2aa0d710dc94b: Booking for the visit is mandatory
82831b65f21bb96634a531e805edcde28497b1f8: Since 2011, ICNF (Instituto da Conservação da Natureza e das Florestas) has limited access — 550 people per day on Berlenga Grande, to preserve the ecosystem.
b9224795095f185c3bd4bf9726a8183aecf1a964: berlengasreservas.icnf.pt → select the date and number of people. Registration (without payment) opens ≥ 30 days before the visit. For July-August, book on the opening day of the window, as spots fill up within hours.
027b081f3555321382a8c607a018f4e9611c0d7a: Feeling Berlenga, Viamar, Julius Berlengas — the main operators (available at Peniche port)
804085787e4fbc844fa9f7c0c4db2a46580ba2bb: Fast boat: €22-28 round-trip, 45 minutes one way
de4aa0bad15ac734a065ec04c8c7ca3e715bf00d: In case of storms, trips are cancelled — you will be refunded or rescheduled
d8319764f01966f28ea89462804f3eca4e5bc5df: 3. On the day of the trip
9ecb9ac11e2dee1bed42413451d17044c4cb66db: Porto de Recreio pier (there's a large car park nearby)
17eb0bcc49449de155636e5f663743854b3c27a2: Show ICNF reservation on paper or from your phone
4cbcabdc135fb466f3e08eb3697ca1e7579f9c8a: Buy sunscreen, water, snacks in Peniche (expensive and limited on the island)
8043d25a12e877f88180bada1cf9de42f7bfafaa: What to see on Berlenga Grande
c1ab56e1413f646471063ca814ffe8e2b4d7afba: Built in 1651 on a separate islet, connected to the main island by a stone bridge. You can stay overnight in the fort's guesthouse (from €40/night, book 6 months in advance). Sunset photos with the bridge are the archipelago's postcard.
603e74c26aeef0fffb63336a266df483e231153c: The only sandy beach. Clear water, 18-22°C in season. Diving and snorkelling — crystal clear water, lots of fish.
720035172f84a78d9d8a4a332186f353e3c15234: Tour on a local boat (€10-15, 45 min): they circle the island, visiting Gruta Azul, Cova do Sonho, and the huge tunnel Furado Grande. Book with fishermen right on the beach.
a257c0c3dd1ba3d26baebf3d0a17394ea05b2537: The only path from the pier to the fort is 1.5 km on foot, a trail over granite cliffs. There is no transport on the island. Routes: to the Duque de Bragança lighthouse (summit, 85 m), to the Farol (lighthouse), circular trail 3 km.
f51d1010093e792c78d3d4a67ac4585d8978f567: 🏰 Forte São João Baptista — guesthouse in former monastic cells. €40-60/night. Book 5 months in advance.
75a412aef323b160ceb4f025d0c65a62a92203c3: 🏕️ Campismo da Berlenga — tent camping. €10-15/night + landing fee. You need to register separately at the ICNF (10 spots/night)
b34f5da78970c858d1fd0647c5553f830cf64f9d: 🐟 There is one restaurant, Mar e Sol (salted fish, sea urchins). €15-25/person
3633e3d43f78fa31ce48c29a5564cd2864962bb3: ❌ No shops, no ATM, no mobile signal (occasionally Vodafone works)
e4a0c7088bb4265b786d138f4fc589d5e1fc5ff7: ⚡ Electricity only from the fort's solar panels — from 18:00 to 23:00
1229a6779434b3ad4882147f28ee5a2e2ceceb2f: When is the best time to go
7995f211446200b2ddc98651a2d3f990f664f7be: Season: mid-May to end of September. Off-season, boats do not operate
96d1e44541a8500539cc5f86edd8f6a98478564b: Weather: July-August — warm, but many tourists. May/June and September — ideal
d4cdc23b6e1e48ca161e3403955dd05018a4757d: Storms in spring = frequent cancellations. Check the forecast for Peniche
97e6de3d08fb01ba622fafe431e99f27304c74c3: The wind is often strong — take a windbreaker even in the heat
f56ab6916ef97c7318bb1a29dfee1c7ff6a8ac02: ☀️ A day on Berlengas = 5-6 hours (arrival at 11:00, return at 16:00-17:00)
1fbe72d51c9a2db531517fa68a3ebc83f6aca1c4: ⚠️ Take a sea sickness tablet (Vertigin, Dramamine) 30 minutes before — the boat rocks
2d5ee459f6724e3df5f6f9d1999c1ec40011918e: 🐟 Fishing on the island is prohibited (nature reserve)
b6dc51c4297538a24929e6478ab9e0b08083fd08: 🐛 There are seagulls — they behave aggressively if food is open; do not feed them
3b77bcba12a08d1fbc75ecbd0cdaec1594d52091: 🏊 Swim only in designated areas — dangerous currents beyond
6f4276a2ad79ef68ae1e54b5ceb8e87b557d1051: 📷 Phone in a plastic zip-lock — sea spray

65a1d83e8bc372d792b166b11ea677d2021270bf: Hiking and eco-trails in Portugal — from weekend to multi-day trips
511dfee117611826d6b43aa1ce97e7fd98f19ee7: Portugal has over 300 marked trails (PR, GR, levadas). The most popular are: Levadas in Madeira (350 km), Rota Vicentina in SW Alentejo (450 km), PR trails in Serra da Estrela, Gerês, Açores. Short day hikes (3-15 km, PR) are available in every natural park; multi-day GR trails are marked red-white, with accommodation in albergues and rural houses. Free entry, but some parks require online registration. Season: April-October mainland; Madeira year-round.
f2f2d0b0b4371a778990fabcfddb68c6417515f8: **Rota Vicentina** (GR 11) — 450 km from Cabo de São Vicente to Santiago do Cacém, SW Alentejo

676b1c9768065bff4117d112a92d869bc7837cd3: Portugal has over 850 beaches and many piscinas naturais (natural pools in the rocks by the ocean). Top: Azenhas do Mar (Sintra), Cabo Mondego (Figueira), Nossa Senhora da Guia (Cascais), cave beaches Alentejo (Zambujeira). Blue flags 2025: over 370 beaches. Free municipal pools are available in most Junta. The warmest: Algarve (22-24°C summer), Madeira (21-23°C year-round). Colder: Atlantic north (17-20°C).

9fd093631793308f3624467ac6038b0e74064eb7: How to find an accountant (contabilista) in Portugal
22b07d6576921d5a5cfb3e746a68ce5422788454: For recibos verdes with turnover >€15,000/year (VAT payers) and all legal entities — a certified accountant (TOC / OCC) is mandatory by law. Cost: €50-150/month for self-employed, €200-600/month for small legal entities. Search: OCC.pt (directory), LinkedIn, Russian/English speakers in expat Facebook groups. What they should do: IRS, IVA, Segurança Social, SAF-T, annual declarations, contact with AT. It is recommended to change if there are more than 3 delays or lack of transparency.
264a4d95880183d25b89c36e0e86180a16ab8556: < €15,000 turnover/year — **not mandatory**, you can do it yourself through Portal das Finanças (simplified IRS)
2d9387a28b5057b48e2614cd76618c0a05773bd5: > €15,000 turnover — IVA is mandatory → **an accountant is legally required**
ba88301546f8ddb8a5a395e16de42503cd32b852: > €200,000 turnover — contabilidade organizada (organised accounting) → an accountant is always required
a4b223d61b21cb329381b73fa68676e83920dad2: With the IFICI regime — often hired even with low turnover for complex declarations
45c34cd2191cb8976a582900f4e0950a475a38a2: **All** Lda, SA, Unipessoal — **always** required to have a TOC (Técnico Oficial de Contas) or OCC member
8b54cd5372e640319b66f229144d29fdfb09a195: Montly: IES (Informação Empresarial Simplificada) — submitted annually
aff4e671e9a8c97002c9537a0a5ba91c20d918ae: SAF-T (Standard Audit File for Tax) — monthly to AT
79755e1b6e45cc640c7fbf6dd42e0ac7e7dd3ac6: Annual audit — for companies > €500k turnover / > 25 employees
184c24d22ac0b45972918f8fc4bdbc5cea7baa93: Employee (contract worker) — the employer pays taxes for you
941087b43c088d76abae236d24ca1885e86cece8: Property as an individual (rental < €7,000/year) — Anexo F in IRS
e78e368d95428cc00d467540d3e37f84fddf3a56: Freelancer < 15k — simplified regime through the Portal independently
df4f4469c71330e458f6e31c9fa250c9f971ba94: Investment income without active activity — Anexo G, E in IRS
7dfb68db99c06be65545028028aae4249ab1d796: Section «Encontrar Contabilista» — search by city, specialisation
7eb650e8b99ddebd8a761e0e6a30f2df1d97ab96: Check registration with everyone you interact with — **do not work with unregistered**
76f1326fe0e8ac3bce713824b88a769bfd231dc4: Plot twist: the government requires TOC to report all clients — if an accountant is without registration, they are breaking the law, and their signatures are not valid
38fcbe2b2ab05015395a22faa0b46f2bff31d30c: **Facebook groups**: "Portugal Americans", "Expats in Lisbon", "Русскоязычные в Португалии" — ask for recommendations
0f9a83ce6a0ecb6ae675b2678d527f7c4800ed2f: **LinkedIn**: search "TOC English-speaking Portugal" — there is a sub-category for expat-friendly
2a8731b62d0f81801dbdcec82b115cfc7937f09a: **Reddit**: r/Portugal, r/portugalexpats — thread with recommendations is updated regularly
6e37ac4cb6bac73b8d6d1a7f963ecbf38752245a: **Coworking spaces**: often have partner accountants with discounts for members (Avila Spaces, Cowork Central)
18599d19c3ba1a23e3a5f41f1c7e6a33ba0c95fa: **For IFICI / NHR**: large firms know the ins and outs — PwC, KPMG, Deloitte (expensive: €1,500-5,000/year)
4f6eadb15ff2d298cc0d49fac93629eba30e9bff: **Digital-first**: JustAccounts, Cegoc, Gurunima — online accounting with English support, €50-150/month
9cdef91d84a20349867b4846e7fcebd49e9f13f4: **Local**: there are options in every city — ask at your residential complex
d504e5125b3069ba486777cbdfc1eb497fe46b9a: 🎯 **Simple recibos verdes (< €15k turnover)**: not mandatory, but for assistance with IRS — €100-200 one-off
f36ea4b0d4fdc6a146c5f52c7b6b0c94fbd1ea2a: 🎯 **Recibos verdes with IVA (€15-75k)**: €50-100/month, includes monthly IVA + IRS year-end
5dc9adaa9a3cbd0f45fe187bef8aad2cbd2bb099: 💳 **Payment**: usually monthly direct debit or transfer, some — quarterly

3c3458e475a728806d14bba6824660b3e8dcffcc: Closing recibos verdes (cessação de atividade): art. 33 CIVA — through Portal das Finanças free of charge within 15 days after the last issued receipt. Required: submit final VAT declaration + Modelo 3 IRS (if there is turnover in the year) + notify Segurança Social. Closing Unipessoal / Lda is more complex: dissolução + liquidação through Conservatória Comercial (€200-400) + final accounts. Process takes 6-12 months. If there are debts — bankruptcy (insolvência) through tribunal.

3d57d4d0d6bd54431fa3457d65a857f027eee081: Labour rights in Portugal — what workers need to know
99b59eeb7d496444571cef76d6b5af7eff2bd2c9: Work in Portugal is regulated by the Labour Code (Lei 7/2009). Key points: 40-hour week (max 48 with overtime), 22 working days of leave + 13 public holidays, maternity leave 120-180 days 90-100% paid, dismissal requires justification + compensation (cap 12-20 months' salary), minimum wage €920/month in 2026. Probation period: 90 days (general), 180 (responsible positions), 240 (executive). Unions are active: CGTP-IN and UGT. Work reports to ACT (labour conditions authority).
2311dfcdd7ccbc2f38a66fac546923fc46933d82: Standard contract, with no end date
4a9f38823d4d9ed1cd226df41f1b9863ce2f5d88: Dismissal by employer: requires justification (despedimento por justa causa, extinction, inadequação) + compensation
57bde36fd43d149ab41032039c566bb7bf697506: Employee dismissal: 30 days' notice (up to 2 years in the company) or 60 days (more)
d7772a6dd5042b6f6d4196c60409d5f446dff871: Only for objective reasons (conjuncture, temporary project)
53d62e3077864879e3eece5b08ae6e788d1c779a: Dismissal without cause is only possible at the end of the term + compensation of 18-24 days' salary per year
7532527de5df1fcbe5efd46a44f476fe2898183b: Fixed-term without a set date (until the completion of the project / replacement of someone on maternity leave). Max 6 years. Compensation is the same as termo certo.

5f1d394104ab9c6bdb642ee9d5f888f7a72ba784: Self-employed-like. Pay your own taxes through Anexo B. NO employment rights (leave, sick pay, decree). For protection — the contract must reflect the actual independent status; otherwise, 'falso recibo verde', punishable by a fine of up to €6,000.
da7212a2f0b30ea1f8f53fe724d7100faaa4ac13: **Subsídio de férias** (holiday allowance): 1 month's salary in June
e68f5a4753918d873b33c56ff29b3f64cd869daa: **Subsídio de Natal** (13th month): 1 month's salary in December
3fbbb893663b10bbf45de0876402c60d6c495c7d: **Subsídio de alimentação** (meal voucher): not mandatory, but common. €6-9/working day. Up to €6/day is exempt from IRS
f3c0aa276d4ada0416ef5a88044a3da2b4bd117a: **Overtime (horas extras)**: +25% for the first hour, +37.5% afterwards
357d79ac3e28d4b9ceeca83c7ca3bc0b6f6fa46a: **Work on weekends/holidays**: +100% extra pay OR compensatory day off
65bab5817f5ce3a0a1a4a69a3d0eacd0b424e918: **Deductions**: IRS (progressive scale 11.97-48%), SS 11%. Gross → net difference ~22-35% depending on salary
9eb58e59eb50f51f56d011355ab3fcbbd051d8ec: **Pay slip (recibo de vencimento)**: must be issued monthly, detailing all deductions
4e50f4b32b6d34d546ac626532ec8b1f9671abfc: **Standard**: 40 hours per week, 8 hours per day
f0b5bb5711e7ab2fb16a076db725119b12a17944: Lunch break: 1-2 hours, minimum 1 hour if the day is >6 hours
ac143bb65ea43f53213c85dd4076a9183ca71ccc: Weekly day off: 1 mandatory + 1 additional (usually Saturday + Sunday)
7b488df404434b20631bf97310b2560d04943a5c: **Remote work (teletrabalho)**: regulated by Lei 83/2021 — written contract, payment for electricity/communication by employer, right to disconnection

4a86d5ca1028dcfaedad63792f55fcc429dd472e: If dismissed: compensation for unused days
ea6e23b9c8531632fe1d8c118a2ce9e26fea8da6: The first 3 days — unpaid (under general rule)
1b2fc828da0b64d5a9a7a8d05786d3ec654ed6a9: From the 4th day — Subsídio de doença from Segurança Social, 55-75% of average salary
b49eb7086f745fe0cb098475a609ca28a9231dd5: In case of long-term illness (>365 days) — 70%

a772540bb19766feb59a24649dc3a409e414c0b2: **180 days**: **90%** (from 2023, Lei 13/2023 — if both parents take ≥ 30 days)
ea9cb0f357ae7fa3561231313e354fc1c42ba27f: Pregnancy: 30+ days mandatory leave before childbirth
f357195fbdf0da2027e87178a97308dd1e3feac5: Feeding: 2 × 30 minutes of paid breaks per day until one year
372c967ff7cc0d9e627e96bc600331d8312ea21f: Dismissal for just cause (due to employee fault)
01c3878eef3db27391ef436082967c7ecd2dcec6: The employer must prove serious misconduct: absenteeism, theft, serious conflict, systematic breach of duties. Written notice + right to defence (direito de audiência prévia). If the court finds it unlawful — compensation + reinstatement.
605954772c1e174e1b9efbef8dde4c3321d4fa7a: Notice period of 30-75 days (depends on length of service)
40bf62ae9418c8671a3f1f559480e095a82b2d21: Compensation: **14 days' salary for each year of service** (cap of 12 × monthly salary or minimum of 3 × monthly salaries)
0b99c0da733aa574cdbe87fceb408d2a80f7a5cc: Unemployment benefit: after dismissal for non-fault reason
051d1168357fce0b79de58afa5fa7c103b752602: If an employee is absent for more than 10 working days without valid reason — the employer may consider it as auto-dismissal. This removes the right to compensation + unemployment benefit.

b25f8876748abded49e9426e0712ec371a2d02b8: For disputes over unjust dismissal, unpaid wages

b67a08d2c092808bfa566c65a0cf6bf276931288: Portugal is one of the most LGBTQ-friendly countries in Europe. Same-sex marriage since 2010 (Lei 9/2010), child adoption since 2016, gender change in documents without medical procedures since 2018 (Lei 38/2018), ban on gay conversion therapy since 2024. Asylum for persecuted LGBT refugees. Pride Lisboa and Porto from June to September. Discrimination based on orientation/identity is a criminal offence (up to 5 years imprisonment).
c20b7eefcf9c0abf9d406ed92c047b9355210b21: ✅ **Civil partnership** (união de facto) for same-sex couples since 2001
1b4d275b22a69f1cf746b9becb67d92310ea6b24: ✅ Full equality in property rights, inheritance, alimony, divorce
762707130f4c07a623413b4bdf02eb2a6a666762: ✅ Foreign same-sex marriages — recognised in PT
0e45a89ca99a9495a4e1dec790f82de190245af4: ✅ Same-sex spouse via reagrupamento familiar — same rights as heterosexual
f9b09eeb08d104200fe6ee5c9d25bc2558cf16b6: ✅ **Reproductive treatment**: lesbian couples since 2016, single women since 2017

51b6ee8e55621a2906cf48d4aad7e8fe5ddf54b3: Support for victims of domestic violence
7504893fdee8dc33a0aebfb1f97967e530d29b50: SOS Line: **800 202 148** (free, 24/7, anonymously, multilingual). APAV (Associação Portuguesa de Apoio à Vítima): 116 006. The law Lei 112/2009 recognises domestic violence as a crime — charges can be made even without the victim's consent (procedimento pública). You have the right to: emergency shelter (casa-abrigo, free), legal assistance, medical examination, protective measures (medidas de coação) against the perpetrator, immigration status if your residence permit depended on your partner. Service in 4+ languages.
cc9b6f1118a8e491623e7888a106cf9bac0b1455: ☎️ **112** — if there is an immediate threat. The police will come and take the perpetrator (medida de coação)
0d05776b98d62ba26cb98f460583ed4e3987f5f7: ☎️ **800 202 148** — National Social Emergency Line (free, 24/7, anonymously, EN/RU/UK sometimes)

5dabd6bf2a0e0dcb01918c0428bab20b34525a4c: 🏛️ **Procedimento pública**: police / prosecution can initiate a case **without the victim's consent**, if they learn about the violence. This means — even if you later 'withdraw' your statement, the case continues.
aa6e871c8cf08cb18812c85907c01490089bc261: 🛡️ **Restraining measures**: proximity restriction, removal from shared accommodation, prohibition of weapon possession, electronic bracelet monitoring
526503b85e7d546564765eaa3ba061a3be9cae36: 🏠 **Shelter**: secret-address shelter, free of charge, up to 1 year. Includes food, lawyer, psychologist. Transport provided by APAV / Cruz Vermelha

d86bebd2c6dccf9514366e6c89e31b0a0001824a: Livro de Reclamações — how to complain properly
254c5c647bb7021946d567539dd64e0eb8364cce: Livro de Reclamações (Complaints Book) — every establishment in PT is required to have it and provide it upon request. The complaint is automatically sent to the regulator (ANAC, ASAE, ERSE, BdP, ANACOM by sector) — this is a powerful tool, unlike Google reviews. Electronic version: livroreclamacoes.pt — submit online, without visit. Response is mandatory within **15 days**. Establishment fines: up to €25,000 for legal entities. Very effective — many malpractices are corrected after a complaint.
bfff9fa39c32a95fb2ca34c88c70ecf433f2bff0: According to **DL 156/2005** — every business providing services to consumers is required to have a Livro de Reclamações (a physical book with standard forms or an electronic version) and to provide it upon the customer's first request.
61f3aaa673164c278313abf5ebddfd3c899bdc81: 📚 **Physical Livro**: paper forms in triplicate — the original remains in the book, 1 copy for you, 1 copy sent to the regulator.
ff4bec644307f5531f88d26ebf4329508ac719f5: 💻 **Electronic Livro**: available at livroreclamacoes.pt — online submission after a visit.
688b40962b6728facc9399e381e42edba510d6d4: 🚫 **Refusal to provide** — this is a violation, fines of €250-3,750 (individuals), €5,000-25,000 (companies).
074e2cf6d4706174be406cf283b922f7d802db97: ⏰ **Response is mandatory within 15 working days** or the business will be fined.
e29c22cc2c12de336bf776e3f9d233c92517659c: 🏢 **Who is required**: restaurants, hotels, banks, insurance companies, telecoms, gyms, medical centres, supermarkets, airlines, taxis — everything except for a few exceptions (government entities have a different system).
6ebc349718b1412f88138388a7817d8cade58d06: 🍽️ **Restaurant**: spoiled food, refusal to accept Multibanco / valid means, additional 'service' fees not per contract.
13c64bfb54442d949bc9ada1c93b25d166155e03: 🏨 **Hotel**: not the room that was booked, broken amenities, false advertising.
e51b01760580e10b9267fef9cb8660949597ce74: 🏦 **Bank**: misleading presentation of financial products, hidden fees, refusal of service.
9158f50da0cf8b326492a923de1c4fa9fda97ce2: 📱 **Telecoms (NOS, MEO, Vodafone)**: incorrect bills, non-functioning internet, refusal to recreate a sub-account.
23036bb146800d1f963c3f8d71e2de70e12b6eb8: 🛡️ **Insurance**: refusal to pay a claim, slow processing, misleading terms.
a41a7161adb5aa654fa45641f2a70e5c851cae4e: 🚗 **Car rentals**: hidden charges, damage claims after vehicle return.
44fbc6b3a28155f738043f4cf37fce530a9a460c: 🛒 **Stores**: refusal to accept returns within 14 days (online), false advertising, defective products.
45f2730d74b3bc70d9595f5ac2295e3a2788bda6: ✈️ **Airlines**: cancellation, delay, baggage issues — separate EU 261/2004 process also applies
8f68adcfa537c803d8702342c1d6b5fe68513b1f: ⚠️ **For public services**: Livro is not working; use Provedor de Justiça (ombudsman)
7b6d3e392e99ac7212477801a5173d5b5da6a276: Request from the manager: «Can you bring me the Livro de Reclamações?»
78aa00b19f2350f53dc4b6b84d0b520ab92caf70: Fill it out on the spot: your name, NIF, contact, description of the situation, date, time
e77f6c7fcaaf7319f6bc69460031d945068228eb: **Be specific**: what exactly happened, what you want (refund, apology, fix)

c823c0400e6ceed38c00e0ca59afae7b0db5f193: Two paths: **Divórcio por mútuo consentimento** (mutual consent) — at the Conservatória, 1 day, €280, no lawyer needed if there are no conflicts. **Divórcio sem consentimento** (through court) — 3-12 months, lawyer required, ~€1,500-€5,000. The mandatory separation period has been abolished since 2008 (Lei 61/2008). For foreigners — the law of the country of marriage applies; PT court follows PT rules if both are residents. Alimony + division of property + child custody — main points of contention. Online divorce platform since 2022.

068d603c4a7dc3823b6e43ad91c8525f76314464: Portugal — top 3 EU countries by solar potential. UPAC (Unidade de Produção para Autoconsumo) systems up to 6.9 kW — no licence (registration with DGEG is free). Turnkey cost: €4,000-€12,000 for a typical flat/house (4-8 panels + inverter + battery optional). Payback period 5-9 years. Surplus supplied to the grid (compensation from the provider €0.04-€0.10/kWh). The Fundo Ambiental programme subsidises up to 70% (€2,500 max).

ee75c32146084e59a66755b06756cd4f5a449e9c: Civil marriage in Portugal — how to register a marriage
550c5ef9e9a01060b235bd90ce70f5c147a79fa5: Civil marriage at the Conservatória do Registo Civil. Available to residents and non-residents. Duration: 30+ days process (from request to ceremony); urgency for tourists 5-10 days. Requirements: passport, birth certificate (with apostille + translation for foreigners), certificado de capacidade matrimonial from your country (if non-PT), divorce certificate (if previously married). Cost: €120 standard, €200 urgent. Same-sex marriage — same procedure since 2010. Religious marriage (Catholic/Evangelical) — also has civil effect.

dc050de3630b620849a07d2b4c65c568e004130f: Estágios (internships) — a common starting point for a career in PT. Types: **Estágios profissionais** (government, IEFP, ~€750/month for 9 months) for unemployed / graduates; **Estágios curriculares** (university partner, no mandatory payment); **Estágios de inserção** (for long-term unemployed, up to 2 years, 80% IAS); **Private internships** (from companies, €600-€1,500/month). After the internship — 70% receive a full-time offer. For foreigners, a valid work visa D / EU passport / TPD is required.

e9b29610c548b1eaa25479ceb20d096542846e69: Pension system — Segurança Social, PPR, for retirees
74196fdcc505b2a265b512652c09c5e754d7e4a1: State pension: age 66 years 7 months (2026), full at 40+ years of service. Amount: average ~€530/month (2026), max ~€3,800. **PPR** (Plano Poupança-Reforma) — private pension with tax incentives (deduction up to 20% of contributions in IRS, 0% tax upon retirement). Foreign pensions (RU, US, UK) under IRS — taxed at progressive scale (10% NHR cap for those who had old NHR). For foreigners: international social security agreements (RU, BR, US, UK) count periods of service.
